name: Kettlebell Figure 8 Tactical Clean Complex
description: A single kettlebell complex combining a figure 8 pass with a tactical clean, performed for time with progressively heavier kettlebell weights.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Figure 8 Tactical Clean
exercises.description: Start in a hike position with the kettlebell. Pass the kettlebell between the legs, around the outside of one leg, then clean it to the rack position on the opposite side. Return to the hike position and repeat on the other side.
exercises.notes: Maintain a strong core and hinge at the hips. Keep the kettlebell close to the body during the clean. Focus on smooth transitions and control throughout the figure 8 movement.

sequence.flow: Perform the Kettlebell Figure 8 Tactical Clean for the specified duration in each round, progressively increasing the kettlebell weight.
sequence.notes: Round 1: 2:00 with a medium kettlebell. Round 2: 1:30 with a medium-heavy kettlebell. Round 3: 1:00 with a heavy kettlebell. Round 4: 0:30 with the heaviest kettlebell. Rest 1:00 between each round.

name: Seated Mini-Band Shoulder + Upper-Back Flow
description: 53-second seated flow with one mini-band (looped, light-medium tension). Heels-tucked seiza/half-lotus base. Continuous flow through ~7 banded shoulder positions — overhead pull-aparts, behind-neck pulldowns, lateral T-reaches, chest pull-aparts, diagonal reaches. No reps counted in the video — appears to be ~5-8s sustained tension at each position. Excellent banded scap/upper-back mobility primer; very 'old-school yoga teacher with a band' energy. Could slot into the W11+ morning flow rotation, especially before pull or push days.
extractionNotes: Video is silent flow (no spoken cues, no rep counts) — exercise breakdown is reconstructed from frames at 1fps over 53s.
Mini-band (loop band) used throughout, never swapped. Looks like medium-resistance.
Athlete is in heels-tucked kneeling/seiza posture the entire video — no standing.
Each position held ~5-8s with sustained tension. No counted reps.
Exercises 4-5 are the most useful 'stretches' the user flagged — the lateral T-reach and chest-level pull-apart open up a lot of upper back.
All exercises here are CANDIDATES — none yet exist in exercises/. Would need new exercise files before this workout can be built into the catalogue.

sets.name: Continuous Flow (~5-8s per position, no rest)

exercises.name: Seated banded overhead pull-apart
exercises.notes: Sit heels-tucked. Band looped between hands, arms straight overhead, hands wider than shoulders. Pull band apart to create tension, ribs down, shoulders away from ears. Sustained tension, slow micro-pulses.

exercises.name: Seated banded behind-neck pulldown
exercises.notes: From overhead position, pull the band down behind the head/neck — elbows drive down toward the ribs, shoulder blades retract. Like a band lat-pulldown without the cable.

exercises.name: Seated banded T-raise (lateral reach)
exercises.notes: Band wraps around the upper back/shoulder blades, hands hold the band ends in front. Press arms straight out to the sides into a T — band creates resistance against the rear delts and mid-traps. Palms can rotate forward.

exercises.name: Seated banded chest pull-apart
exercises.notes: Band held in front at sternum height, arms straight. Pull band apart, scaps retract, chest opens. Slower than the overhead version — feels like the 'core' opener of the sequence.

exercises.name: Seated banded reverse fly
exercises.notes: From the chest pull-apart position, sweep arms back further behind the body line. Rear delts and rhomboids drive it. Subtle — easy to overrotate the lower back here.

exercises.name: Seated banded diagonal reach (high-low triangle)
exercises.notes: One arm reaches high overhead, other reaches diagonal-out — band makes a slanted line/triangle. Like a half-windmill with band. Switches sides in the video. Lat + side-body opener.

exercises.name: Seated banded overhead V-hold
exercises.notes: Closer to a Y-hold — both arms reach high and wide, band stretched between hands in a V/diamond. Hold the tension at end-range, breathe. Closes the flow.

tips: All seven moves use one mini-band, looped — no equipment swap.
Seated heels-tucked posture forces upper-back work without lower-body cheat.
Sustained tension, NOT reps. Hold each position ~5-8s with steady pull, breathe.
Best slot: morning flow primer before push or pull day. Hits the same scap-prep work as wall slides + face pulls but with less setup.
If shoulder/neck tight, drop band tension before reducing range of motion.

name: Kneeling Dumbbell Shoulders & Biceps Circuit
description: A dumbbell circuit targeting shoulders and biceps, performed kneeling to engage core stability. Includes curl and press variations for upper body strength.

activities.notes: Nice and low down, a lot of tension. Brace the rib cage.
Drive the knees, think of the grip of the elbow.
Palms towards up, full external rotation, proud chest. Each side.
Pressing away, back and return. Focus on top ends of front delts.

tips: All exercises performed kneeling on a mat
Brace core and rib cage throughout
3 sets of 10 reps per exercise

name: Banded Shoulder Flow (Seated, KB-Day Variant)
description: 12 min. Reusable Tue/Thu morning flow variant — built around a 7-position seated mini-band sequence (@sculptherbody). Sustained tension, NOT reps. Pairs well with KB days because it primes scaps, rear delts, and lats for overhead/rack work without burning the legs. Bookended with the standard thoracic + hip openers from the W7-W8 KB flow.

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Deep thoracic rotation — primes the spine for the band work.
Full spinal wave, slow.

sets.name: Seated Banded Shoulder Flow

exercises.notes: Heels tucked. Arms straight overhead, hands wider than shoulders. **Sustained tension**, ribs down — not snappy reps.
Pull band down behind the head — **elbows drive to ribs**, scaps depress. Don't crank the neck forward.
Band wraps the upper back, hands hold the ends in front. **Press straight out to T** — feel the rear delts and mid-traps.
Band at sternum height, **pull apart and squeeze scaps**. Slower than overhead version — chest opens, ribs stay down.
Sweep arms further back behind the body line. Subtle — **don't overarch the lower back** to chase range.
30s = 15s each side. One arm overhead, other diagonal-out. **Lat + side-body stretch** with band tension. Switch sides.
Both arms in overhead V/Y. **Hold and breathe** — lower traps fire here. Closes the flow.

sets.notes: 2 rounds, ~3 min each. Sustained tension throughout, no rest between positions. Mini-band stays in your hands the whole time.

exercises.notes: 30s each side.
20s each side. Press into the stretch.

tips: **KB-day variant** — designed to pair with Tue/Thu KB sessions where upper-body skill work dominates.
**Sustained tension, not reps.** Each banded position = ~20s steady pull, breathe through it.
Mini-band only — no equipment swaps. Keeps the flow continuous.
If the seated heels-tucked posture is uncomfortable, sit cross-legged or on a yoga block.
Excellent prep for bent press, windmill, TGU — all the overhead-load KB skills.

name: Pull Day Morning Flow
description: 12 min upper back and lat primer + hip openers before pull day. Dead hangs, scap work, thoracic mobility, plus hip stretches for desk-bound humans.

exercises.notes: Slow. Focus on thoracic extension on the cow.
Decompress spine, stretch lats. Relax into it.
From the hang — pull shoulder blades down and together without bending arms. Activates lats.
Squeeze rear delts and rhomboids. Warms up the pulling muscles.
Big circles. Loosen shoulder capsule.
Press chest toward floor. Stretches lats and shoulders for overhead pulling.
30s each side. Sit tall, rotate between internal and external hip rotation.
30s each side. Wall or couch. Opens hip flexors — the muscles that get tightest from sitting.
Deep squat hold. Elbows press knees out, chest tall. Hang out and breathe.

tips: 12 min — upper back primer + hip openers
If no pull-up bar at home, skip dead hang and scap pulls — do them at the gym before your first set
The dead hang alone makes a huge difference for pull-up performance

name: Push Day Morning Flow
description: 10 min shoulder/chest opener before push day. Loosen thoracic spine, warm up rotator cuffs, get blood into chest and shoulders. Mat or floor only.

exercises.notes: Slow. Wake up the thoracic spine.
Small to big. Forward then backward.
Light band or no band — just squeeze shoulder blades. Opens up the chest.
Push hips high, press chest toward floor. Stretches shoulders and lats.
Easy reps. Slow descent, feel the stretch at the bottom. Blood flow, not fatigue.
Hang from bar or doorframe. Depress and retract scapulae. Activates the stabilizers.

tips: 10 min max — this is a primer, not a workout
If no band available, just do the pull-apart motion with arms extended
Focus on opening the chest and warming rotator cuffs

name: Sunday Long Flow — Yin/Restorative (25-30 min)
description: **Permanent addition to the functional-bulk weekly schedule (W12D1 discussion outcome).** Long-hold yin/restorative session for the connective tissue. Morning flows are activation-grade (30-60s holds). This is *release-grade* — 90s to 2min holds, where fascia and joint capsule actually start to lengthen. Goes on the Sunday rest day. Three blocks: opening / hips / spine + shoulders / closing. Goal is parasympathetic shift + connective tissue catch-up after a heavy gym week. No equipment, floor only, can do at home.

dayOverview.theme: Yin-style long-hold flow for connective-tissue recovery. Morning flows wake the body; this lengthens it. 90s-2min holds are what fascia responds to — anything shorter is mobility prep, not release.
watchpoints: **Long holds, not stretches.** Don't push to end-range and bounce. Find ~70% of available range and STAY. The release happens in time, not in depth.
**Breathe long and slow.** 4-6 breaths per minute. The parasympathetic shift IS the intervention — recovery happens in parasympathetic state, not from the stretch itself.
**Skip anything that lights up.** If pigeon or 90/90 triggers the right-shoulder grip pattern or QL spasm, bail and switch to a milder shape.
**Pair with the post-gym cool-down**, don't substitute. End-of-session cool-down is activation-release after load; Sunday flow is dedicated recovery on a non-training day. Different jobs.

sets.name: Opening — Wake the System (~5 min)

exercises.notes: Slow, full ROM. Wake the spine segment-by-segment. Match the wave to slow breathing — inhale on cow, exhale on cat.
Knees wide, big toes touching. Arms forward, forehead on the floor. Breathe into the back ribs — that's where parasympathetic shift starts.

Knees under hips, chest sinks toward the floor with arms extended forward. Long hold of the lat + shoulder opener. **Especially relevant** for the W12 shoulder pattern — opens the pec minor/lat fascial line.

sets.notes: Get the system parasympathetic before going into the long holds. Don't rush.

sets.name: Hips — Long Holds for the QL/Glute/Hip-Rotator Pattern (~10 min)

exercises.notes: Soles together, knees out. Hinge gently from the hips — don't round the spine. Inner thigh + groin lengthen.
**90s each side.** Front leg + back leg at 90°. Sink hips toward the floor. Long hold targets the hip-rotator capsule — what gets gripped during squats and KB work. Switch sides at 90s.

**120s each side** (vs. morning flow's 30-60s). The long hold is what releases the glute + QL chain. **W12 QL watchpoint:** if the QL spasm pattern fires, drop to figure-4 (supine, ankle on knee) instead. Switch sides at 120s.
On the back, knees toward armpits, hands grip outside of feet (or shins). Gentle rock side to side or stay still. Releases sacrum + lower back.

sets.notes: Hip block is the longest because the lower body takes the heaviest loaded work. QL, glutes, hip flexors all get pinned in flexion patterns during squats and deadlifts.

sets.name: Spine + Shoulders — Release the Upper-Trap/Pec/Neck Cluster (~7 min)

exercises.notes: **90s each side.** Quadruped, thread one arm under the other, rest the shoulder on the floor. Thoracic rotation + posterior shoulder lengthen. Directly addresses the W12 neck-shoulder-pec cluster pattern.
**90s each side.** Lie back, draw one knee across the body, opposite arm extended. Long hold lets the spinal erectors and obliques actually lengthen. Breathe into the rib cage on the rotated side.
Legs extended forward, hinge from the hips. Don't pull to depth — find a 70% range and stay. Posterior chain + low back decompression.

**60s each at LOW / MID / HIGH elbow positions.** All three positions to hit different fibers of pec minor + major. This is the direct intervention for the W12 right-shoulder pattern — pec minor lengthening decompresses the brachial plexus.

sets.notes: Upper body block targets exactly the cluster from W12D1: neck + shoulder + right pec as one unit. Long holds, three angles on the pec via doorframe.

sets.name: Closing — Parasympathetic Reset (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Final return to child's pose. Long slow breaths, forehead on the floor or a block.

sets.notes: **Optional 2-5 min savasana after** — lie flat on the back, palms up, eyes closed. Not 'rest,' deliberate stillness. This is when the parasympathetic shift finishes consolidating. Worth the time.

tips: **Long holds (90s-2min), not stretches.** 70% of available range, stay. Release happens in time.
**Breathe slow.** 4-6 breaths per minute. The parasympathetic shift IS the intervention.
**Hip block is the longest** — pigeon 2 min/side is non-negotiable for the QL/glute pattern.
**Pec doorframe at 3 positions** addresses the W12 right-shoulder cluster directly.
**Pair with weekday morning flows + post-gym cool-down**, don't substitute. Three layers of recovery, different jobs.
**Skip anything that lights up symptoms** — pigeon/QL or doorframe/shoulder. Switch to a milder shape rather than push.
**Optional savasana 2-5 min after** is when the parasympathetic shift consolidates. Worth the time.
formPhilosophy: Yin-style long-hold work for connective tissue recovery. Morning flows activate. Post-gym cool-down decompresses. Sunday long flow lengthens. Three layers, different timescales. Added W12 after the user surfaced the new-muscle + universal-tightness pattern + the right-shoulder cluster. The connective tissue lags muscle adaptation by 6-8 weeks; this is the input that lets it catch up.

name: Week 1 Day 2 Morning Flow
description: Morning flow after Push day. Core-heavy floor work, bodyweight legs, hip mobility, and light pushup variations for blood flow. No equipment needed — just a mat. Helps you recover faster while still getting work in.

exercises.notes: Slow — exhale on cat, inhale on cow. Wake up the spine.
30s each side. Push hips forward gently. Your hip flexors are tight from sitting.
30s each side. Rock gently into it. Opens up glutes and hips.
Touch toes, grab ankles, drop into deep squat, stand. Repeat.
Pedal out the feet, push hips high. Stretches hamstrings and shoulders.
Opposite arm/leg. Keep lower back glued to floor. Slow and controlled.
Slow — touch elbow to opposite knee, fully extend the other leg.
Extend opposite arm/leg, hold 2 seconds, squeeze glute. Core stability.
Squeeze everything — glutes, core, quads. Don't sag.
Full depth, arms in front. Slow on the way down — 2 second descent.
Deep lateral squat, heel stays planted. Opens up adductors and hips.
Squeeze hard at top, 2 second hold. Feel the glutes, not the lower back.
Slow walking or reverse lunges. Control the descent, drive through front heel.
Action Jacquelyn. Wide stance, toes out, drop into a plié and pulse. Inner thighs and quads.
Kneel upright, body straight, lean backwards as far as you can control. Quads fight the lean. Come back up. Go only as far as you can return from.
Easy reps — not to failure. Just getting blood into chest/tris/shoulders to help recovery.
Wider grip, feel the chest stretch at the bottom. Easy pace.
Slow and controlled — bring knee to chest, hold 1 second. Core + hip flexors.
30s each side. Deeper than the warmup — you're warm now.
Deep squat hold — just hang out. Scroll your phone. Opens everything up.
Slow, end on cow (extension). Finish with a good spine position.

tips: This is RECOVERY — nothing should feel hard. If it does, do fewer reps.
Core and legs are the focus since your upper body is wrecked from Push day
The pushup block is just for blood flow — do NOT go to failure
Cossack squats are gold for hip mobility AND leg strength
Asian squat hold at the end: just sit in it, let gravity do the work
Total: ~30 min on a mat. No equipment needed.

name: Week 1 Day 4 Morning Flow
description: Hip-heavy morning flow before KB Conditioning day. Built around the @biolifex 8-exercise mobility flow for hip opening and spinal mobility, plus light core and deep hip stretches from @_fitnesshub. Everything is sore — this is about moving through it.

exercises.notes: Squat to standing, head stays down, hands touching toes. Wake up the hips.
Keep hips low, alternate sides. Opens adductors and inner hips.
Easy twist from forward fold. Spine rotation + hamstring stretch.
Step from standing to pigeon, hold briefly. Deep hip opener.
Pulse hips forward and back. This is where the magic happens for sore hips.
Breathe with movement — inhale arch, exhale round. Spine + shoulders.
Figure-four position, lean forward gently. Gets deep into the glutes.
Smooth transitions, slide forward through the floor. Feels amazing on sore everything.
Opposite arm/leg. Keep lower back glued to floor.
Extend opposite arm/leg, hold 2 seconds. Core stability without loading the spine.
Squeeze everything. Don't sag.
Easy reps — blood flow for chest/tris recovery from push day.
Slow and controlled — also hits hip flexors which need the work today.
Each side — keep hips square, fold over front leg. Deep glute/hip stretch.
Each side — elbows down for deeper stretch. Hip flexors and groin.
Gently press knees down, hinge from hips. Inner thighs.
Keep shoulders down, controlled side-to-side. Spinal rotation + hip release.

tips: Block 1 is the whole @biolifex mobility flow — do it as one continuous sequence, no rest
Everything is sore and that's fine — move THROUGH it, not around it
Core is only 2 rounds today — just enough to wake up the stabilizers
Pushups are pure blood flow — do NOT push hard, your chest is still recovering
Block 4 deep stretches: just breathe into them and let gravity work
Total: ~30 min on a mat. You'll feel way better after this.

name: Week 1 Day 5 Morning Flow
description: Quick hip primer before Legs day. Just mobility and stretching — no core, no pushups, no leg work. Open up the hips, wake up the spine, get out the door.

exercises.notes: Slow — wake up the spine. Exhale cat, inhale cow.
Squat to standing, head stays down. Opens up the squat pattern.
30s each side. Push hips forward. Your hip flexors need this before squats.
Pulse hips forward and back. Opens inner hips and groin.
30s each side. Rock gently. Gets deep into glutes.
Pedal out the feet. Hamstrings and calves — both about to get worked.
Touch toes, drop into deep squat, stand. Rehearsing the squat pattern.

tips: This is a 15-minute hip primer — NOT a workout
No core, no pushups, no leg exercises. Save your legs for squats.
The goal is to open up your hips so your squat depth is better at the gym
Dynamic malasana and squat-to-stand are rehearsing the squat movement pattern
If you're short on time, just do cat-cow + hip flexor stretch + squat-to-stand

name: Week 1 KB Conditioning
description: Fast-paced kettlebell conditioning circuit — swings, cleans, snatches, and thrusters in a short, brutal format. Designed to pair with cardio equipment for a complete 30-min session. All fixed exercises.

exercises.notes: Loosen up fast
Easy swings to prime the hinge
Two-handed, explosive
Clean to rack, strict press, switch sides
Squat to press in one fluid motion
Full power, punch through at the top
Halo one direction, lunge, halo other direction, lunge other leg
Figure-8 pass then step back into lunge. Alternate legs.

tips: This is meant to be fast — minimal rest between exercises, 45s between rounds
Use a lighter bell than your strength days. This is conditioning, not max effort.
Pair with 15 min on assault treadmill, stairmaster, or rower for a complete session
The complex hits every movement pattern: hinge, push, squat, pull
If snatches aren't in your toolbox yet, sub high pulls or more clean-and-presses

name: Week 1 KB Full Body
description: Full-body kettlebell strength session covering all movement patterns — squat, hinge, push, pull, and core. Heavier on lower body with goblet squats, Bulgarian splits, and RDLs. Single bell, ~40 min. All fixed exercises.

exercises.notes: Light, loosen shoulders and thoracic spine
Easy reps, find your depth
Easy swings, groove the hinge
Full depth, chest up, elbows inside knees at the bottom
Hold KB goblet style at chest. Rear foot on bench/chair/couch. Control the descent.
Slow on the way down, squeeze glutes at the top. Feel the hamstrings.
Explosive hips. This is the power complement to the slow RDL.
Clean to rack, strict press overhead. Alternate arms each set or do all one side then switch.
Hinge position, pull elbow past ribs, squeeze at top
Slow and controlled. Eyes on the bell. This is core stability + hip mobility.
Last push — full power, empty the tank

tips: One kettlebell, ~40 minutes — covers every movement pattern
The Bulgarian splits and RDLs give this way more lower body work than typical KB circuits
Use a moderate bell you can press for 8+ reps — lower body exercises will feel easy but that's fine
For the Bulgarian split squat, any elevated surface works: bench, chair, couch, stairs
Progress by adding reps, then adding a round, then going heavier

name: Week 1 Legs (Quads/Hamstrings/Glutes/Calves)
description: Foundation leg day grouped by station — squat rack for squats and RDLs, leg press for quads and calves, leg curl machine, then dumbbells for lunges. Minimizes moving around a crowded gym. All fixed exercises.

exercises.notes: Full depth, arms in front for balance
Open up the hips
Bodyweight walking lunges, feel the stretch
Barbell back squat. Below parallel, chest up, drive through heels. Main lift — add weight when you hit 4×8.
Same bar, strip some weight. Slow descent, feel hamstrings stretch, squeeze glutes at top.
Feet shoulder-width, mid-platform. Full depth without butt lifting.
Same machine — feet low on platform, toes only. Full stretch at bottom, squeeze at top.
Full range of motion, squeeze at peak contraction, slow 2-second negative.
Walking or reverse. Dumbbells at sides. Control the descent, drive through front heel.

tips: Grouped by station — squat rack → leg press → leg curl → dumbbells
Squat rack: do squats AND RDLs here — same bar, just change the weight
Leg press: do calf raises here too — same machine, just move your feet down
Squat is your main lift — warm up with progressively heavier sets first
Rest 2 min between squat/RDL sets, 60s between everything else
Total volume: ~17 working sets for legs

name: Week 1 Pull (Back/Biceps/Rear Delts)
description: Foundation pull day grouped by station — pull-up bar, then barbell rows, then all cable work, then dumbbells to finish. Minimizes equipment switching. All fixed exercises.

exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades, warm up upper back
Loosen thoracic spine
Decompress spine, activate grip and lats
Full dead hang to chin over bar. Add weight when you hit 3×8. If you can't do 5, use a band or do negatives.
Hinge at hips, pull to lower chest, squeeze at top. Keep core tight, don't round the back.
Wide grip, lean back slightly, pull to upper chest. Control the eccentric.
Swap to close-grip handle. Chest up, pull to belly button, squeeze shoulder blades.
High cable, rope attachment. Pull to face, externally rotate at the end. Light weight.
Low cable, straight bar or EZ bar. Full extension, squeeze at top. Finish all cable work here.
Bent over on a bench or standing. Slight bend in elbows, squeeze rear delts.
Same dumbbells, neutral grip. Hits brachialis and forearms.

tips: Grouped by station — pull-up bar → barbell → cable machine → dumbbells
Cable station has 4 exercises — just swap attachments and weight, don't move
Pull-ups and rows are your main lifts — progressive overload these
Face pulls are non-negotiable — they keep your shoulders healthy for pressing days
Total volume: ~18 working sets for pull muscles

name: Week 1 Push (Chest/Shoulders/Triceps)
description: Foundation push day grouped by station — bench work first, then dumbbells, then cables. Minimizes equipment switching in a crowded gym. All fixed exercises for tracking progression.

exercises.notes: Forward then backward, loosen shoulders
Light band, squeeze shoulder blades
Easy reps, get blood flowing to chest and tris
Flat barbell bench. Plant feet, arch slightly, drive through chest. Add weight when you hit 4×8.
Adjust bench to 30-45°. Controlled descent, press up and slightly in. Grab DBs before you start.
Seated or standing. Full lockout overhead, controlled descent to ears.
Same dumbbells but lighter — or grab a second pair. Lead with elbows, slight lean forward.
High or mid-cable. Slight forward lean, big stretch, squeeze at center.
Drop the weight, single arm cross-body. Constant tension.
Swap to rope or V-bar. Elbows pinned, full extension, squeeze at bottom.

tips: Grouped by station — claim one spot, do all your work there, move on
Bench station is your main lift — focus on progressive overload here
Cable station: just swap the attachment between exercises, no need to move
Rest 2 min between bench sets, 60s between isolation sets
Total volume: ~16 working sets for push muscles

name: Week 10 KB Conditioning — Skill Drill + Mobility (Light, Form-First)
description: Thursday is the lightest day of the program — by design. Recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs. Drill Tuesday's cues at low load, refine what was clunky, groove what clicked. **Spotlight: bent press** (lateral hinge mechanics — the leverage cue from W7 takes weeks to make automatic). 20 min, zero leg loading, no swings, no C&P, no goblet squats. Heaviest bell touched: 25lb. Should feel effortless. Ends with a hip-and-adductor cooldown — hips get tight on KB days.

dayOverview.theme: Thursday is recovery + skill drilling, NOT a workout. Drill Tuesday's cues at low load. Should leave you fresher, not more cooked.

spotlights.why: Bent press is mechanically the most technique-dense KB lift — lateral hinge + overhead load + eye-track + leverage press. The W7 click ('groove the path') needs weeks of light reps to become automatic. Drill it here at 18-20lb where the brain can pay attention to the cues, not the load.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 bent press · W11 windmill mechanics · W12 TGU left-side checkpoints · W13 cycle back. Thursday is always lighter than Tuesday's spotlight.
watchpoints: **Zero leg loading.** No swings, no C&P, no goblet squats, no lunges. Legs day is tomorrow.
If anything feels heavy, the load is wrong. Pull weight or skip the block.
Boring is the goal. Don't innovate on light skill day — same blueprint as W7-W8 with cleaner cues.
5 hard days/week = injury. Thursday MUST stay light, even if you feel fresh.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Slow circles, both directions. Shoulder loosener — that's it.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (Scap Activation, ~3 min)

exercises.notes: Heels tucked, mini-band overhead, hands wider than shoulders. **Sustained tension** — primes scap upward rotation for bent press + TGU.
Pull band down behind head — **elbows drive to ribs**, scaps depress.
Band wraps the upper back. **Press out to T** — fires rear delts and mid-traps.
Band at sternum, **pull apart, scaps retract**, ribs down.
Sweep arms further back. **Don't overarch** the lower back.
15s each side. Half-windmill with band — **primes the bent press lateral hinge cue** (today's spotlight).
Both arms in V/Y overhead. **Hold and breathe** — lower traps fire here.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Scap + rear delt activation before the bent press spotlight + skill work. Sustained tension, no rest between positions.

sets.name: OTD Flow (Light Groove)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 20lb. Light and smooth.
- Bell starts on the floor between feet
- **Hips drive the lift**, not the arm
- Catch in rack with **knees slightly bent** (Tuesday's spotlight cue)
Goblet/rack position. **Sit tall**, full depth, no bouncing. Same depth as gym squats — find the floor of the squat.
Stride out wide, **sit into the working leg**, keep the other leg long. Working leg's hip in external rotation. Mobility, not strength.
**Tuesday's spotlight cue** — see Skill Drilling block below for the full pattern. Light groove here.
**Control on the way down** — bell to floor with the same cleanness as the way up. The tactical clean's whole point is the controlled descent.

sets.notes: 20lb, 2 reps each side x 2 rounds. Should feel effortless throughout.

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 18-20lb (light to keep the cues clean, NOT 25). 2 rounds (not 3 — keep it skill-day light).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Bell starts in rack, eye on bell throughout** — the head turn IS part of the lift
- **Lateral hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — push the hip out sideways, knee soft on bell side
- **Spine stays long** — this is a *lateral hinge with overhead load*, NOT a side bend. If you feel the lower back compress, you're side-bending.
- **Press up by moving body DOWN, not by pushing bell UP** — the press is mostly leverage. The bell barely moves; your body changes shape under it.
- Slow descent, controlled return to rack — no slam
- **Free hand:** can drift to inside of front foot for counterbalance, but the bell-side stays vertical

**Reflection:** does the lateral hinge feel like a hinge, or are you side-bending? Side-bending = lower back load. Hinging = hip + glute load.

**LOAD:** 18-20lb. Light. Same hinge family as bent press.
- Bell locked overhead, **eye tracks the bell**
- **Hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — same pattern as bent press
- Reach free hand toward inside of front foot
- **Long spine** — stretch should land in the **hip flexor of the back leg** and the hamstrings, NOT the lower back

**LOAD:** 25lb (max bell of the day). Lighter intent than Tuesday — drill the **left side** transitions (still the weak link).
- Pause at every checkpoint: lying → elbow → hand → hip bridge → leg sweep → lunge → stand
- Reverse with the same discipline — the descent is half the lift
- Left side first, get the freshest reps to the weak side
60s each side. Hip flexor release — KB flow + cossack + lateral lunge fries the hip flexors. This counters.
60s. Adductors get tight from B-stance, cossack squats, and lateral lunges. Sit, soles together, hinge forward, breathe.

tips: **THURSDAY = SKILL + MOBILITY, NOT WORKOUT.** ~23 min, light. Recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs.
**NEW W10:** Seated mini-band shoulder prep added after the halo warmup (@sculptherbody flow). 7 positions, ~3 min, sustained tension. Primes the bent press lateral hinge spotlight.
**Zero leg loading.** No swings, no C&P, no goblet squats, no lunges.
Heaviest bell: 25lb (TGU only). Most of the session at 18-20lb.
Spotlight: bent press lateral hinge. Drill the leverage cue at light load — automaticity is the goal.
Skill maintenance + Tuesday's cue refinement. If a cue from Tuesday felt off, this is when you fix it (low-stakes reps).
Cooldown is mandatory — pigeon + butterfly. Hips and adductors get tight on KB days.
Should feel effortless. If anything feels like work, you've gone too hard. Pull back.
formPhilosophy: Light grooves are how cues become automatic. Bent press leverage takes weeks of low-load reps before the body 'gets it.' Same with TGU left-side stability. This is the day where complex skill becomes default movement — without ego, without load, without rush.

name: Week 10 KB Full Body — Skill + Mobility (Form-First, Light)
description: First KB session back after W9 sick-week rest — and structurally lighter than past Tuesdays. **Five hard days a week is the road back to injury.** This week, KB Tuesday is reframed: skill + mobility + light grooving, NOT a fourth strength day. The heavy strength block from W8 is gone. C&P, rows, and goblet squats stay light (35lb max). Swings cut to half-volume. Spotlights: C&P (technique drilling) and TGU (left side weak link). Modern KB cues throughout. If anything feels heavy, you've gone too far — pull weight, slow down.

dayOverview.theme: Tuesday is now a skill + mobility day, not a strength day. Mon/Wed/Fri are the strength days; Tue/Thu are the recovery/skill flank. Form is the only progression on KB days.

spotlights.why: C&P at 50lb was a W7-W8 breakthrough lift, but it's also a complex technique. W10 drops to 35lb and drills the technique — knee-bent clean catch, hip drive, ribs-down lockout. Build the pattern, not the load.
Left side has been the weak link for weeks. W10 spotlights TGU checkpoints — every position gets a pause, left side gets the freshest reps. Stay at 25lb, no chase.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 C&P + TGU · W11 swing mechanics + bent press leverage · W12 windmill + cossack mobility · W13 cycle back to C&P + TGU. ~3 weeks per cycle.
watchpoints: **Re-acquaintance week — back to home base, not chasing PRs.** Bent press / cossack / windmill at 25 (W7 proven sweet spot). C&P starts at 35, caps at 40 today. **No 50 C&P** — going there cold after 3 weeks off is the same trap that triggered the W8 QL spasm. Climb the ladder 35→40→45→50 over the next 3-4 KB sessions.
QL/lower back on swings — even at 25lb, the hip-hinge-under-load pattern is the W8/W9 trigger. Cut the set if it talks.
5 hard days/week = injury territory. Tue/Thu should leave you fresher, not more cooked. The pushing days are Mon/Wed/Fri.

exercises.notes: Light bell (15-20lb), big slow circles. Loosen the shoulders before any overhead work.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Find your depth — same depth focus as gym squats. Sit in the bottom for a beat. NOT a working set, just mobility.

**LOAD:** 25lb (light, just for the hinge groove).
- **Hike pass** between the legs, NOT a squat-and-lift
- Lats packed down, bell stays close to the body
- Hips snap forward — bell floats to chest height on momentum
- Watch the QL even at this weight

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (Scap Activation, ~3 min)

exercises.notes: Heels tucked, mini-band overhead, hands wider than shoulders. **Sustained tension** — primes scap upward rotation for bent press + windmill.
Pull band down behind head — **elbows drive to ribs**, scaps depress. Lat-pulldown pattern.
Band wraps the upper back. **Press out to T** — fires rear delts and mid-traps before C&P.
Band at sternum, **pull apart, scaps retract**, ribs down.
Sweep arms further back. **Don't overarch** the lower back to chase range.
15s each side. Half-windmill with band — **lat + side-body opener**, primes the bent press lateral hinge.
Both arms in V/Y overhead. **Hold and breathe** — lower traps fire here. Closes the prep.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Scap + rear delt activation before the heavier KB skill work. Sustained tension throughout, no rest between positions.

sets.name: Block 1: B-Stance Flow

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb (W7-W8 sweet spot, hold).
- B-stance: one foot back, **heel up** on the back foot — most weight on the front leg
- Hike pass between legs, lats engaged
- Hips snap forward, NOT lifted by the arms
**Catch into rack on the swing.** Soft elbow into the body — no slam. Flow should feel smooth, not violent.
Stay in B-stance, squat from rack. Front-loaded squat — chest tall, back foot heel up.
Punch overhead from rack. Lock out hard at the top — bicep by the ear, ribs down (no rib flare).
Bell locked overhead, step into lunge. **Bicep stays glued to the ear** — if it drifts forward, the lockout is fake. Long stride, vertical front shin.

sets.notes: 25lb. 3 reps each side x 3 rounds. Same volume as W7-W8 — proven. Don't add load.

sets.name: Block 2: Skill Work (Bent Press / Cossack / Windmill)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb (back to home base — W3/W7 proven sweet spot). Drop to 20 if anything feels off after 3 weeks away.
- **Bell starts in rack, eye on bell throughout** — head turn is part of the lift
- **Lateral hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — knee soft on bell side
- **Spine stays long** — lateral hinge with overhead load, NOT a side bend
- Press up by *moving body down*, not by pushing bell up — the press is leverage
- Slow descent, controlled return to rack

**LOAD:** 25lb goblet (W7 sweet spot — best depth at this weight, don't chase heavier).
- Sit DEEP, heels stay down
- Working leg in full external rotation
- **Mobility, not strength** — heel popping up = drop to 20 or shorten stance

**LOAD:** 25lb (W7 home base). Drop to 20 if the hinge gets sketchy — long spine wins over load.
- Bell locked overhead, eye tracks bell
- **Hip hinge AWAY from bell side** (same as bent press)
- Reach free hand toward inside of front foot
- Long spine — stretch should be in the **hip flexor of the back leg**, NOT lower back

**LOAD:** 25lb (hold). Reps trimmed (2 not 3) — quality over quantity.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — every checkpoint gets a deliberate pause:**
- **Lying:** bell punched up, opposite leg straight, opposite arm 45° to body
- **Roll to elbow:** drive through opposite heel, eyes ON the bell
- **Roll to hand:** straight base arm, chest up
- **Hip bridge:** glute squeeze, ribs down
- **Sweep leg through to lunge:** plant the knee under the hip
- **Stand up:** drive through the front heel, bell stays vertical
- **Reverse:** same checkpoints in reverse, no rushing

**Left side first** — give it the freshest reps. The weak side gets the priority, not the leftovers.

**Reflection:** which checkpoint felt off on the left side? That's next week's drill.

**LOAD:** 35lb start, 40lb cap today. **No 50 today — that's the QL-spasm-territory weight after 3 weeks off, asking for the same injury back.** If 35×5 is invisible in round 1, jump to 40 for round 2 and stop there. The ramp 35 → 40 → 45 → 50 plays out over the next 3-4 KB sessions, not today.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Catch the clean with knees slightly bent** — absorbs impact, makes press seamless (W3 discovery, the cue that made 50 click)
- **Hips drive the clean**, NOT the arm pulling — feel the hinge, then the snap
- At rack: bell on the chest, **elbow tucked into ribs**, wrist neutral
- Press: **ribs DOWN** (no rib flare), bicep finishes by the ear, full lockout
- Lower with control to rack — no slam
- **Both sides feel symmetric?** That's the goal. Right has been ahead of left — make left match right's quality, not chase right's speed.

**Reflection:** does the knee-bent catch feel automatic? That cue should be invisible by now.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Light row, 2s pull / 2s lower. NOT a strength set — just enough to feel the back. Skip if shoulders are cooked from yesterday's push.
60s each side. Hip flexor release — same as legs day cooldown. Don't shortcut this.
60s. Adductors get tight on KB days from B-stance and cossacks.

tips: **RESTRUCTURED FROM W8** — Tuesday is a skill + mobility day now, NOT a strength day. The heavy strength block (50lb C&P/rows/goblet/swings) is gone.
**NEW W10:** Seated mini-band shoulder prep added after the warmup (@sculptherbody flow). 7 positions, ~3 min, sustained tension. Activates scaps + rear delts before bent press / TGU / C&P.
Heaviest bell touched today: 35lb (and that's the C&P spotlight — technique drilling at light load).
5 hard days/week = injury territory. Tue should leave you fresher than Mon, not more cooked.
Spotlights: TGU checkpoints (left side weak link) and C&P technique (knee-bent catch + ribs-down lockout).
Modern KB cues embedded — hike pass, B-stance heel up, lateral hinge bent press, checkpoint TGU, knee-bent C&P catch.
Cooldown is mandatory: pigeon + butterfly. Hips and adductors get tight on KB days — don't skip.
If anything feels heavy, the load is wrong for this day. Pull weight, slow down, or skip the block.
formPhilosophy: KB Tuesday is skill + mobility, not strength. Modern KB cues drilled at light loads — hip drive on swings, leverage on bent press, checkpoint discipline on TGU, knee-bent catch on C&P. Form is the entire progression. The strength days are Mon/Wed/Fri; this is the recovery flank.

name: Week 10 Legs — Form-First Return (Spotlight: Squat + Incline RDL)
description: Form-first cycle, easing back from W9 deload. NOT a return to W8 PR loads. **Spotlight lifts:** squat (drill the bottom-position depth + intra-abdominal bracing — your W9D5 milestone of being able to live in the bottom is the foundation) and incline RDL (drill the hip hinge + slow eccentric — the W7/W8 lesson that slow IS the progression). Lunges drop way back (40 → 25) — the QL spasm origin lift, treated with respect. Watch the back all session.

dayOverview.theme: Easing back from W9 deload with form-first cues. Squat depth + bracing get drilled. QL stays the watchpoint — abort any lift that twinges.

spotlights.why: W9D5 milestone: 55/side became a place you could live in the bottom of. W10 builds on that — drill the brace pattern + depth at slightly heavier (65/side), with full attention on the bottom position. Form is the progression.

W7/W8 confirmed: 25lb dead slow with 2s pause torches the hammies. The slow eccentric IS the stimulus. Drill the hip hinge pattern — this is the incline RDL on the 45° bench, not a lower-back extension.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 squat + incline RDL · W11 leg press · W12 seated leg curl + calf raise · W13 lunge (when QL is fully cleared) · W14 cycle back. ~4 weeks per cycle.
watchpoints: **QL is the #1 priority.** Any twinge = abort the lift. The whole point of this week is to confirm recovery, not push.
Lunges are the QL spasm origin lift — 25lb max, drop further if anything feels off, or skip outright if the back is talking
Incline RDL is in the SPOTLIGHT but loaded conservatively — slow tempo, don't chase the 25lb plate yet
Leg press at 3 plates medium ROM (W9 deload load) — DON'T jump to 4 plates full ROM yet, that's the hidden hip-stabilizer hammer

exercises.notes: Full depth, slow. Find where the hips are tight and breathe into it. This is also a depth-check before loading.
Big slow circles. Wake up the hip stabilizers gently.
Spine mobility — extra important post-QL-spasm.
Bodyweight, feel the stretch. Use this to assess the QL — if anything talks here, modify the day.
Set 1: 45/side x8. Set 2: 55/side x8. Same depth as working sets. The W9D5 depth — sit in the bottom position, this is where you live now.

**LOAD:** 65/side (one notch back from W8's 75-80). 4 sets at 65 — clean and deep, not a pyramid this week.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Bar setup:** high-bar position on upper traps (NOT on the neck). Squeeze the upper back to make a shelf.
- **Brace like prepping for a punch** — deep belly breath at the top, hold the breath through the descent (intra-abdominal pressure), exhale on the drive up. This is NOT a lower-back exercise; it's an abs-and-everything exercise.
- **Hips back AND down**, not just down. The hips initiate the descent.
- **"Spread the floor"** with the feet — active outward rotation through the feet engages the glutes / hip abductors. Feels like screwing the feet into the ground.
- **Knees track over toes** — the 2010 myth is dead. Knees following toes is normal and healthy. What matters is the line, not the position.
- **Same depth as W9D5** — sit in the bottom, no rushing. Brief pause before the drive.
- **Drive through the WHOLE foot** — heel + ball + outer edge together. Not toes, not heels alone.

**Reflection:** does the bottom position feel as solid at 65 as it did at 55 on W9D5? If yes, the brace is working. If not, the weight is ahead of the form.

**LOAD:** 25lb plate, dead slow with 2s pause at the bottom (hold W8 — slow IS the stimulus, no need to load up).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Pad position:** just below the hip crease — NOT on the upper thigh, NOT on the stomach. The pad is the hinge fulcrum.
- **This is the incline RDL on the 45° bench — a HIP HINGE, not a lumbar extension.** The lower back stays NEUTRAL throughout — neither rounded at the bottom nor hyperextended at the top.
- **At the bottom:** keep neutral spine, feel the stretch in the **hamstrings and glutes**, NOT the lower back. If the lumbar rounds, you're going too deep for current mobility.
- **Drive up by squeezing the glutes and hamstrings** — these are the prime movers, NOT the spinal erectors.
- **At the top:** stop at neutral. Driving past neutral = lumbar load = QL irritation. The top is a straight line from heels to head, no further.
- **Tempo:** 3s eccentric, 2s pause at bottom, 1s drive up. The slow eccentric is the entire reason this lift works.

**Reflection:** are you feeling this in the hamstrings/glutes (correct) or in the lower back (wrong — modify pad position or pull weight)?

**LOAD:** 3 plates/side, **medium ROM** (the W9 deload load — DO NOT jump to 4 plates full ROM yet). Hold week — spotlight is W11.
- Feet shoulder-width, mid-foot on platform, slight toe-out
- **Press through the WHOLE foot** — heels grounded, push the platform with heels and ball together
- Medium ROM — stay in the strong part of the range. No deep 4-plate version this week (that's the stabilizer hammer)
- Slow descent (3s), no bounce at bottom
- **Watch:** if the hips lift off the pad at depth, you're going too deep — back off

**LOAD:** 3 plates/side, single leg (back from W8's 4 plates). Hold week.
- Ball of foot on platform edge
- **Full stretch at the bottom** — let the heel drop below the platform, that's the growth signal
- 1s squeeze at the top, full plantarflexion
- 2s descent, no bouncing
- Plate-under-foot variation lives in W11+ when the back is fully quiet

**LOAD:** 100 (back from W8's 110). Settings 5/m/0/1. Hold week.
- Ankles UNDER the pad, knees aligned with the machine pivot
- Lap pad locked down firmly
- Curl heels toward glutes — full ROM, full squeeze
- 2s eccentric, 1s squeeze at peak
- Don't let the torso lift forward — that's lower back, not hamstring

**LOAD:** 25lb DBs (WAY back from W8's 40 — this is the QL spasm origin lift, treated with respect). Walking lunges.
- **Long stride forward** — short stride = knee crashes forward = knee stress
- **Vertical shin on the front leg** — knee tracks toes, doesn't crash forward
- **Back knee toward floor** — 1-2" off, don't slam
- **Drive up through the front HEEL**, not the toes
- Torso vertical — no leaning forward to cheat

**ABORT IF:** any twinge in the lower back / QL area. Stop the set immediately. This lift is the canary — if the back is talking here, drop weight further or skip outright. Lunges return to spotlight in W13 only when the QL is fully cleared.

tips: **FORM-FIRST WEEK** — easing back from W9 deload, NOT chasing W8 PRs. The QL is still the watchpoint.
**Spotlight lifts** are squat + incline RDL — drill those cues every set. Other lifts get sharp/short cues only.
**Squat 65/side**: drill the bottom position (the W9D5 milestone). 4 clean sets, not a pyramid this week.
**Incline RDL 25lb dead slow**: hip hinge, not lower-back work. Hamstrings/glutes feel it, NOT lumbar.
**Leg press stays at 3 plates medium ROM** — DON'T return to 4 plates full ROM yet. That's the hidden stabilizer hammer.
**Lunges 25lb max** — way down from 40. This is the QL spasm origin lift. Abort any lift that talks.
**Rotation plan:** W11 leg press · W12 seated leg curl + calf raise · W13 lunge (when QL is fully cleared).
Rest 3 min squats, 90s incline RDL, 2 min leg press, 90s isolations.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building. Modern 2026 cues — knees-over-toes is fine, hip hinge for posterior chain, brace like prepping for a punch. Build the bottom position before chasing depth at heavier loads. Building decades, not weeks. The QL is still the boss this week.

name: W10 Morning Flow — Bodyweight Spine + Hip (All Days)
description: 15 min bodyweight floor flow — **universal W10 morning flow, used every training day** (Mon push / Tue KB / Wed pull / Thu KB / Fri legs). Three-part: spine primer → bodyweight work → deep stretch arc. All bodyweight, no equipment beyond a mat. Scorpion-family moves carry the spine work; cossack BW + 90-90 + active pigeon + lizard + frog carry the hips. Replaces the day-specific morning flows (push pec-minor protocol, pull dead-hang/scap-prep, legs QL release) with one consolidated bodyweight flow. **Day-specific therapeutic work to do separately when needed:** doorframe pec-minor stretch (push days, anytime desk work tightens it back up), 90s pigeon (legs day, if QL talks), dead hang + scap pull-ups (pull day, if grip needs warming).

sets.name: Primer — Wake the Spine (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Full spinal wave — ride the breath. Inhale on cow (chest open, gaze up), exhale on cat (round, tuck). 10 slow reps.
Slow flow back and forth. Hips back to heels in child's, then slide forward and press up into cobra. Spine wave, not a calisthenics rep.
30s each side. Deep thoracic rotation — sink the shoulder, breathe into the stretch. **This is the scorpion family's setup move.**

sets.notes: Get the spine moving before any load. Slow, breath-led, no rush.

sets.name: Work Block — Bodyweight Activation (~5 min)

exercises.notes: 5 slow reps. Hinge to floor, walk hands out to plank, **brief chest-down stretch in plank**, walk back. Hamstrings + shoulders + core in one move.
45s. **Slow** — knee to elbow, hold a beat, switch. NOT a circuit rep speed. The hold opens the hip and challenges anti-rotation at the same time.
45s. Flow plank ↔ down-dog with intent. Calves stretch every time the heels press toward the floor.
5 each side, slow. Opposite arm + leg, **pause at full extension** for 2 counts. Anti-rotation + glute activation.
30s each side. Side plank, top arm threads under and back. **Lateral core + thoracic rotation** — the standing equivalent of a windmill, on the floor.

sets.notes: Light bodyweight challenge — gets warm without fatiguing. If anything feels hard, slow down (don't drop reps).

sets.name: Yoga Flow — Deep Stretch Arc (~7 min)

exercises.notes: 30s each side. **The signature move of this flow.** Lie face down, sweep one leg across to the opposite hand. Keep chest and arms flat. Breathe into the rotation — this opens hip flexors AND mobilizes the lumbar/thoracic spine simultaneously.
30s each side. Front leg 90°, back leg 90°. **Sit tall on the front-leg sit-bone** — hinge forward over the front shin for the deeper version.
30s each side. Low lunge with hands inside the front foot. Hip flexor + adductor stretch — the back-leg quad lengthens at the same time. Drop to the elbows for deeper.
Bodyweight, slow. **Mirrors the Tuesday loaded cossack** — same depth focus, no load. Heel stays down on the working leg.
30s each side. Press into the stretch — actively engage the front-leg glute, don't just sink. Hip flexor of the back leg stays long.
60s. Knees wide, shins parallel, hips back over heels. **Adductor opener** — the move B-stance flow + cossacks won't reach. Stay only as long as the stretch feels productive.

sets.notes: Continuous arc — flow between shapes, breathe deeply. Goes last because the spine and hips are now warm and stretch deeper.

sets.name: 📍 Day-Specific Add-On — Pick by Today's Training (~3-5 min)

exercises.notes: **📍 PUSH DAYS — KEY W9 PROTOCOL MOVE.** 30s each at LOW / MID / HIGH elbow positions (each hits a different pec minor fiber angle). Arm bent 90°, forearm on door frame, step through and rotate torso AWAY. **Desk work continuously re-tightens pec minor — doing this daily is what keeps the right-shoulder-blade ache from returning.** Carried over from `fb-w9-push-morning-flow`.

**📍 PUSH DAYS** — Active side of the pec-minor fix. Face down, arms in Y overhead, hold at top 1 second. Lower trap + rotator cuff; pulls the shoulder blade down and back — direct antagonist to tight pec minor.
**📍 PUSH DAYS** — Arms locked, only shoulder blades move (retract → protract). Serratus activation helps unstick a tight pec minor.
**📍 PUSH DAYS (optional)** — Lie on side, elbow at 90°, rotate forearm up. Gravity is the resistance. Slow, controlled. Adds to the rotator cuff prep if shoulder feels off.
**📍 PUSH DAYS (optional)** — Back flat against wall. Pins the shoulder blades in the corrected position — counteracts forward rolling from pec tightness.
**📍 PULL DAYS** — 25s. Decompress shoulders, warm grip. Do at the gym before pull-ups if no bar at home.
**📍 PULL DAYS** — From dead hang, just the scapulae retracting (no elbow bend). Crisp scap engagement before loaded pulls. Carried over from `fb-w8-pull-morning-flow`.

**📍 LEGS DAYS — W8 QL-SPASM PROTOCOL.** 90s each side (longer than the universal flow's 30s active pigeon). The QL/psoas share fascial connections — the *long* hold is what lets them release. **Non-negotiable on legs day.** Carried over from `fb-w9-legs-morning-flow`.
**📍 LEGS DAYS** — 60s each side. Gentle twist for the QL — let gravity do it. If a lacrosse ball is around, roll the QL above the iliac crest 1-2 min before this.
**📍 LEGS DAYS (optional)** — 60s each side. Deep hip flexor stretch — exactly the area that was seizing on W8D5. Add if hip flexors feel tight.
**📍 LEGS DAYS (optional)** — 60s each side. Most direct quad + hip flexor stretch. Wall or couch — knee bent, opposite foot up behind.

sets.notes: **Pick today's day-specific protocol from the list — push / pull / legs.** ~3-5 min total. KB days (Tue/Thu) skip this block — the in-workout banded prep covers the equivalent. **The universal flow above intentionally doesn't carry these moves; this block is the show-notes reminder so they don't get lost.**

tips: **15 min main flow + 3-5 min day-specific add-on** — universal across W10 (Mon push / Tue KB / Wed pull / Thu KB / Fri legs).
**Scorpion-family carries the spine work** — thread-the-needle, scorpion stretch, side-plank thread-needle, child's pose to cobra. Same intent as the seated banded flow, no equipment.
Work block before the yoga flow — warm body = deeper stretches.
Should feel restorative, not draining. The training session follows; this is prep, not a workout.

**Day-specific add-on block at the end** — push days do the pec-minor protocol (doorframe stretch + prone Y + scap push-ups), pull days do dead hang + scap pull, legs days do 90s pigeon + supine twist. KB days skip the add-on (banded prep in the workout itself covers it).
Variety comes from the W11 flow next week (same shape, rotated moves).
If short on time, the non-skippable moves are: scorpion, 90-90, cossack BW, active pigeon, plus today's day-specific add-on.
formPhilosophy: Bodyweight floor mobility, scorpion-family spine work, hip-flexor/adductor stretches. Designed for home (no bands) and structured around the same logic as the in-workout banded prep: sustained shapes prep the KB hinge before any load.

name: Week 10 Pull — Form-First Return (Spotlight: Pull-up + DB Row)
description: Form-first cycle, weights pulled back ~10-15% from W8 highs. **Spotlight lifts:** pull-up (drill the active-hang start + elbows-down-to-ribs cue) and DB row (drill the chest-supported torso lock + elbow-to-hip line). Everything else holds. Pyramid sets stay (DB row), but the heaviest set comes off — it's about clean reps, not chasing 85.

dayOverview.theme: Form-first return from W9 deload. Pull-up pattern + row pattern get drilled. Modern 2026 cues over chasing reps.

spotlights.why: W7 hit 32 with mixed grips, but cumulative fatigue made later sets sloppy. W10 lock in the active-hang start position + elbows-down-to-ribs pull pattern so every rep is structurally honest, not just a chin-over.
W7 hit 80x10 clean on bench. Drill the chest-supported torso lock (no rotation cheating) + elbow-to-hip pull line. The 80 stays as top set — no 85 push this week, the cue is the progression.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 pull-up + DB row · W11 chest-supported row + lat pulldown · W12 face pull + reverse fly · W13 preacher curl + high cable curl + cable crunch. ~4 weeks per cycle, then refresh.
watchpoints: Grip fatigue limiting lat pulldown more than back strength (W3 onward) — straps are an option if it persists
Wider grip on lat pulldown reduces bicep involvement — keep flexible, wide on heavy pull-up days
Reverse fly: seated bent-over is the default form (W3 discovery), not standing

exercises.notes: Scap activation. Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the back, soft elbow.
Spine warm — slow, full ROM each direction.
**Active hang, not collapsed** — engage the lats, pull the shoulders down away from the ears even at full extension. This is the position the pull-up starts from.

**LOAD:** Bodyweight. Vary grips across sets — climber handles, wide, normal.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Start from an ACTIVE hang** — at full extension, lats engaged, shoulders pulled DOWN away from ears. Not a dead/collapsed hang. The active hang IS the start position.
- **Pull elbows DOWN to the ribs**, NOT chin to bar. The chin clearing is a marker; the work is the lat squeeze.
- **Chest leads to bar** at the top — drive the chest forward, mid-back squeezed, no neck craning.
- **Eyes ahead, head neutral** — no chin tucking, no head pumping. The neck is along for the ride.
- **Slow eccentric**, ~2-3 seconds down, return to active hang (not collapsed).
- **No kipping.** If you can't get a clean rep, the set ends — better to do 6 strict than 8 with kip.

**Reflection:** which grip felt the most honest today? That's a clue about which lat angle is strongest right now.

**LOAD:** 12.5/side (back from W8's 15/side). Sharp cues only — spotlight is W13.
- **Armpit pressed firmly into the pad** — not just resting. Back of upper arm flat against the pad.
- Full stretch at the bottom — don't lose tension by curling short
- Curl toward face, not chest
- 3s eccentric — fight the long-head stretch on the way down

**Swap:** if preacher is taken, incline curl at 25 (W7+ backup, proven solid — `incline-curl`, NOT concentration curl; user has never done concentration variation).

**LOAD:** Pyramid 60x10, 70x10, 80x8 (no 85 this week — cleanest 80 you can do is the goal).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Chest-supported on the incline bench** — chest fully on the bench, no arching, head neutral. The bench eliminates torque cheating.
- **Pull elbow toward HIP, not toward armpit** — toward armpit = rear delt; toward hip = lat.
- **Don't rotate the torso** — both shoulder blades stay parallel to the floor at all times. Rotation = unilateral lat torque, fakes the weight.
- **Slow 2s descent**, brief 1s squeeze at the top
- **Don't slam the DB at the bottom** — control the stretch, that's where the lat lengthens
- Head stays neutral throughout — no looking up to help heave

**Reflection:** does the elbow-to-hip line feel different from rowing-toward-armpit? You should feel mid-back instead of rear delt.

sets.name: Station 2: Chest Supported Row + Lat Pulldown

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 35lb (back from W8's 40-45). Hold week — spotlight is W11.
- Chest stays glued to the bench — if it lifts off, the weight is too heavy
- Same elbow-to-hip cue as DB row
- 1s squeeze at top, 2s eccentric

**LOAD:** 145 strict (back from W8's 160). Hold week.
- **Wide grip near the bend** — reduces bicep involvement, isolates lats
- Slight lean back ~15°, NO further (more = becomes a row)
- **Pull bar to upper chest** — never behind the neck (shoulder grinder)
- Elbows down and back, "lats to back pockets"
- 1s pause at the bottom, 2s return to full overhead stretch

**LOAD:** 45 (back from W8's 50). Hold week — slow tempo is the stimulus.
- Rope at upper-chest / face height, step back to set tension
- **Pull rope to face, ELBOWS HIGH (above shoulders)**
- **External rotate at the top** — knuckles point UP, like a double-biceps pose
- 1s pause at peak contraction
- Slow controlled return

**LOAD:** 22.5 (back from W8's 25). Hold week.
- **Seated bent-over** — chest toward thighs (the W3 discovery, default form)
- Soft elbow ~10-15°, fixed throughout
- Lead with elbow, hand passive
- Wings out and slightly UP to shoulder height
- Slow controlled both directions, no swing

sets.name: Finisher: High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 50 (back from W8's 60). 2 sets only.
- Stand between cables, arms out at shoulder level
- **Upper arms parallel to floor, fixed** — if elbows drop, becomes a regular curl
- Curl hand to ear, peak squeeze
- Slow eccentric, no bounce at start

**LOAD:** 100 (back from W8's 110-120). 2 sets only.
- Kneel under high cable, rope behind head, **HIPS LOCKED**
- **Crunch from the abs only** — spine flexes, hips don't move. Hips moving = hip flexor work, not abs.
- Chest toward thighs, hold the crunch 1 second
- Slow eccentric

tips: **FORM-FIRST WEEK** — every weight is intentionally below W8. The progression IS the form, not the load.
**Spotlight lifts** are pull-up + DB row — drill those cues every set. Other lifts get sharp/short cues only.
**Rotation plan:** W11 chest-supported row + lat pulldown · W12 face pull + reverse fly · W13 preacher curl + finisher work.
Pull-up: clean strict reps only. If a rep can't be clean, the set ends. Better 6 strict than 8 with kip.
DB row: pyramid stays (60/70/80) but no 85 push this week. The cleanest 80 you've ever done is the win.
Cable row is permanently gone. Chest supported row is the replacement (W7 lock-in).
Rest: 2.5 min pull-ups, 2 min stations 1-2, 90s station 3, 60s finisher.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building. Modern 2026 cues, not 2010 bro-science. Active hangs > dead hangs. Elbow-to-hip > elbow-to-armpit. ROM, tempo, and joint stacking are the progressions. Building decades, not weeks.

name: Week 10 Push — Form-First Return (Spotlight: Push Press + Incline DB)
description: Form-first cycle, NOT a return-to-PR week. Weights pulled back ~10-15% from W8. The progression this week is the form. **Spotlight lifts (drill these all session):** push press and incline DB press. Everything else holds and gets sharp cues only. Goal: leave the gym feeling like every rep was honest.

dayOverview.theme: Form-first return from W9 deload. Modern 2026 cues, lighter loads, cleaner reps.

spotlights.why: W7 found parallel feet > duck feet. W10 locks that in plus rib-down + vertical bar path. Make it automatic this week.
W9D1 discovered the forearm-stacked-over-elbow cue makes 40lb honest. Drill that cue until it's the default setup, not a thought.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 push press + incline DB · W11 chest press machine · W12 lateral raise + cable fly · W13 cable lat raise + tri ext + straight arm pulldown. ~4 weeks per cycle, then refresh.
watchpoints: Right scap → front-pec referral on chest press (W9D1) — slow tempo first, drop weight only if needed
Yuri band warmup is non-negotiable for shoulder tolerance
If a cue can't be held, the cue wins — drop the weight or the rep

exercises.notes: Slow, big circles. Wake up scap glide before loading.
Full sequence with red band. The shoulder won't tolerate cable work without it — never skip on push day.

Sets the press template for the day.

**Form cues:**
- Ribs down, glutes tight, body in one line — no pike, no sag
- Hands stack DIRECTLY under shoulders (not in front, not wide)
- Slow eccentric, 1s pause with chest 1-2" off the floor

**LOAD:** 40/side (one notch back from W8's 45 — give the form room to rebuild).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Feet parallel, hip-width** — NOT duck stance. The W7 discovery, locked in.
- **Ribs DOWN before the dip** — squeeze the front of your ribs toward your hips. No rib flare = no lower-back compensation.
- **Shallow dip** (~3-4 inches), straight down through the heels. The dip is for legs to drive, not for momentum.
- **Vertical DB path** — dumbbells go from front-of-shoulders straight up to *over the heels* at lockout (two parallel lines next to your ears). Head pokes through at the top. If the DBs drift forward, the lockout is fake.
- **Breath:** in at the dip, out on the drive.

**Reflection:** which cue slipped first when you got tired? That's next week's drill. Stop the set if rib position breaks.

**LOAD:** 20lb (hold). Sharp cues only — this is a hold week for laterals.
- Soft elbow ~10-15° bend (locked elbow stresses the joint, recruits traps)
- Lead with the elbow, hand passive
- Stop at shoulder height — higher just shrugs traps
- 2-second eccentric

**LOAD:** 165 (back off from W8's 180/185). Hold-and-clean week — spotlight is W11.
- Seat aligned so handles hit **mid-chest / nipple line**, NOT shoulder height
- Set scaps back AND down into the pad before unracking
- Slow eccentric, 3 seconds down
- Press to ALMOST lockout — leave a hair of bend
- **Watch:** right scap → front-pec referral (W9D1). If it twinges, slow down further before dropping weight.

**LOAD:** 45lb pair (back off from W8's 50).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set. The W9D1 discovery is the foundation:**
- **Bench at ~30°**, NOT 45°. Steeper = more front delt, less upper chest.
- **Shoulders packed back AND down** — pin the back of the shoulder into the bench, like you're trying to break it in half with your scaps.
- **Forearm stacked vertically over the elbow at the bottom** — elbow directly under the wrist when DBs are at chest level. *This* is what made 40lb feel hard W9D1. The cue IS the progression.
- **Elbows ~45° from torso** — not flared 90°, not tucked tight
- **Pause 1s at the bottom** — "dead weight" before driving up
- Press to a fingerwidth apart at the top. No DB clack.

**Swap option:** if the shoulder feels off, swap to seated DB shoulder press at 35-40lb using the W9D1 rotation cue — DBs facing IN at the bottom (palms toward face), rotating to facing-OUT at the top. That cue gives the shoulder real relief.

**Reflection:** does the forearm stack feel automatic by set 4, or are you still thinking about it?

**LOAD:** Kinesis 8 (hold).
- Slight forward hinge at hips — chest leads, not head
- Soft elbow ~10-15°, fixed throughout
- Think "hug a tree" — squeeze from chest, not arms
- Stop a fistwidth apart at top, no crash
- Slow eccentric for full pec stretch

**LOAD:** cable column ~30-40 (W7-W8 working range was 45-50; pull back ~10-15% for form-first). One arm at a time, 3 sets per side.
- Stand sideways to the machine, working arm farther from the stack
- Soft elbow ~10°, fixed throughout
- Lead with elbow, hand passive — wrist follows
- Stop at shoulder height (any higher = traps)
- Fight the cable on the way down, no snap-back
- **W10 trick that worked:** massage out right pec between sets — kills the crackle. Roll/engage scapula too.

**LOAD:** ~50 (cable column kneeling — your actual setup, not 'incline' as the name implies).
- Kneel facing away from the stack, rope attachment overhead
- Torso slightly forward, head between arms — that's your 'incline' position
- Elbows pinned, only forearms move
- Full stretch at the bottom, long head loads deep
- Stop just shy of lockout at top, slow eccentric

**LOAD:** cable column ~100 (W7=110, W8=120; W10 form-first pullback). Cable column has been the setup the entire program — W7-W8 logs and W10D1 actuals all confirm.
- Stand a step back, hinge ~15° at hips
- Slight elbow bend (5°), fixed throughout — perfectly straight = injury risk
- Pull to **thighs**, NOT past — past = shoulders roll forward, lats off
- Squeeze lats hard at bottom — "oranges into back pockets"
- Slow controlled return to full overhead stretch

tips: **FORM-FIRST WEEK** — every weight is intentionally below W8. The progression IS the form, not the load.
**Spotlight lifts** are push press + incline DB — drill those cues every set. Other lifts get sharp/short cues only.
**Rotation plan:** W11 chest press · W12 lateral raise + cable fly · W13 cable lat raise + tri ext + straight arm pulldown. ~4-week cycle.
If a cue can't be held, the cue wins — drop weight, slow tempo, or skip the rep. Don't grind a bad rep.
Yuri band warmup is non-negotiable. Push press swap to seated DB shoulder press (rotation cue) is a legit option if the shoulder isn't ready to stand-and-load.
Rest 2 min push press, 2.5 min chest press (form takes more rest than ego does), 75s cables.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building. Modern 2026 cues, not 2010 bro-science. Joint stacking, ROM, tempo, breath are the progressions. Load is the last lever. Building decades, not weeks.

name: Week 11 KB Conditioning — Skill Drill (Spotlight: Windmill Mechanics)
description: Thursday is the lightest day of the program. Recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs. **Spotlight rotates to windmill mechanics** — the bent press's sibling lift, same lateral-hinge family. Drill the long-spine + hip-flexor-of-back-leg cue at 18-20lb. Tuesday's bent press leverage cue should now feel automatic; the windmill borrows the same hinge and adds the deeper hamstring-and-back-leg-quad reach. 20 min, zero leg loading, no swings, no C&P, no goblet squats.

dayOverview.theme: Thursday recovery + skill drilling. Spotlight: windmill — the bent press's sibling. Same lateral-hinge family, deeper reach into the back leg.

spotlights.why: Windmill and bent press share the lateral-hinge family. Bent press got the spotlight in W10 Thursday; W11 Thursday hands the spotlight to windmill so the family completes. The windmill goes deeper into the back leg's hip flexor and the hamstrings — drill that reach at light load (18-20lb), spine-long, eye-on-bell.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 bent press · W11 windmill mechanics (current) · W12 TGU left-side checkpoints · W13 cycle back to bent press. Thursday is always lighter than Tuesday's spotlight.
watchpoints: **Zero leg loading.** No swings, no C&P, no goblet squats, no lunges. Legs day is tomorrow.
If anything feels heavy, the load is wrong. Pull weight or skip the block.
Windmill stretch should land in the **hip flexor of the back leg + hamstrings**, NOT the lower back. Lower-back stretch = side-bending, not hinging.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Slow circles, both directions. Shoulder loosener.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Heels tucked, mini-band overhead, hands wider than shoulders. **Sustained tension.** Or use the W10D2 alt: blue rope band gripped in hands.
Pull band down behind head — **elbows drive to ribs**, scaps depress.
Band wraps the upper back. **Press out to T** — fires rear delts and mid-traps.
Band at sternum, **pull apart, scaps retract**, ribs down.
Sweep arms further back. **Don't overarch** the lower back.
15s each side. Half-windmill with band — **primes today's windmill spotlight directly.**
Both arms in V/Y overhead. **Hold and breathe** — lower traps fire here.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Sustained tension, no rest between positions. Diagonal reach is today's direct prime for the windmill spotlight.

sets.name: OTD Flow (Light Groove)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 20lb. Light and smooth.
- Bell starts on the floor between feet
- Hips drive the lift, not the arm
- Catch in rack with knees slightly bent
Goblet/rack position. Sit tall, full depth, no bouncing.
Stride out wide, sit into the working leg, keep the other leg long. Mobility, not strength.
Tuesday's spotlight cue (leverage — body goes down, bell barely moves). Light groove here.
**Control on the way down** — bell to floor with the same cleanness as up.

sets.notes: 20lb, 2 reps each side x 2 rounds. Should feel effortless throughout.

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 18-20lb (light to keep cues clean — NOT 25). 2 rounds.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Bell locked overhead, EYES TRACK THE BELL** — same as bent press. Lose the eyes, lose the path.
- **Hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — this is the windmill's bent-press inheritance. Push the bell-side hip OUT.
- **LONG SPINE** — the stretch should land in the **hip flexor of the back leg + hamstrings**, NOT the lower back. If you feel it in the lumbar, you're side-bending — re-set, hinge instead.
- **Reach the free hand toward the INSIDE of the front foot** — that's the line of pull. Outside foot = wrong angle.
- **Bell stays vertical** the entire time — if it drifts inward, the arm is collapsing
- **Slow descent, brief pause at the bottom, controlled return** — speed kills the groove

**Reflection:** does the stretch land in the back leg's hip flexor (correct), or the lower back (wrong)? The windmill is a hip-and-back-leg lift; the lower back should feel NEUTRAL throughout. If it doesn't, drop to 15lb or shorten the descent.

**LOAD:** 18-20lb. Light groove of last week's spotlight — the leverage cue should now feel automatic.
- Lateral hinge AWAY from bell side
- Press up by moving body DOWN — bell barely moves
- Eye on bell, slow descent, controlled

**LOAD:** 25lb (max bell of the day). Light intent — drill the **left side** transitions (still the weak link).
- Pause every checkpoint: lying → elbow → hand → hip bridge → leg sweep → lunge → stand → reverse
- Reverse with the same discipline
- Left side first
60s each side. Hip flexor release — KB flow + cossack + lateral lunge fries the hip flexors. This counters.
60s. Adductors get tight from B-stance, cossack squats, and lateral lunges.

tips: **THURSDAY = SKILL + MOBILITY, NOT WORKOUT.** ~23 min, light. Recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs.
**Spotlight: windmill mechanics.** Drill the long-spine + back-leg hip-flexor reach at 18-20lb. The bent press's sibling lift — same hinge family.
**Zero leg loading.** No swings, no C&P, no goblet squats, no lunges.
Heaviest bell: 25lb (TGU only). Most of the session at 18-20lb.
**Banded prep diagonal reach is today's direct prime** — half-windmill with band sets up the windmill spotlight.
Cooldown is mandatory — pigeon + butterfly.
Should feel effortless. If anything feels like work, you've gone too hard.
Rotation plan: W12 TGU left-side checkpoints · W13 cycle back to bent press.
formPhilosophy: Thursday = skill becomes default movement. Windmill at light load drills the lateral hinge family deeper now that the bent press version is automatic. No load chase, no rep chase — the cue is the whole point. Hips and adductors get the cooldown they need.

name: Week 11 KB Full Body — Skill + Mobility (Spotlight: Swing Mechanics + Bent Press Leverage)
description: Second week back from W9 sick rest. Tuesday-as-skill/mobility-flank framing held in W10D2 — left fresher, no QL signal on swings, big TGU breakthrough at 40lb. **Spotlights this week:** swing mechanics (the B-stance flow opener — drill the hike pass + hip snap) and bent press leverage (the lateral hinge cue — drill it deeper now that 25lb feels home-base). **C&P projection from W10D2:** start at 40, work toward 45. **TGU:** can run 30 or 40 — W10D2 confirmed 40 felt weightless with the 'focus on the clean' cue.

dayOverview.theme: Tuesday is still the skill/mobility flank, NOT a strength day. Spotlights drill swing mechanics + bent press leverage. C&P ramps per the W10D2 projection (start at 40, toward 45). TGU stays in the 'clean is the work' groove.

spotlights.why: The B-stance flow opens every Tuesday at 25lb x 3 each side x 3 rounds. W10D2 'felt great, light' — that's the green light to drill the mechanics deeper, NOT to add load. The hike pass + lats packed + hips snap forward pattern shows up in EVERY KB lift downstream (clean, snatch, swing). Make it bulletproof here, the rest of the day inherits it.

W10D2 'back to home-base 25, felt right' — the lateral hinge cue is durable. W11 drills it deeper now that the load isn't a question. The press-up-by-moving-body-DOWN cue is the unlock; this week make it automatic, not a thought.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 C&P + TGU · W11 swing mechanics + bent press leverage (current) · W12 windmill + cossack mobility · W13 cycle back to C&P + TGU. ~3 weeks per cycle.
watchpoints: **C&P ramp from W10D2 projection:** 40 start, 45 if 40 feels grooved by round 2. **NO 50 yet** — that's the QL-spasm-territory weight from W8. The 35→40→45→50 ramp needs all 3 KB sessions.

**TGU at 30 or 40** — W10D2 'focus on the clean itself made each side feel weightless' even at 40. If the clean groove is there in the warmup, run 40. If the clean feels off, run 30. Not 25 anymore — that's behind you.
**Banded prep — blue rope band swap (W10D2 win)** is the reusable alternative to the mini-band. Both work. Pick what's around.
QL/lower back on swings — even at 25lb, the hip-hinge-under-load pattern is the W8/W9 trigger. W10D2 was clean. Keep watching.

exercises.notes: Light bell (15-20lb), big slow circles. Loosen the shoulders before any overhead work.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Find your depth. Sit in the bottom for a beat. Mobility, NOT a working set.

**LOAD:** 25lb (light, just for the hinge groove).
- Hike pass between the legs, NOT a squat-and-lift
- Lats packed down, bell stays close
- Hips snap forward — bell floats to chest height
- **This is the spotlight cue preview** — make this 10-rep warmup the cleanest thing of the day.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Heels tucked, mini-band overhead, hands wider than shoulders. **Sustained tension.** **Or use the W10D2 alt: blue rope band gripped in hands** — produced 'massage-level' shoulder relaxation.
Pull band down behind head — **elbows drive to ribs**, scaps depress.
Band wraps the upper back. **Press out to T** — fires rear delts and mid-traps before C&P.
Band at sternum, **pull apart, scaps retract**, ribs down.
Sweep arms further back. **Don't overarch** the lower back to chase range.
15s each side. Half-windmill with band — primes the bent press lateral hinge spotlight.
Both arms in V/Y overhead. **Hold and breathe** — lower traps fire here.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Sustained tension throughout, no rest between positions.

sets.name: 🔆 Block 1: B-Stance Flow (Swing Mechanics Spotlight)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD — W10D2 confirmed sweet spot, 'felt great, light'). Same volume as W10D2 (3 each x 3 rounds).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **B-stance: heel UP on the back foot** — most weight on the front leg (~70/30)
- **Hike pass between the legs**, NOT a squat-and-lift. The bell goes BACK before it goes UP.
- **Lats packed DOWN** — bell stays close to the body, doesn't drift away
- **Hips snap FORWARD** — bell floats to chest height on momentum, NOT lifted by the arms. The arms are ropes; the hips are the engine.
- **Glutes finish the snap** — at the top of the swing, glutes squeezed hard, ribs down (no flare)
- Eye on the bell, stay tall, no looking down

**Reflection:** does the hike pass feel like a hike, or like a lift? If the arms are doing more than guiding, slow down and re-find the hinge.
**Catch into rack on the swing.** Soft elbow into the body — no slam. The W3 'knee-bent catch' cue applies here too — absorb the impact.
Stay in B-stance, squat from rack. Front-loaded squat — chest tall, back foot heel up.
Punch overhead from rack. Lock out hard at the top — bicep by the ear, ribs down (no flare). The snatch INHERITS the swing mechanics drilled above — if the swing was clean, the snatch is clean.
Bell locked overhead, step into lunge. Bicep stays glued to the ear. Long stride, vertical front shin.

sets.notes: 25lb. 3 reps each side x 3 rounds. Same volume as W10D2 — proven dose. Don't add load.

sets.name: 🔆 Block 2: Skill Work (Bent Press Leverage Spotlight + Cossack + Windmill)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD — W10D2 confirmed home-base felt right). 2 rounds.
