**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the leverage every rep:**
- **Bell starts in rack, eye on bell throughout** — the head turn IS part of the lift
- **Lateral hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — push the bell-side hip out sideways, knee soft on bell side. NOT a side bend.
- **Spine stays LONG** — if the lower back compresses, you're side-bending. Hinge = hip + glute load. Side-bend = QL load.
- **THE LEVERAGE CUE — 'press up by moving body DOWN, not by pushing bell UP.'** The bell barely moves; your body changes shape under it. This is the durable cue from W7 — make it automatic this week.
- Free hand can drift toward inside of front foot for counterbalance — bell-side stays vertical
- Slow descent back to rack, controlled — no slam

**Reflection:** at rep 3, are you pressing the bell up, or moving your body down? They're not the same lift. The leverage version conserves shoulder energy; the press version torches it.

**LOAD:** 25lb goblet (HOLD — W10D2 'slow-and-deep produces a hard stop at end-range, range is the work'). Spotlight is W12.
- Sit DEEP, heels stay down
- Working leg in full external rotation
- Mobility, not strength — heel popping up = drop to 20 or shorten stance

**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD — W10D2 'felt good'). Spotlight is W13 (Thursday) and revisits via cycle.
- Bell locked overhead, eye tracks bell
- **Hip hinge AWAY from bell side** (same pattern as bent press — they share the family)
- Reach free hand toward inside of front foot
- Long spine — stretch in **hip flexor of the back leg**, NOT lower back

**LOAD:** **30 or 40** (W10D2 confirmed 40 'feels weightless' with the clean-focus cue — pick based on how the clean grooves in the first round). NOT 25 — that's behind you. Spotlight was W10; sharp checkpoint discipline only this week.

- Pause at every checkpoint: lying → elbow → hand → hip bridge → leg sweep → lunge → stand → reverse
- **Eyes ON the bell** every checkpoint
- **'Focus on the clean itself' cue** (W10D2 unlock) — the setup IS the work: punch the bell to a stable STRAIGHT-ARM overhead lockout before the get-up. The TGU is a straight-arm lift — the arm stays locked vertical throughout, no rack hold during the get-up. If the overhead lockout is wobbly, the rest of the lift inherits the wobble.
- **Left side first** — give it the freshest reps. Weak side first, every time.

**LOAD:** **40 start, 45 if 40 feels grooved** (W10D2 explicit projection: 'start at 40 next week, work toward 45'). Spotlight was W10; sharp cues only.

- **Catch the clean with knees slightly bent** — the W3 cue, still durable
- **Hips drive the clean**, NOT the arm
- Rack: bell on chest, elbow tucked into ribs, wrist neutral
- Press: **ribs DOWN** (no rib flare — the W8 trigger pattern), bicep finishes by the ear, full lockout
- Lower with control — no slam

**Both-sides symmetry check:** right has historically led; left should match right's *quality*, not chase right's speed. If left at 45 feels rougher than right at 40, drop left to 40 — quality wins.

**NO 50 today.** That's W12 territory at earliest, not now.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Light row, 2s pull / 2s lower. NOT a strength set — just enough to feel the back. Skip if shoulders are cooked.
60s each side. Hip flexor release.
60s. Adductors get tight on KB days from B-stance and cossacks.

tips: **Spotlights: B-stance swing mechanics + bent press leverage.** Both at home-base 25lb. The cue is the progression, not the load.
**Swing mechanics drill:** hike pass (bell goes BACK before UP), lats packed down, hips snap forward. The arms are ropes; hips are the engine. This pattern shows up in every downstream lift.
**Bent press leverage drill:** 'press up by moving body DOWN, not pushing bell UP.' The body changes shape under the bell. Lateral HINGE, not side bend.
**C&P ramp 40 → 45 per W10D2 projection** — start at 40, jump to 45 if 40 grooves. NO 50 yet.
**TGU at 30 or 40** based on how the clean grooves — NOT 25 anymore. Left side first.
**Banded prep:** mini-band OR blue rope band held in hands (W10D2 alt). Both work.
Cooldown is mandatory — pigeon + butterfly. Hips and adductors get tight on KB days.
Rotation plan: W12 windmill + cossack mobility · W13 cycle back to C&P + TGU.
formPhilosophy: KB Tuesday = skill + mobility. Spotlights drill the durable cues at home-base loads. The W10D2 lessons (swing was 'almost too light' = right dose, TGU 'clean is the work', C&P 35→40 ramp on track, bent press home-base felt right) ARE the program. Form is the entire progression.

name: Week 11 Legs — Form-First (Spotlight: Leg Press)
description: Second week of the form-first cycle. Spotlight rotates to **leg press** — drill the whole-foot drive + medium-ROM discipline at 3 plates. **Big W10D5 takeaways baked in:** squat holds at 65/side (the 'weightless middle' is normal biomechanics, brace pattern is locking in — drill the brace deeper, don't chase 70 yet); seated leg curl gets a HARD RESET to 50 with corrected ankle placement (W10D5 discovery — the prescribed 100 was a wrong-lever weight); calf raise switches to double-leg slow OR single-leg faster (single-leg + slow eccentric was too much together). Lunges held at 25 — no QL spasm last week.

dayOverview.theme: Leg press in the spotlight. Squat holds at 65 — the brace pattern is the work, not 70. Seated leg curl resets to 50 with corrected ankle placement (W10D5 form discovery). Calf raise tempo strategy changes.

spotlights.why: W10D5 confirmed 3-plate medium ROM is doing the protective work — when depth got deeper the QL engaged and you backed off. That's exactly the right pattern, and it's a SETUP problem, not a strength problem. W11 spotlights the leg press: drill foot position + whole-foot drive + medium-ROM discipline. The rep that goes 'a little deeper' than yesterday's W10D5 rep is what unlocks the next phase — but only if foot mechanics are dialed in first.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 squat + back extension · W11 leg press (current) · W12 seated leg curl + calf raise · W13 lunge (only when QL fully cleared) · W14 cycle back.
watchpoints: **QL still the watchpoint** — W10D5 confirmed lunges at 25 produced no spasm (big win), but the leg press still talks when depth goes too deep. Hold 3 plates medium ROM. Don't jump to 4 plates full ROM yet.

**Seated leg curl HARD RESET — start at 50 with corrected ankle placement.** The W10D5 discovery: the prescribed 100 was working through a wrong lever (pad too high on calf). Rebuild from 50 with the pad UNDER THE ANKLES.
**Calf raise tempo strategy shift** (W10D5): single-leg + slow eccentric was too much together. This week: double-leg with 3-4s eccentric, OR single-leg at faster tempo. Pick one, not both.
Squat 'weightless middle' is biomechanically normal — mid-range is the easiest zone of a back squat. The bottom and top are where brace + drive matter.

exercises.notes: Full depth, slow. Find where the hips are tight and breathe into it. Depth-check before loading.
Big slow circles. Wake up the hip stabilizers gently.
Spine mobility — extra important post-QL-spasm history.
Bodyweight, feel the stretch. Use this to assess the QL — if anything talks here, modify the day.
Set 1: 45/side x8. Set 2: 55/side x8. Same depth as working sets.

**LOAD:** 65/side (HOLD — W10D5 confirmed bottom feels as solid at 65 as 55 felt at W9D5; 'last 2 sets got better as the brace locked in'). 4 sets at 65 — not chasing 70 this week. The brace deeper IS the progression.

**Sharp cues only — spotlight was W10:**
- Bar high on upper traps (NOT on neck), upper-back shelf squeezed
- **Brace like prepping for a punch** — deep belly breath at top, HOLD through descent, exhale on drive up
- **Hips back AND down**, not just down
- 'Spread the floor' — screw feet into the ground for glute / hip abductor activation
- Knees track over toes (line, not position)
- Same depth as W9D5 — sit in the bottom
- Drive through the WHOLE foot — heel + ball + outer edge

**Watchpoint — the 'weightless middle':** mid-range of a back squat IS biomechanically the easiest zone (moment arms favor you when both hip + knee are at moderate angles). Feeling lighter through the middle = you're moving smoothly through the easy zone, NOT a strength leak. Bottom and top are where the brace + drive matter — that's where to focus attention.

**Reflection:** does the bottom position feel cleaner this week than W10D5? If 'sit in the bottom and breathe' feels automatic at 65, the brace pattern is locked. THAT is the green light for 70 — not a calendar week.

**LOAD:** 3 plates/side, **medium ROM** (HOLD — W10D5 confirmed this is doing the QL-protective work; depth past medium = QL engages). 4 sets here (up from W10's 3 — the spotlight gets the volume).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Foot position FIRST:** feet shoulder-width, mid-foot on platform, slight toe-out. **Set this BEFORE loading the press, not after — if the feet are wrong, every rep inherits the wrongness.**
- **Press through the WHOLE foot** — heels grounded, push the platform with heels and ball together. NOT toes (knee stress, quad-dominant), NOT heels alone (loses balance).
- **Medium ROM — stay in the strong part of the range.** The depth past medium is the W10D5 QL trigger; the protective work is staying ABOVE that depth. This is NOT a 'go deep or go home' lift right now.
- **Slow descent (3s), no bounce at the bottom** — count the eccentric.
- **Don't lock out hard at the top** — leave a hair of bend, don't joint-stack.
- **Watch:** if the hips lift off the pad at depth = too deep. Back off. The hip lift IS the QL signal.
- **Don't chase the load** — W10D5 'easy, hard not to add more' is the trap. The cue is the progression: medium-ROM with cleaner foot mechanics is the work.
- **Breath:** in on descent, out on drive.

**Reflection:** does medium ROM feel different on set 4 with foot mechanics dialed vs W10D5's set 3? If the rep feels *cleaner* at the same depth, the spotlight is working — the next progression is more depth at clean foot mechanics, NOT more weight at sketchy depth.

**LOAD:** 3 plates/side. **Tempo strategy: PICK ONE this week (W10D5 discovery — single-leg + slow was too much together):**

**Option A — DOUBLE-LEG SLOW:** both feet on the platform, **3-4s eccentric**, 1s squeeze at the top. The slow eccentric IS the stimulus — don't worry about 'unilateral' work this week.

**Option B — SINGLE-LEG, FASTER:** ball of foot on platform edge, normal tempo (~1-2s descent), full ROM. Add the slow eccentric back in once the calf has the strength.

**Recommend Option A this week** — W10D5 said 'really hard slow, dying at 15, needed other-foot support for the negatives.' That's the calf saying 'I can't carry both load AND tempo on one foot yet.' Double-leg slow gives the slow tempo without the unilateral load.

**Form (both options):**
- Ball of foot on platform edge
- **Full stretch at the bottom** — let the heel drop below the platform, that's the growth signal
- Full plantarflexion at the top
- No bouncing at the bottom or top

Spotlight is W12 (joint with seated leg curl). Sharp cues only this week.

sets.name: Seated Leg Curl (HARD RESET — Corrected Ankle Placement)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** **50** (HARD RESET — W10D5 discovery: the prescribed 100 was working through a WRONG LEVER because the pad was too high on the calf. The correct setup is the pad UNDER THE ANKLES, and at correct lever 50 is genuinely working the hamstrings).

**Setup discipline — THIS IS THE WORK THIS WEEK:**
- **Ankles UNDER the pad** — not the mid-calf, not the upper calf. The ankle bone is the contact point.
- Knees aligned with the machine pivot — adjust the seat back if needed
- Lap pad locked DOWN firmly (not loose)
- Settings on Arrilaga machine: **5/m/0/1** (W8 corrected baseline)

**Movement:**
- Curl heels toward glutes — full ROM, full squeeze
- 2s eccentric, 1s squeeze at peak
- **Don't let the torso lift forward** — that's lower back, not hamstring
- No bouncing out of the bottom

**Reflection:** does 50 with correct ankle placement feel like genuine hamstring work? If yes — that's the new baseline. The W7-W10 numbers (90, 100, 110) are NOT comparable to this scale because they were on the wrong lever. Rebuild upward from 50 over the next 2-3 sessions, with the pad-under-ankles cue as the non-negotiable.

Spotlight is W12 — when it cycles in, the spotlight will be the form correction itself.

**LOAD:** 25lb plate (HOLD — W10D5 'first set easy, second set way harder, slow tempo really is hard'). Dead slow with 2s pause at the bottom. Spotlight was W10; sharp cues only.

**Naming note:** this is the **incline RDL** on the 45° hyperextension bench, NOT a back extension. The form is a hip hinge — not a lumbar extension. Calling it 'back extension' has caused drift; the lift is RDL on a machine.
- Pad just below the hip crease (NOT upper thigh, NOT stomach)
- Hip hinge — think 'Romanian deadlift on the incline bench'
- Lower back NEUTRAL throughout
- At the bottom: stretch in **hamstrings and glutes**, NOT lower back
- Drive up by squeezing glutes and hamstrings
- At the top: stop at neutral — driving past = lumbar load = QL irritation
- Tempo: 3s eccentric, 2s pause, 1s drive

**W10D5 watch:** 'left side legs really tight' — the hammie tightness pattern. If left tightness gets worse this week, drop to 20lb or shorten ROM.

**LOAD:** 25lb DBs (HOLD — W10D5 confirmed 'no QL spasms' at this load, big confidence boost). Walking lunges. Spotlight is W13.

**Sharp cues:**
- **Long stride forward** — short stride = knee crashes forward = knee stress
- **Vertical shin on the front leg** — knee tracks toes
- Back knee toward floor — 1-2" off, don't slam
- Drive up through the front HEEL, not the toes
- Torso vertical — no leaning forward to cheat

**ABORT IF:** any twinge in the lower back / QL area. The W10D5 'no QL spasms at 25' is a green light to HOLD 25, NOT a green light to push to 30. Earn the way back to 30 over 2-3 weeks of clean 25, not in one jump.

Lunges return to spotlight in W13 — by then, multiple clean weeks at 25 should make a careful 30-35 ramp possible.

tips: **Spotlight: leg press.** Drill foot position + whole-foot drive + medium-ROM discipline at 3 plates. The cue is the progression, NOT the load.
**Squat HOLDS at 65/side** — the W10D5 brace is locking in. The 'weightless middle' is biomechanically normal (mid-range is the easiest zone). Drill the brace deeper, don't chase 70.
**Seated leg curl HARD RESET to 50** — the W10D5 discovery: prescribed 100 was working a wrong lever. Pad UNDER ANKLES. Rebuild from 50.
**Calf raise: pick ONE — double-leg slow OR single-leg faster.** W10D5 confirmed single-leg + slow eccentric was too much together. Recommend double-leg slow this week.
**Lunges HOLD at 25** — W10D5 'no QL spasms' is a green light to HOLD, NOT a green light to push. Earn the way back to 30 over 2-3 clean weeks.
**Back extension HOLDS at 25lb dead slow** — left-side hammie tightness watch from W10D5.
**QL still the boss.** Any twinge = abort the lift.
Rotation plan: W12 seated leg curl + calf raise · W13 lunge (when QL fully cleared) · W14 cycle back.
Rest 3 min squats, 2 min leg press, 90s isolations.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building, week 2. Loads HOLD; foot mechanics + ankle placement + brace pattern get drilled deeper. The W10D5 form discoveries (leg curl wrong lever, calf raise tempo trap, squat brace locking in, lunges QL-clear at 25) ARE the program — the next phase only unlocks when the cues are automatic, not when the calendar says so.

name: W11 Morning Flow — Bodyweight Spine + Hip (All Days, Variety Week)
description: 15 min bodyweight floor flow — **universal W11 morning flow, used every training day** (Mon push / Tue KB / Wed pull / Thu KB / Fri legs). **Same shape as W10, rotated moves for variety.** Three-part: spine primer → bodyweight work → deep stretch arc. All bodyweight, no equipment beyond a mat. W11 swaps in puppy-pose cat-cow, plank bird-dog, down-dog knee-to-head, hip-circle, and mountain-to-pigeon flow. Scorpion stays — signature move, still working. Day-specific therapeutic work (doorframe pec-minor stretch, 90s pigeon for QL, dead hang + scap pull-ups) lives outside this flow — do separately when needed.

sets.name: Primer — Wake the Spine (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Knees under hips, chest sinks toward the floor with arms extended forward, hold 2-3 breaths. Then walk back into cat-cow. **Variety on W10's cat-cow** — adds the shoulder/lat opener to the spine wave.
Slow flow. **W10 had this too — keep it, the spine wave is the bridge into the rest of the flow.**
30s each side. Deeper than W10 — by W11 the rotation should reach further.

sets.notes: Slightly different opener than W10 — puppy-pose cat-cow adds the lat opener that the seated banded flow does on Tuesday.

sets.name: Work Block — Bodyweight Activation (~5 min)

exercises.notes: 5 slow reps. Same as W10 — keep it, this move covers a lot of ground per rep.
45s alternating. From down-dog, draw one knee toward the forehead, hold a beat, switch. **Core + hip + shoulder integration** — replaces W10's plank-to-down-dog.
45s alternating. Plank with opposite-arm-and-leg lift — harder version of W10's kneeling bird-dog. Anti-rotation under more load.
45s slow. Same as W10 — too good to skip, the slow-hold version stays in.
30s each side. Same as W10 — windmill on the floor. Should feel cleaner than W10 if the lateral hinge cue is sticking.

sets.notes: Slightly more demanding than W10 (plank bird-dog + down-dog knee-to-head are both more challenging than their W10 equivalents). If anything feels heavy, slow the tempo or drop the reps — don't push.

sets.name: Yoga Flow — Deep Stretch Arc (~7 min)

exercises.notes: 30s each side. **Stays — the signature move and the named winner from W10D2.** Should feel deeper this week.

30s each direction. Quadruped hip circles — slow, full ROM, draw big circles with the knee. **Replaces W10's 90-90** — different hip pattern, same goal of getting the hip joint moving in all planes before deep static stretches.
30s each side. Same as W10 — hip flexor + adductor + back-leg quad. Drop to elbows if accessible.
Bodyweight, slow. **Stays every week** — mirrors the Tuesday loaded cossack.
30s each side. **Replaces W10's active pigeon** — flowing entry from down-dog/mountain into pigeon. Adds the transition practice that static pigeon doesn't have.
45s, slow pulses. **Replaces W10's frog-stretch** with the gentle pulse version — more dynamic, easier to bail if it's not opening up that day. Adductors get the same treatment.

sets.notes: Continuous arc — flow between shapes, breathe deeply. Same logic as W10 but the moves rotated for variety.

sets.name: 📍 Day-Specific Add-On — Pick by Today's Training (~3-5 min)

exercises.notes: **📍 PUSH DAYS — KEY W9 PROTOCOL MOVE.** 30s each at LOW / MID / HIGH elbow positions. Arm bent 90°, forearm on door frame, step through and rotate torso AWAY. **Desk work continuously re-tightens pec minor — daily.**
**📍 PUSH DAYS** — Active side of the pec-minor fix. Lower trap + rotator cuff; pulls shoulder blade down and back.
**📍 PUSH DAYS** — Serratus activation, helps unstick a tight pec minor.
**📍 PUSH DAYS (optional)** — Slow rotator cuff work. Add if shoulder feels off.
**📍 PUSH DAYS (optional)** — Pins shoulder blades in corrected position; counteracts forward roll from pec tightness.
**📍 PULL DAYS** — 25s. Decompress shoulders, warm grip.
**📍 PULL DAYS** — Scapulae retracting only, no elbow bend. Crisp scap engagement before loaded pulls.
**📍 LEGS DAYS — W8 QL-SPASM PROTOCOL.** 90s each side (longer than the universal flow's mountain-to-pigeon). The long hold is what lets QL/psoas release. **Non-negotiable on legs day.**
**📍 LEGS DAYS** — 60s each side. Gentle twist for the QL. Lacrosse-ball QL roll above iliac crest beforehand if available.
**📍 LEGS DAYS (optional)** — Deep hip flexor stretch. Add if hip flexors feel tight.
**📍 LEGS DAYS (optional)** — Most direct quad + hip flexor stretch. Wall or couch.

sets.notes: **Pick today's day-specific protocol — push / pull / legs.** ~3-5 min total. KB days (Tue/Thu) skip this block — the in-workout banded prep covers the equivalent.

tips: **Same shape as W10, rotated moves.** Variety keeps the flow interesting — same intent, different shapes.
**15 min main flow + 3-5 min day-specific add-on** — universal across W11 (Mon push / Tue KB / Wed pull / Thu KB / Fri legs).
**Scorpion + cossack BW + lizard pose stay every week** — those are the durable spine + hip + adductor anchors.
Plank bird-dog and down-dog knee-to-head are slightly harder than W10's equivalents. Slow the tempo if needed.
All bodyweight, all floor — home-friendly.
**Day-specific add-on block at the end** — push days do the pec-minor protocol, pull days do dead hang + scap pull, legs days do 90s pigeon + supine twist. KB days skip the add-on.
If short on time: scorpion + lizard + cossack BW + mountain-to-pigeon, plus today's day-specific add-on.
formPhilosophy: Same as W10. Variety = different shapes hitting the same patterns. Scorpion-family spine work + hip openers + adductor stretches. Designed for home, structured around the in-workout banded prep's logic.

name: Week 11 Pull — Form-First (Spotlight: Chest-Supported Row + Lat Pulldown)
description: Second week of the form-first cycle. Spotlights rotate to **chest-supported DB row + lat pulldown** (per the rotation tracker). **Equipment-consolidated layout** — direct response to the W10D3 meta-feedback that the workout was bouncing between cable column / Kinesis / single pulley / DBs / racks. W11 groups everything by zone so you finish all the work at one piece of equipment before walking to the next: pull-up bar → DB rack/bench (4 exercises, one trip) → lat pulldown machine → cable column (face pull + curl + crunch, one trip). Each cable lift now specifies WHICH machine (so weights stay comparable week-to-week, the other W10D3 callout).

dayOverview.theme: Spotlights move to chest-supported row (drill chest-glued-to-bench + elbow-to-hip) and lat pulldown (drill wide grip + bar to upper chest, NEVER behind neck). **Equipment-consolidated** — pull-up bar → DB rack/bench (all 4 DB lifts in one circuit) → lat pulldown → cable column (face pull + bicep curl + crunch). Bicep work and crunch live at the cable column, right next to the lat pulldown. No backtracking.

spotlights.why: W10D3 held at 35lb cleanly — 'don't feel the need to go up.' That's the green light to drill the form, not the load. Chest-glued-to-bench + elbow-to-hip pull line at 35 is the work — when a rep feels easy at this weight with strict form, THAT'S the progression.

W10D3 held 140 with 'rested more before last set, full 12 slow' — the slow controlled rep IS the progression. W11 drills wide-grip + bar-to-upper-chest + slow return at 140. No jump to 150 yet — the cue is the progression.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 pull-up + DB row · W11 chest-supported row + lat pulldown (current) · W12 face pull + reverse fly · W13 preacher curl + high cable curl + cable crunch · W14 cycle back to pull-up + DB row.
watchpoints: **Recent ~15lb weight gain** — pull-ups are noticeably harder than W7's 32. Quality reps are the priority, NOT chasing W7 numbers.
**Left bicep weaker than right** (W10D3 incline curl: right 10 hard, left 7 strict + assist). Add to the asymmetry watchlist; left side gets the freshest reps.

**Equipment-specificity protocol** (W10D3 callout): every cable lift below names WHICH machine. High cable curl scales differently across single vs dual pulley, cable crunch scales differently across cable column vs single-pulley regular machine. Weights aren't comparable across setups.
Grip fatigue limiting lat pulldown more than back strength (W3+ pattern) — straps are an option.

exercises.notes: Scap activation. Squeeze shoulder blades together, soft elbow.
Spine warm — slow, full ROM each direction.
**Active hang, not collapsed** — engage lats, pull shoulders DOWN away from ears even at full extension. This is the position the pull-up starts from.

**LOAD:** Bodyweight. **Reps adjusted DOWN from W10's 6-8** because the recent ~15lb gain is making pull-ups noticeably harder. 5-7 strict > chasing 8 with kip.

**Mix grips across sets:**
- Set 1: adjustable climber-style handles, **narrow** grip (W10D3 won here — felt natural)
- Set 2: adjustable climber-style handles, **normal** grip
- Set 3: adjustable climber-style handles, **wide** grip
- Set 4: any grip — go for clean reps

**Form cues (sharp — spotlight was W10):**
- Active hang start (not collapsed)
- Pull elbows DOWN to ribs, NOT chin to bar
- Chest leads to bar at the top
- Eyes ahead, head neutral — no chin tucking
- Slow eccentric, ~2-3s, return to active hang
- **No kipping.** Set ends if you can't get a clean rep.

sets.name: 🔆 Station 2: DB Rack / Bench Block (Chest-Supported Row Spotlight + DB Row + Concentration Curl + Reverse Fly)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 35lb (hold — W10D3 confirmed 'don't feel the need to go up' and 'feel the engagement well').

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Chest stays GLUED to the bench** — if it lifts off, the weight is too heavy. The bench eliminates torque cheating; that's the whole point.
- **Pull elbow toward HIP, not toward armpit** — toward armpit = rear delt; toward hip = lat.
- **Both elbows pull together, simultaneous** — asymmetric pulling = slow down.
- **Head neutral** — don't drop or lift, changes the spinal line.
- **2s eccentric, 1s squeeze at top** — slow tempo at 35 is the progression.
- Don't slam DBs at the bottom — control the stretch.

**Reflection:** does 35 feel different at the end of slow set 3 vs end of set 1? When 35 with strict form starts feeling 'too easy' across all 3 sets, THAT'S the green light for 40 — not before.

**LOAD:** Pyramid 60x10, 70x10, 80x8 (hold — W10D3 confirmed 'all sets per plan, hard but doable, don't feel need to go up'). Spotlight was W10; sharp cues only.
- Chest-supported on incline bench — chest fully on the bench
- Pull elbow toward HIP, not armpit
- Don't rotate the torso — both shoulder blades parallel to floor
- Slow 2s descent, 1s squeeze
- No slam at the bottom

**LOAD:** 25lb (hold — W7+ proven swap from preacher; 25 is honest). 3 sets.
- Sit on incline bench (~45-60°), back against the bench, DBs hanging at sides
- **Full stretch at the bottom — pause 2s** (W11D3 user cue)
- Curl smoothly to peak squeeze
- 2-3s eccentric — fight the long-head stretch

**Left bicep watchlist (W10D3):** right 10 was hard, left got 7 strict + assist for last 3. Hit LEFT FIRST today, give it the freshest reps. If left dies at 7 strict, stop the set there — don't assist past the asymmetry. The weak side gets quality, not chase.

**Swap:** preacher curl at 12.5/side if it's free — same full-stretch-at-bottom cue.

**LOAD:** 25lb (hold — W10D3 bumped from 22.5 to 25, 'felt good'). Spotlight is W12.
- **Seated bent-over** — chest toward thighs (W3 default form)
- Soft elbow ~10-15°, fixed throughout
- Lead with elbow, hand passive
- Wings out and slightly UP to shoulder height
- Slow controlled both directions, no swing

sets.notes: **One trip to the DB rack covers all 4 lifts.** Recommended flow: pyramid your DB row weights first (need to pick up 60s/70s/80s anyway), then chest-supported row at 35, incline curl at 25, reverse fly at 25. Either run as a circuit (A→B→C→D, rest, repeat 3x) or as straight sets (3 of A, then 3 of B, etc.) — both work since you're not moving equipment. The bench stays at incline ~30° for the rows; for the incline curl keep the bench inclined (back against it), then sit upright for the fly.

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 140 (hold — W10D3 confirmed 4 sets at 140 'solid', the slow rep IS the progression at this weight). 4 sets.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Wide grip near the bend** — wider grip = more lat isolation, less bicep. Critical when biceps are torched from pull-ups.
- **Slight backward lean ~15°, NO further** — more = becomes a row. The bar travel should feel like the bar comes to YOU, not you to the bar.
- **Pull bar to UPPER CHEST — NEVER behind the neck.** Behind-the-neck pulldowns are a shoulder grinder with zero benefit.
- **Elbows down and back, 'lats squeezing oranges into back pockets'** — the lat squeeze IS the rep, the bar is just along for the ride.
- **1s pause at the bottom contraction** — count it.
- **2s controlled return to full overhead stretch** — this is where the lat lengthens. Speeding up the return = losing the stimulus.
- Knees pinned firmly under pad, feet flat.

**Reflection:** does the 'lats to back pockets' cue feel different on the 4th set than the 1st? If form is holding 4 sets at 140 (W10D3 only had 3 + 1 rested), the cue is sticking. Arrilaga lat pulldown weight scales vary across machines.

exercises.equipment: cable column (mid/upper-chest height + rope) — THE cable column for the whole finisher
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column **42.5** (hold — W10D3 confirmed 'machine doesn't have 45'). Slow tempo IS the stimulus. Spotlight is W12.

**Setup — pick the cable column you'll use for the rest of the workout** (this is where the high cable curl and cable crunch live too — pick one that's not getting hogged):
- **Cable column, rope at upper-chest / face height** — step back to set tension. After this exercise, you'll just slide the rope up to the high attachment for the curl + crunch finisher.

**Form cues:**
- **Pull rope to face, ELBOWS HIGH (above shoulders)** — this is the make-or-break cue
- **External rotate at the top** — knuckles point UP, like a double-biceps pose
- 1s pause at peak contraction
- Slow controlled return
- W10D3: hard at rep 17 set 2, hard at rep 19 set 3 — that's exactly the right load.

sets.name: Station 5: Cable Column — Finisher (High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch, Same Cable As Face Pull)

exercises.equipment: cable column (high attachment) — same column you just used for face pull
exercises.notes:
**LOAD: FIND IT — first time on the cable column for this lift.** 2 sets only. Spotlight is W13.

**Why no number:** historical data is on different machines and won't translate:
- W7 dual pulley: ~60
- W8 / W10D3 single high pulley (regular pulley): 100-120 ('120 a lot better/harder still slow')
- **Cable column: NO history.** Pulleys, mechanical advantage, and increments vary — 120 from the regular pulley will NOT feel like 120 here.

**How to find it (set 1 is the calibration):**
- Start conservative (~50) and ramp up by 10-20 between reps if it feels light
- Target the W10D3 quality cue: **'still slow' on the eccentric, peak squeeze at the top, upper arm parallel and fixed.** When that's the limiter, you've found the weight.

**Setup — stay at the cable column:**
- **High attachment, single D-handle** (one arm at a time) OR **rope** (both arms simultaneously, faster). Pick one.
- Step back so the cable runs at a slight forward angle from the high anchor to your shoulder
- Stand sideways (D-handle, one arm) or facing the column (rope, both arms), arm(s) out at shoulder level

**Form cues:**
- **Upper arm parallel to floor, FIXED** — if elbow drops, becomes a regular curl
- Curl hand to ear, peak squeeze
- Slow eccentric, no bounce at start

**Bail-out:** if calibrating on the cable column eats both your sets and you didn't get any quality work, walk to the regular pulley next session and run 120 there — the consolidation isn't worth losing the stimulus.

exercises.equipment: cable column (high attachment + rope) — same column, just swap attachment
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column **110-120** (hold — W7-W8 baseline on the cable column). 2 sets only. Spotlight is W13.

**Setup — stay at the cable column:**
- **High attachment + rope** (swap from the D-handle / curl rope if needed — same column)
- Kneel under the high cable

**Form cues:**
- Rope behind head, **HIPS LOCKED** (most important cue)
- **Crunch from the abs only** — spine flexes, hips don't move
- Hips moving = hip flexor work, not abs
- Chest toward thighs, hold the crunch 1 second
- Slow eccentric

**If the cable column is taken** and you use a different cable machine (e.g., the W10D3 single-pulley regular machine where 57.5 was easy), the scale is different — 110-120 here won't translate.

tips: **Spotlights: chest-supported row + lat pulldown.** Drill the form-cues at last week's loads — 35 and 140 are the working numbers, not the targets to beat.

**Equipment-consolidated order** (W10D3 meta-feedback) — pull-up bar → DB rack/bench (4 lifts in one trip: chest-supported row + DB row + incline curl + reverse fly) → lat pulldown machine → cable column (face pull + high cable curl + cable crunch all stay at the same cable). One trip per zone, no backtracking.

**Bicep work lives at the cable column now (in spirit)** — incline curl is at the DB rack only because it shares the bench with rows; the high cable curl is right next to the lat pulldown at the cable column. After lat pulldown, you stay at the cable column for face pull → curl → crunch.
**Equipment-specificity protocol** (W10D3 callout) — every cable lift names WHICH machine. Weight scales differ across machines.
**Pull-up reps adjusted to 5-7** — recent ~15lb gain is making them harder, no point chasing W7's 32. Mix grips: narrow / normal / wide / clean.
**Concentration curl: LEFT FIRST today** — left bicep is the weak side (W10D3 asymmetry watchlist). Freshest reps to the weak side.
**DB reverse fly held at 25** (W10D3 bump confirmed). DB row pyramid held at 60/70/80.
Rotation plan: W12 face pull + reverse fly · W13 preacher curl + high cable curl + cable crunch · W14 cycle back.
Rest: 2.5 min pull-ups, 90s DB/bench block (4 lifts in circuit), 2 min lat pulldown, 90s face pull, 60s finisher.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building, week 2 of the cycle. Same loads as W10D3, sharper layout. Station discipline (the W10D3 ask) lets the cues land — when you stop machine-hopping, the brain has more bandwidth for chest-glued-to-bench, lats-to-back-pockets, hips-locked. The cue IS the progression.

name: Week 11 Push — Form-First (Spotlight: Chest Press Machine)
description: Second week of the form-first cycle. Spotlight rotates to **chest press machine** — drill the seat-height + scap-pack pattern at 165 (the W10D1 edge-of-form weight, no jump to 170 yet). W10D1 callouts baked in: scap-reset between lateral raise sets, kneeling cable column on the overhead tri ext, cable column 100 on straight-arm pulldown. **Equipment-consolidated layout** — one trip to each zone. DB rack zone (push press + lat raise + incline DB) → chest press machine → Kinesis → cable column block. No backtracking.

dayOverview.theme: Chest press machine moves into the spotlight. Other lifts hold their W10 edge-of-form weights with sharp cues only. **Equipment-consolidated order** — finish all DB-rack work before moving to the chest press machine, then Kinesis, then cable column. One trip per zone.

spotlights.why: W10D1 hit 165x10/10/10/6 — three honest sets, fourth set form broke (couldn't pick up after rest). 165 IS the edge of form right now, not a stepping stone to 170. W11 drills the seat alignment + scap pack + 3s eccentric at 165 to make every rep cleaner. The right-pec/scap referral is real — slow tempo first, drop reps before dropping weight.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 push press + incline DB · W11 chest press machine (current) · W12 lateral raise + Kinesis cable fly · W13 cable lat raise + incline tri ext + straight-arm pulldown · W14 cycle back to push press + incline DB.
watchpoints: **Right scap → front-pec referral on chest press** (W9D1, persisting through W10D1) — slow tempo first, drop reps before dropping weight. Massage out right pec between sets — W10D1 trick that worked.

**Right shoulder crackle** — present but manageable. Scap reset between sets (roll shoulders + active engage/disengage scap) is the W10D1 fix that turned bad sets into good ones. Default it now, don't wait for the crackle.
Yuri band red warmup is non-negotiable for shoulder tolerance.
If a cue can't be held, the cue wins — drop weight, slow tempo, or skip the rep.

exercises.notes: Slow, big circles. Wake up scap glide before loading.
Full sequence with red band. Non-negotiable on push day — the cable lat raise crackle is a scap-glide problem, not a strength problem.

**Form cues:**
- Ribs down, glutes tight, body in one line — no pike, no sag
- Hands stack DIRECTLY under shoulders (not in front, not wide)
- Slow eccentric, 1s pause with chest 1-2" off the floor

sets.name: Station 1: DB Rack — Overhead (Push Press + Lateral Raise)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 40/side (hold — W10D1 confirmed this is the right working weight). 3 sets, 6-8 clean.
- **Feet parallel, hip-width** — locked in W7
- **Ribs DOWN before the dip** — W10D1 set 1 had rib flare, controlled by sets 2-3. Catch it earlier this week.
- Shallow dip ~3-4", straight down through the heels
- Vertical DB path, head pokes through at the top
- Stop the set if rib position breaks.

**LOAD:** 15lb x 20 (hold — W10D1 actuals were 15lb x 20 each set; the W10 prescription said 20lb x 15 but the user dropped to 15 x 20 and it worked). Sharp cues only — spotlight is W12.
- Soft elbow ~10-15° bend (locked elbow stresses joint, recruits traps)
- Lead with elbow, hand passive
- Stop at shoulder height — higher just shrugs traps
- 2s eccentric
- **W10D1 default:** roll shoulders + actively engage/disengage scapula BETWEEN sets. Don't wait for the crackle — set 1 got the shooting line last time, sets 2-3 were way better after the scap reset. Make the reset the default protocol.

sets.name: Station 2: DB Rack — Incline Bench (Same Zone, Adjust Bench to 30°)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 45lb pair (hold — W10D1 actuals 45x10/8/9/7 across 4 sets; honest at 30°, hard from rep 7-9, no need for 2-up-2-down). Spotlight is W14 (cycle back); sharp cues only this week.
- **Bench at 30°**, NOT 45°. Steeper = more front delt, less upper chest.
- **Forearm stacked vertically over the elbow at the bottom** — automatic at 30°. Keep it.
- **Shoulders packed back AND down** into the bench — pin the back of the shoulder, like trying to break the bench in half with scaps
- Elbows ~45° from torso
- 1s pause at the bottom
- Press to a fingerwidth apart, no DB clack

**Equipment note:** stay at the DB rack — adjust the same bench you used for warmup/lat raise to 30°. Same zone as the overhead block, just sit down. No need to walk anywhere yet.

**Swap:** seated DB shoulder press at 35-40lb with the W9D1 rotation cue (DBs facing IN at bottom → OUT at top) if shoulder isn't ready to stand-and-load.

**LOAD:** 165 (hold — W10D1 confirmed this is THE edge-of-form weight, NOT a stepping stone to 170 yet). 4 sets, target 10/10/10/8.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Seat height: handles align with mid-chest / nipple line**, NOT shoulder height. High handles = front-delt press, not chest. Set this BEFORE you sit down — get out and re-adjust if it's wrong.
- **Sit into the backrest — shoulders pinned BACK against the back pad, chest tall.** Sitting upright is the foundation. If your shoulders roll forward off the backrest, the load shifts from chest to shoulders. Settle in BEFORE engaging the foot lever / handles.
- **Hands neutral on the handles, wrists stacked over forearms** — no wrist break.
- **Slow eccentric, 3 seconds back toward the chest** — count it. The handle path is guided, so all your attention can go to tempo. The W10D1 'huge struggle on the third set' was form starting to break because tempo got rushed. Slow IS the stimulus.
- **Stretch at the back without shoulders rolling off the backrest** — let the handles travel back as far as the machine allows. If shoulders chase more stretch by rolling forward, you've lost the line. Stop where shoulders stay pinned.
- **Press to ALMOST extension** — leave a hair of bend at the elbow, don't lock and stack on the joint.
- **Squeeze the chest at the press** — the machine guides the path, so use the freed attention to actively contract the pecs at the top of each rep.
- **Right pec / scap referral protocol:** if it twinges → slow tempo FIRST, drop reps SECOND, drop weight LAST. **Massage out the right pec between sets** — W10D1 trick that worked. Roll the right shoulder + reset scap retraction against the pad too.
- **Breath:** in as the handles travel back, out on the press.

**Reflection:** does the right pec referral feel different by set 4 with the shoulder-pinned-to-backrest + pec massage protocol baked in? If the form-first protocol is working, set 4 should be cleaner than W10D1's set 4 (which couldn't pick up after rest).

**LOAD:** Kinesis 8 (hold — W10D1 confirmed 'doing these correctly is super hard' at 8). Spotlight is W12; sharp cues only this week.
- Slight forward hinge at hips — chest leads, not head
- **Soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED** throughout — these are NOT bicep curls
- 'Hug a tree' — squeeze from chest, not arms
- Stop a fistwidth apart at top, no crash
- Slow eccentric for full pec stretch

sets.name: Station 5: Cable Column Block (Lat Raise → Tri Ext → Straight Arm Pulldown)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column — find the working number, recent range 30-40lb (W7-W8 was 45-50; W10 form-first pullback). 3 sets per side, one arm at a time.
- Stand sideways to the cable column, working arm farther from the stack
- Soft elbow ~10°, fixed throughout
- Lead with elbow, hand passive — wrist follows
- Stop at shoulder height (higher = traps)
- Fight the cable on the way down, no snap-back
- **W10D1 protocol that worked:** massage out right pec between sets — kills the crackle. Don't wait for symptoms.
- **Skip the 'run line behind body' cue** — W10D1 confirmed it doesn't apply at this gym's cable geometry. Don't waste time on it.

**LOAD:** cable column kneeling — find your working number (the 'incline' in the name is misleading; this is **kneeling on the cable column facing away from the high pulley**, not a bench). W10D1: 'went great / felt great, hard at end' — keep this setup.
- Kneel facing AWAY from the stack, rope attachment overhead
- Torso slightly forward, head between arms — that's your 'incline'
- **Elbows pinned**, only forearms move
- Hands shoulder-width on rope (full long-head emphasis)
- Full stretch at the bottom, long head loads deep
- Stop just shy of lockout at top, slow eccentric

**LOAD:** cable column 100 (hold — W10D1 was 100 'oof really hard today'; W7=110, W8=120 but W10 form-first pullback held). 3 sets here.
- Stand a step back, hinge ~15° at hips
- **Slight elbow bend (5°), fixed throughout** — perfectly straight = elbow injury risk
- **Pull to thighs, NOT past** — past = shoulders roll forward, lats off
- Squeeze lats hard at bottom — 'oranges into back pockets'
- Slow controlled return to full overhead stretch

tips: **Spotlight: chest press machine.** Drill the seat-height + scap-pack + 3s eccentric at 165. No jump to 170 yet — last week's set 4 broke form, the protocol fix is the work this week.

**Equipment-consolidated layout** — finish ALL DB-rack work (push press → lat raise → incline DB) before walking to the chest press machine. Then Kinesis. Then cable column. One trip per zone, no backtracking.

**DB-rack zone covers stations 1+2** — same DB rack, same adjustable bench. Stand for push press + lat raise (3 rounds), then drop the bench to 30° and sit for incline DB (4 rounds). No machine change in between.
**Hold week for everything else** — push press 40, lateral raise 15, incline DB 45, Kinesis fly 8, straight-arm pulldown 100. The W10D1 actuals confirmed these are honest.
**Right pec / scap referral protocol** — slow tempo first, drop reps second, drop weight last. Massage out right pec between heavy sets (chest press, cable lat raise).
**Lateral raise scap reset is the default now** — don't wait for the crackle; roll shoulders + engage/disengage scapula between every set.
**Skip the 'run line behind body' cable lat raise cue** — doesn't apply at this gym's cable geometry (W10D1 confirmation).
Yuri band red warmup non-negotiable.
Rotation plan: W12 lateral raise + Kinesis cable fly · W13 cable lat raise + incline tri ext + straight-arm pulldown · W14 cycle back.
Rest 2 min DB-rack work, 2.5 min chest press (form takes more rest than ego does), 75s cables.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building, week 2 of the cycle. The progression IS the form: scap pack, slow eccentric, joint stacking, breath. Loads hold where W10D1 found edge-of-form. The chest press spotlight asks: can you make 165 cleaner with the right-pec referral protocol baked in?

name: Week 12 KB Conditioning — TGU Spotlight + 🆕 Figure-8 → Tactical Clean Flow
description: Thursday — lightest day, recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs. **Spotlight rotates to TGU left-side checkpoints** per the W11 plan. The W10D2 TGU breakthrough ('focus on the clean itself makes load disappear') and the W11D2 pattern ('2 forward + 2 reverse per side') stack into the W12 spotlight: drill the LEFT side specifically — every checkpoint deliberate, the to-standing transition (the historical weak link) gets the slowest, cleanest reps. **NEW (W12D3 request — dance/passing flow):** added the @kettleclubfam **Figure-8 → Tactical Clean** complex (hike → figure-8 exits into clean → tactical clean → hike → switch sides). Full workout source file lives at `workouts/kettlebell/kb-figure-8-tactical-clean-complex.json` for future use; W12D4 runs a LIGHT 2-set version (12kg only) as a skill block, not the full ascending pyramid. Bent press dropped this week — already 'light groove / rested' on Tuesday. **W11D4 was skipped** — re-acquaint at light loads.

dayOverview.theme: Thursday recovery + TGU left-side spotlight + 🆕 figure-8 → tactical clean flow as the dance/passing skill block. Two skill streams: vertical (TGU checkpoints) and horizontal (figure-8 pass → clean catch). Bent press rests.

spotlights.why: W2 first TGU at 15lb — left clunky. W3 25lb workable, left still unstable. W7 25lb x 3 each side controlled. W10D2 BREAKTHROUGH — 'focus on the clean itself' made load disappear, bumped to 40 mid-session. W11D2 added the '2 forward + 2 reverse' pattern. W12 Thursday spotlights the LEFT SIDE specifically — same load as the right, but every checkpoint deliberate. The to-standing transition is where the left has historically been hardest; this is the work.

🆕 W12D3 user request — wanted a 'traditional KB flow, dance-like, passing through legs.' The @kettleclubfam Figure-8 → Tactical Clean complex matches exactly: the pass-through IS the entry to the clean (hand catches behind, pull becomes the clean to rack). Today's slot is the LIGHT version — 2 sets at 12kg (26lb) only, focus on the pass-to-clean transition. Full ascending pyramid (12→16→20→24kg) lives in the standalone workout file for future use.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 bent press · W11 windmill mechanics · W12 TGU left-side + figure-8 → tactical clean (current) · W13 cycle back to bent press, figure-8 flow stays in rotation if W12D4 lands well.
watchpoints: **W11D4 skipped** — no recent Thursday data. Loads stay light to re-acquaint, not push.
**Minimal leg loading** — figure-8 holds a wide low stance for the pass-through, but no reps of squatting. Legs day is tomorrow.
**TGU left side gets the freshest reps.** Weak-side-first protocol — left first, right second. Every set.

**The setup IS the work** (W10D2 unlock) — punch the bell to a stable STRAIGHT-ARM overhead lockout before starting the get-up. The TGU is a straight-arm lift: the arm stays locked vertical the whole way up and down — no rack hold during the get-up. Wobble at the overhead lockout = wobble through the whole lift.
**Figure-8 → clean transition is the new skill** — pass between legs, hand catches behind, the catch becomes the clean to rack. Practice the pass-through first slowly before chaining the clean.
**Right shoulder watch on the clean** — same crackly pattern as push days. If anything talks during the catch, drop to just figure-8 passes (no clean) and finish the round.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Slow circles, both directions. Shoulder loosener.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Mini-band overhead OR blue rope band gripped in hands (W10D2 alt).
Elbows drive to ribs, scaps depress.
Press out to T — fires rear delts and mid-traps.
Pull apart at sternum, scaps retract, ribs down.
Sweep arms back. Don't overarch.
15s each side. Primes the lateral hinge — supports the TGU's hand-down + leg-sweep transitions.
Both arms in V/Y overhead. Hold and breathe — lower traps fire here, supports the TGU overhead lockout.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Sustained tension, no rest between positions.

sets.name: OTD Flow (Light Groove — Re-Acquaint After W11D4 Skip)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 20lb. Light and smooth.
- Bell starts on the floor between feet
- Hips drive the lift, not the arm
- Catch in rack with knees slightly bent (the W3 cue that made everything click)
Goblet/rack position. Sit tall, full depth, no bouncing.
Stride out wide, sit into the working leg, keep the other leg long. Mobility, not strength.
Light groove of W10's spotlight — leverage cue (body goes down, bell barely moves). Should feel automatic now.
Control on the way down — bell to floor with the same cleanness as up.

sets.notes: **This is a continuous FLOW, not 5 separate stations.** Move through the sequence as one chain — dead clean → front squat → lateral lunge → bent press → tactical clean — letting each move feed the next, then switch sides and flow back. About 2 reps' worth at each move, but don't stop to count — let the rhythm carry it. 20lb throughout, 2 rounds. Should feel effortless — re-acquaint after the W11D4 skip.

exercises.reps: 1 forward + 1 reverse, LEFT SIDE FIRST then right
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb (light — W11D4 was skipped, so re-acquaint at the home-base weight). 3 rounds.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the LEFT SIDE every rep:**
- **Left first, every round** — the weak side gets the freshest reps every time
- **The setup IS the work** (W10D2 unlock) — punch the bell to a stable STRAIGHT-ARM overhead lockout before starting the get-up. The TGU is a straight-arm lift: the arm stays locked vertical the whole way up and down — there is NO rack hold during the get-up itself (the clean-to-rack is only the brief entry before you press overhead). If the overhead lockout is wobbly, the rest of the lift inherits the wobble. Re-set until it's stable.
- **Eyes ON the bell** every checkpoint — eyes leaving the bell = bell drift
- **Pause at every checkpoint** — lying → elbow → hand → hip bridge → leg sweep → lunge → stand → reverse. No rushing. The descent (reverse) is half the lift.
- **The to-standing transition is the LEFT-SIDE WEAK LINK** (historical pattern from W2 onwards). Slow down here specifically. Plant the back knee directly under the hip; drive through the FRONT HEEL; bell stays vertical.
- **Hip bridge: glute squeeze, ribs down (no flare)** — rib flare on the bridge = lower back load = next week's QL flag.
- **Reverse with the same discipline as forward** — slow, deliberate, every checkpoint paused. The reverse is where most people rush.

**Pattern (W11D2 carry-forward):** **1 forward + 1 reverse per side per round, 3 rounds = 3 forward + 3 reverse per side total.** Cut from W11D2's 2+2 because the focus is left-side quality — fewer reps, cleaner reps.

**Reflection:** does the left-side to-standing transition feel different by round 3? If the deliberate-checkpoint approach is sticking, round 3 left should feel as solid as round 1 right. If it's still shakier on left, that's confirmation that the left needs more deliberate volume — drill it again next Thursday at 25 before pushing the load.

**Swap option:** if the to-standing transition still wobbles at 25 left, drop to a half get-up (lying → hand, then reverse) for left only. Get the lower checkpoints clean before adding the upper-half load.

**LOAD:** 18-20lb (light — keep cues clean). 2 rounds, 5 reps each side (cut from 3 to make room for the figure-8 flow block).
- Light groove of W11's spotlight — long-spine + back-leg hip flexor reach
- Bell locked overhead, eyes track bell
- Hip hinge AWAY from bell side
- Stretch in **back leg's hip flexor + hamstrings**, NOT lower back
- Slow descent, brief pause, controlled return

sets.name: 🆕 Skill Block: Figure-8 → Tactical Clean (LIGHT version of @kettleclubfam complex)

exercises.notes:
**LIGHT version of the @kettleclubfam pyramid — 12kg (26lb) only this week.** Full pyramid (12→16→20→24kg) lives in `workouts/kettlebell/kb-figure-8-tactical-clean-complex.json` for future weeks; today's slot is skill-only.

**Per round: 60s continuous flow at 12kg (26lb).** The figure-8-tactical-clean is ONE compound move (figure-8 pass exits into a clean to rack), repeated alternating sides.

**Pattern (alternates sides as the pass-through switches hands):**
- START hike position (bell between legs, hinged at hips)
- **Figure-8 → exit into Tactical Clean to rack** — pass between legs, hand catches BEHIND, the catch pulls the bell up into a clean to rack on one side
- Lower bell back through hike position
- Next figure-8 pass switches sides → clean to rack on the other side
- Continue alternating for the full 60s

**Cues:**
- **The figure-8 EXITS into the clean** — bell does NOT return to a hand mid-pass. Hand catches behind the body, the pull becomes the clean.
- **Tactical clean = controlled, threaded into rack.** No yank, no forearm slam. The 'tactical' is the deliberate wrist-rotation into the rack position.
- **Hike is home base** — every transition resets through hike. If you lose the bell, get back to hike before re-starting.
- **Right shoulder watch on the rack catch** — same crackly pattern as push days. If anything talks during the catch, drop to JUST figure-8 passes (no clean) and finish the round.
- **Pace yourself** — 60s feels long mid-flow. Find a sustainable rhythm.

**Reflection:** does the figure-8 → clean transition feel like one movement by round 2, or is the pass-through still a separate beat from the catch? If it's still beat-1-then-beat-2, that's the skill — practice slow before adding load. **DO NOT progress to heavier bell this week** — 12kg only. The ascending pyramid version is for full sessions, not pre-legs Thursday.

sets.notes:
**🆕 First time this complex is in the program.** Light skill version — 2 rounds at 12kg, 60s of continuous flow each. Total ~3 min work + rest. The full @kettleclubfam workout (ascending 12→24kg pyramid) is a 25-min session — that's a Tuesday slot eventually, not Thursday.

Source: @kettleclubfam Facebook reel — https://www.facebook.com/reel/2784846568518362

exercises.notes: 60s each side. Hip flexor release — TGU hip bridge + lateral lunge fries hip flexors. This counters.
60s. Adductors get tight from cossacks + lateral lunges.

tips: **THURSDAY = SKILL + MOBILITY, NOT WORKOUT.** ~25 min, light. Recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs.
**Spotlight: TGU left-side checkpoints.** Drill the to-standing transition specifically. Weak-side-first, every round.

**🆕 NEW skill block: figure-8 → tactical clean flow** (@kettleclubfam, light version). 12kg (26lb) only, 2 rounds of 60s continuous flow. The pass exits INTO the clean — that's the new skill. Hike is home base.

**The setup IS the work** (W10D2 unlock) — punch the bell to a stable straight-arm overhead lockout before any get-up movement. The arm stays locked vertical throughout — no rack hold during the get-up. Wobble at the lockout = wobble through the lift.
**Minimal leg loading** — figure-8 holds a wide low stance but no squat reps. Save legs for tomorrow.
**Heaviest bell: 25lb (TGU only).** Figure-8 at 12kg (26lb). Most of the session at 18-20lb.
**W11D4 was skipped** — re-acquaint at light loads. No load chase.
**Bent press rests this week** — got its spotlight W10 + light groove W11 + Tuesday already loaded overhead 4 ways. Returns W13.
**Right shoulder watch on the clean catch** — same crackly pattern as push days. If it talks during the catch, drop to figure-8 passes only and finish the round.
Cooldown mandatory — pigeon + butterfly. Hips and adductors get tight on KB days.

**The full @kettleclubfam ascending pyramid** (12→16→20→24kg, 4 sets descending time) lives in `workouts/kettlebell/kb-figure-8-tactical-clean-complex.json` — that's a full Tuesday session eventually, not Thursday.
Should feel effortless. If anything feels like work, it's too hard.
Rotation plan: W13 cycle back to bent press, figure-8 flow stays in rotation if W12D4 lands well.
formPhilosophy: Thursday = skill becomes default movement. Two skill streams today: vertical (TGU checkpoint discipline at left-side) and horizontal (figure-8 → tactical clean transition at light load). The cue is the whole point — quantity is not.

name: Week 12 KB Full Body — Asgooch Overhead Mobility Flow (Spotlight: Cossack + Windmill)
description: Rotation week — B-stance flow steps out, **kb-overhead-mobility-complex** (@Asgooch — 5 moves, single bell: Row → Snatch → OH Lunge → OH Squat → Push Press) steps in as the new Block 1 flow. Genuinely fresh — never integrated into the program before. The overhead-mobility theme pairs naturally with the W12 windmill spotlight (overhead bell + lateral hinge family). Bent press is rested this week (got its full spotlight W10 + light groove W11; the new flow already loads overhead pressing in 4 of 5 moves). C&P ramps per W11 (45 if grooved, NO 50 yet). TGU keeps the W11D2 '2 forward + 2 reverse per side' pattern.

dayOverview.theme: Fresh flow week. Block 1 becomes the @Asgooch overhead mobility complex (Row → Snatch → OH Lunge → OH Squat → Push Press). Spotlights stay on cossack + windmill mobility per the rotation plan — both pair with the overhead-mobility theme of the new flow. Bent press rested this week.

spotlights.why: W10D2 'slow-and-deep produces a hard stop at end-range, range is the work.' W11 held 25lb x 8 each side x 2 rounds. W12 drills the depth pattern — heels stay down, full external rotation on the working leg. The cue is **range, not load**.

Shares the overhead-stability pattern with the new Block 1 flow (snatch + OH lunge + OH squat all hold the bell overhead). W12 transfers windmill cues into a session that already loads overhead — drill the lateral hinge with a shoulder that's already grooved from the flow. **Hip flexor of the back leg is where the stretch lives, NOT the lower back.**

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 swing mechanics + bent press leverage · W12 windmill + cossack mobility (current) · W13 cycle back to C&P + TGU · W14 dead clean flow gets a turn if Asgooch flow is too overhead-heavy.
watchpoints: **NEW flow — @Asgooch overhead mobility complex.** Single bell, 5 rounds each side, all reps on one side before switching. **Start at 20-25lb** (Asgooch used 24kg ≈ 52lb; that's the W13+ target if W12 is grooved). Right shoulder still has the crackle pattern — drop weight or skip the OH squat depth if it talks.
**Overhead squat is the hardest move in the flow** — if mobility limits, REDUCE DEPTH instead of the load. Bell stays stacked over shoulder, no arching.
**Bent press rested this week** — got its full spotlight W10 + light groove W11. The new flow already loads overhead pressing in 4 of 5 moves; pulling bent press keeps shoulder volume reasonable.
**TGU pattern from W11D2:** 2 forward + 2 reverse per side. Keep this — it's a skill addition, not a volume bump.
**C&P ramp from W11:** start 45 (W11 'everything went great' suggests 45 was hit). NO 50 yet — that's W13+ territory.

**Right shoulder watch through ALL overhead work** — the new flow stacks 4 overhead moves consecutively. If the shoulder talks during the snatch or OH lunge, drop the weight or modify (front-rack lunge instead of OH lunge).

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Big slow circles — open up the shoulders before loading overhead 4 different ways.
**LOAD:** 20lb. Light, test the overhead position. If anything cracks/twinges, do extra band warmup before loading the flow.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Open hips. Sit in the bottom for a beat.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Mini-band OR blue rope band held in hands (W10D2 alt — both work).
Elbows drive to ribs, scaps depress.
Don't overarch the lower back to chase range.
15s each side. Primes the lateral hinge for windmill spotlight.
Both arms in V/Y, hold and breathe. Lower traps fire — directly preps the OH lunge / OH squat overhead lockout in the flow.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Sustained tension. The overhead-V hold is today's direct prime for the new flow's overhead loading.

sets.name: 🆕 Block 1: Asgooch Overhead Mobility Complex — LEFT SIDE

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 20-25lb (start at 25 if it's smooth, drop to 20 if the right shoulder warmup felt off). Left hand. Controlled pull, full extension. Hips hinged, back flat.
Left hand. **Hips drive the bell**, NOT the arm. Bell goes straight overhead — punch through at the top, no looping. **Smooth catch overhead** sets up the next 3 moves.

Left hand overhead. **Lock the elbow, bell over shoulder, torso TALL.** Step back into reverse lunge OR forward (your call). Drive through the front HEEL on the way up. **If the right shoulder talks, modify to front-rack lunge** — drop the bell to the rack on this move only and continue the flow.

Left hand overhead. **Hardest move in the flow.** Full depth IF mobility allows; **reduce depth before reducing load** if shoulder mobility limits. Bell stays stacked over shoulder, core braced, no lower-back arching. If overhead squat is unsafe today, **swap to front-squat-with-bell-in-rack** — keep moving through the flow.
Left hand. **Dip and drive** — legs do the work, arm just guides. After the OH squat hold, the press feels easier. Smooth descent back to rack. **End of one round on left.**

sets.notes:
**5 rounds LEFT side, all moves continuous, no rest within a round.** Bell stays in the working hand for the entire round. Rest 30-45s between rounds (within left side) if breathing dictates. **Weak side first** — left gets the freshest reps.

**Reflection:** does the OH squat on round 5 left feel like round 1, or has the depth shrunk? If shrunk, that's the actual working depth — drill there, don't cheat it.

sets.name: 🆕 Block 1 (continued): Asgooch Overhead Mobility Complex — RIGHT SIDE

exercises.notes: Right hand. Same flow — controlled pull, full extension.
Right hand. Hips drive, punch through at top.
Right hand overhead. **W10D1 right shoulder watchpoint** — this is where the crackle most likely shows up. Modify to front-rack lunge if needed.
Right hand overhead. Same depth/load discipline as left side. Reduce depth before load.
Right hand. Finish strong. **End of full complex.**

sets.notes:
**5 rounds RIGHT side after left is done.** Same rest pattern. Total flow volume: 50 reps each side (5 moves x 5 reps x 2 sides x 5 rounds = 250 reps total).

**Reflection:** does the right side overhead lunge feel different from left, given the W10D1 right-shoulder pattern? If right is sketchy and left is solid, that's the asymmetry to track — log both sides explicitly today.

sets.name: 🔆 Block 2: Cossack + Windmill (Mobility Spotlights — bent press rested this week)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb goblet (HOLD — W11 + W10D2 both confirmed 25 is the sweet spot, range is the work).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the depth every rep:**
- Sit DEEP, heels stay down
- Working leg in full external rotation
- Slow descent, hard stop at end-range — that's the work, NOT failure
- Heel popping up = drop to 20 or shorten stance
- Mobility, not strength

**Reflection:** at rep 8, is the depth where it was at rep 1? If shrunk, that's the actual working range.

**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD — W10D2 + W11 'felt good' at 25).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the lateral hinge pattern with an already-grooved overhead shoulder:**
- Bell locked overhead, eye tracks bell throughout
- **Hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — push the bell-side hip out sideways
- Reach free hand toward inside of front foot
- **Stretch lives in the hip flexor of the back leg, NOT the lower back.** If lower back is loading, hinge isn't deep enough — push the hip further sideways.
- Long spine throughout — no compression

**Reflection:** the new Block 1 flow already loaded the overhead position 4 different ways — does the windmill feel more grooved as a result, or is the shoulder fatigued? If fatigued, drop windmill to 18-20lb and keep the cue clean.

sets.name: Block 3: TGU (W11D2 Pattern: 2 Forward + 2 Reverse per Side)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 30 (light — the new Block 1 flow already loaded the shoulder hard; don't push to 40 today). Pick by how the clean grooves in round 1.

**Pattern (W11D2 carry-forward):**
- 2 reps standard direction (lying → standing)
- THEN 2 reps reverse direction (standing → lying)
- Per side. **Left side first** (weak side first protocol).

- Pause at every checkpoint: lying → elbow → hand → hip bridge → leg sweep → lunge → stand → reverse
- **Eyes ON the bell** every checkpoint
- **'Focus on the clean itself'** (W10D2 unlock) — the setup IS the work.

sets.name: Block 4: C&P (Ramp 45 — W11 Hit the 40→45 Projection)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** **45 start** (W11 prescription was 40 start with 45 if grooved; W11D2 'everything went great'). NO 50 yet — W13+ territory.

- **Catch the clean with knees slightly bent** (W3 cue, durable)
- **Hips drive the clean**, NOT the arm
- Press: ribs DOWN (W8 trigger), bicep finishes by the ear, full lockout
- Lower with control — no slam

**Symmetry check:** if left at 45 feels rougher than right at 40, drop left to 40. Quality wins.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Light row, 2s pull / 2s lower. Skip if shoulders are cooked from the new flow.
60s each side. Hip flexor release.
60s. Adductors get tight on KB days from cossacks + lateral lunges (cossack carries forward, lateral lunge is no longer in Block 1 since the new flow doesn't use it).

tips: **Block 1 swap:** dead-clean-press flow → @Asgooch overhead mobility complex (5 moves, single bell, 5 rounds each side). Genuinely fresh — never integrated before.
**The new flow stacks 4 overhead moves** (snatch, OH lunge, OH squat, push press) — start LIGHT (20-25lb), Asgooch's 24kg/52lb is W13+ territory.
**Modification options if right shoulder talks:** OH lunge → front-rack lunge; OH squat → front squat with bell in rack. Keep the flow moving, don't grind.
**Overhead squat is the hardest move** — reduce DEPTH before reducing load if mobility limits.
**Spotlights stay: cossack + windmill mobility.** Both at 25lb. Range is the work, not load.
**Bent press rested this week** — got full spotlight W10 + light groove W11; the new flow already loads overhead pressing 4 different ways.
**TGU pattern from W11D2:** 2 forward + 2 reverse per side at 30lb (light — flow loaded the shoulder already).
**C&P ramp:** start 45 (W11 hit the 40→45 projection). NO 50 yet.
**Weak side first** on the new flow AND on TGU — left gets freshest reps every round.
Cooldown mandatory — pigeon + butterfly. Hips and adductors get tight.
formPhilosophy: Fresh flow week with the @Asgooch overhead mobility complex. The flow's overhead-mobility theme pairs with the windmill spotlight (overhead bell + lateral hinge). The cue is still the progression — start light, find the working load, modify before grinding.

name: Week 12 Legs — Form-First (Spotlight: Seated Leg Curl + Calf Raise)
description: Third week of the form-first cycle. Spotlights rotate to **seated leg curl + calf raise**. **W11D5 takeaways baked in:** squat jumped to 70/side and 'felt pretty easy and good for all sets — QL finally caught up' (W12 pushes to 75); leg press went slightly past medium ROM cleanly at 3 plates (W12 keeps 3 plates, drills the depth push); seated leg curl ramped 50 → 65 → 90 → 90 with corrected ankle placement (90 = new working weight at the corrected lever); calf raise double-leg slow was 'slightly easy' (W12 drills the deep heel-drop stretch + slow eccentric at the same load); incline RDL 25lb dead slow held; lunges weren't logged in W11 (optional finisher this week).

dayOverview.theme: Seated leg curl + calf raise in the spotlight. Squat pushes to 75 after W11D5 cleared the QL signal. Leg press drills depth at 3 plates (W11D5 went past medium ROM cleanly). Incline RDL holds 25 dead slow. Lunges optional — earn the way back to 30 over multiple clean weeks at 25.

spotlights.why: W11D5 ramped 50 → 65 → 90 with corrected ankle placement (pad UNDER the ankles, not on the calf — W10D5 form discovery). The W7-W10 numbers (90-110) were on the wrong lever. **90 at the corrected lever is the new baseline** — W12 drills the ankle placement + 5/m/0/1 settings + slow tempo at 90. The lift is genuinely working the hamstrings now.

W11D5 confirmed double-leg slow was 'slightly easy' at 3 plates — Option A worked, single-leg + slow eccentric was the W10D5 trap. W12 holds the load and drills the deep heel-drop stretch + slow eccentric: balls of the feet on the bottom platform edge, heels dropping off for the full stretch. The slow eccentric is the stimulus, not more weight.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 squat + incline RDL · W11 leg press · W12 seated leg curl + calf raise (current) · W13 lunge (when QL fully cleared — W11D5 cleared one week, this is the second clean week) · W14 cycle back to squat + incline RDL.
watchpoints: **Squat pushes to 75/side** — W11D5 70/side 'felt pretty easy' and user said QL caught up. 75 is the next step, well within the W7-W8 baseline. Permission to drop back to 70 if anything's off in the warmup.

**Leg press depth push at 3 plates first, NOT load.** W11D5 went slightly past medium ROM cleanly. Add depth before adding plates. If 3 plates full ROM is clean for all sets, W13 can test 4 plates medium ROM.
**Seated leg curl 90 at corrected ankle placement** — pad UNDER ANKLES, machine settings 5/m/0/1. W7-W10 numbers don't translate; this is a new baseline.

**Calf raise: balls of the feet on the bottom platform edge, heels hanging off** — the platform edge gives the heel drop, no plate needed. Bottom-half ROM, deep stretch, slow eccentric. Stay double-leg. The slow eccentric is the stimulus.
**QL has been quiet for W11D5 + W11 KB** — that's two clean weeks. Lunges return to spotlight in W13 IF this trend holds. Optional finisher today; skip if anything's off.

exercises.notes: Full depth, slow. Find where the hips are tight and breathe into it. Depth-check before loading.
Big slow circles. Wake up the hip stabilizers.
Spine mobility — extra important post-QL-spasm history.
Bodyweight. Use this as a QL check — if anything talks here, modify the day.
Set 1: 45/side x8. Set 2: 65/side x8. Same depth as working sets.

**LOAD:** **75/side** (push from W11D5's 70 — 'felt pretty easy and good for all sets, QL finally caught up'). 4 sets at 75. **Permission to drop back to 70 if the warmup feels off** — but the W11D5 signal was clear, this is the right next step.

**Sharp cues only — spotlight was W10:**
- Bar high on upper traps (NOT on neck), upper-back shelf squeezed
- **Brace like prepping for a punch** — deep belly breath at top, HOLD through descent, exhale on drive up
- **Hips back AND down**, not just down
- 'Spread the floor' — screw feet into the ground
- Knees track over toes (line, not position)
- Same depth as W11 — sit in the bottom
- Drive through the WHOLE foot — heel + ball + outer edge

**Watchpoint — the 'weightless middle':** mid-range is biomechanically the easiest zone. Bottom + top are where the brace + drive matter. Don't be surprised by it again — it's normal.

**Reflection:** does the bottom position feel as solid at 75 as it did at 70 last week? If yes, the brace pattern is generalizing. If 75 is shakier, hold 75 for another week before pushing to 80.

**LOAD:** 3 plates/side (HOLD — W11D5 went slightly past medium ROM cleanly, no QL flag). **Push depth, NOT load.** Spotlight was W11; sharp cues only.

- **Foot position: shoulder-width, mid-foot on platform, slight toe-out** — set BEFORE loading
- **Press through the WHOLE foot** — heels grounded, push platform with heels and ball together
- **W12 progression: go DEEPER at 3 plates** — chase full ROM, not more weight
- Slow descent (3s), no bounce at the bottom
- Don't lock out hard at the top — leave a hair of bend
- **Watch:** if hips lift off the pad at depth = too deep, back off. The hip lift IS the QL signal.
- **Breath:** in on descent, out on drive

**Test for W13:** if 3 plates full ROM is clean across all 4 sets this week, W13 can try 4 plates at medium ROM. Don't jump there yet — earn it with one more clean week first.

sets.name: 🔆 Calf Raise (Spotlight — Deep Heel-Drop Stretch + Slow Eccentric)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 3 plates/side, **double-leg, on the incline leg press machine.** 3 sets.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the stretch every rep:**
- **Setup:** balls of both feet on the BOTTOM EDGE of the platform, heels hanging off the edge. No plate under the foot — the platform edge already gives the heel drop. This is a bottom-half-ROM movement: the stretch comes from the heels dropping below the platform edge.
- **Both feet on the platform** (W11D5 confirmed double-leg slow was sustainable; single-leg + slow was the W10D5 trap)
- **Full stretch at the bottom** — let the heels drop as far below the platform edge as they'll go. **The deep stretch + slow eccentric is the stimulus, NOT more weight.**
- **Drive to FULL plantarflexion at the top** — calves love full range, half reps don't grow them
- **3-4s eccentric** — slow descent, count it
- **1s squeeze at peak**
- **No bouncing** at the bottom or top
- **Breath:** continuous, don't hold

**Reflection:** the slow eccentric is the whole lift here — if the descent count isn't hard, slow it down further before adding load.

**Test for W13:** if double-leg slow at 3 plates is dialed for all 3 sets, W13 can test single-leg at the same load (per the W10D5 lesson — change one variable at a time).

sets.name: 🔆 Seated Leg Curl (Spotlight — 90 at Corrected Lever)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** **90** (W11D5 working weight at corrected ankle placement). 3 sets.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the setup every rep:**
- **Ankles UNDER the pad** — not the mid-calf, not the upper calf. **The ankle bone is the contact point.** The whole W7-W10 history of 100-110 was on the wrong lever (pad too high on the calf); 90 here is GENUINELY working the hamstrings.
- **Knees aligned with the machine pivot** — adjust the seat back if needed. Misalignment compounds across reps.
- **Lap pad locked DOWN firmly**, not loose. Loose pad = torso lift = lower back work, not hamstring.
- **Settings: 5/m/0/1** (W8 corrected baseline — verify before loading)
- **Curl heels toward glutes — full ROM, full squeeze.** Partial ROM is the most common fault here.
- **2s eccentric, 1s squeeze at peak**
- **Don't let the torso lift forward** — that's lower back, not hamstring. Stays anchored to the seat.
- **No bouncing out of the bottom**
- **Breath:** out on curl, in on return

**Reflection — this is the spotlight question:** does 90 with strict ankle placement feel like genuine hamstring work today, or is the form drifting back toward the wrong lever? **The ankle position is the entire lift right now.** If you catch the pad creeping up the calf mid-rep, stop and reset.

**For W13+:** when 90 with the strict setup feels like W11's 65 felt at the corrected lever, push to 100 — but only at the corrected lever. The numbers from W7-W10 stay invalid forever; this is a new scale.

**LOAD:** 25lb plate (HOLD — W11D5 'felt good otherwise did all sets, super slow, rested more between, squeezed glutes'). Dead slow with 2s pause at the bottom. Spotlight was W10; sharp cues only.
- Pad just below the hip crease (NOT upper thigh, NOT stomach)
- **Hip hinge — Romanian deadlift on the 45° bench, NOT a lumbar extension**
- Lower back NEUTRAL throughout
- At the bottom: stretch in **hamstrings and glutes**, NOT lower back
- Drive up by squeezing glutes and hamstrings
- At the top: stop at neutral — driving past = lumbar load
- Tempo: 3s eccentric, 2s pause, 1s drive

**W11D5 said extra rest between sets felt right** — keep that. Quality > tempo of session pacing here.

**LOAD:** 25lb DBs (HOLD — W10D5 + W11 KB clean of QL signal; W11D5 didn't log lunges). **Optional this week** — skip if time-short or anything feels off in the warmup. Spotlight is W13.

**Sharp cues:**
- **Long stride forward** — short stride = knee crashes forward
- **Vertical shin on the front leg** — knee tracks toes
- Back knee toward floor — 1-2" off, don't slam
- Drive up through the front HEEL
- Torso vertical

**ABORT IF:** any twinge in the lower back / QL area. The W10D5 + W11 trend has been QL-clear; if W12 stays clear, W13 spotlights lunges and a careful 25 → 30 ramp becomes the work.

tips: **Spotlights: seated leg curl + calf raise.** Drill the corrected ankle placement (90) and the plate-under-foot stretch progression.
**Squat pushes to 75/side** after W11D5's 70 felt easy and 'QL caught up.' Permission to drop back to 70 if warmup is off.
**Leg press: depth push at 3 plates, NOT load.** W11D5 went past medium ROM cleanly — chase full ROM. W13 tests 4 plates at medium ROM if this week's full ROM is clean.
**Seated leg curl 90** at corrected ankle placement (pad UNDER ankles, settings 5/m/0/1). W7-W10 numbers don't translate.
**Calf raise: balls of the feet on the bottom platform edge**, heels hanging off for the deep stretch — no plate needed. Double-leg slow stays; the slow eccentric is the stimulus.
**Incline RDL holds 25lb dead slow.** Extra rest between sets per W11D5.
**Lunges optional** — earn the way back to 30 over multiple clean weeks at 25. W12 is the second clean week; if it stays clean, W13 spotlights them.
**QL watchpoint stays.** Two clean weeks doesn't mean the QL is forgotten — abort any lift that twinges.
Rotation plan: W13 lunge spotlight (when QL fully cleared) · W14 cycle back to squat + incline RDL.
Rest 3 min squats, 2 min leg press, 90s isolations.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building, week 3 of the cycle. The spotlights drill the setup details that make the lift work — ankle placement, full ROM stretch, foot mechanics. The squat push to 75 is earned (not forced) by W11D5's clear signal. Building decades, not weeks.

name: W12 Morning Flow — Bodyweight Spine + Hip (All Days, Variety Week)
description: 15 min bodyweight floor flow — **universal W12 morning flow, used every training day** (Mon push / Tue KB / Wed pull / Thu KB / Fri legs). Same shape as W11 with rotated moves for variety. Three-part: spine primer → bodyweight work → deep stretch arc. Day-specific therapeutic add-on at the end (push = pec-minor protocol; pull = dead hang + scap pull; legs = 90s pigeon + supine twist; KB days skip — banded prep covers it).

sets.name: Primer — Wake the Spine (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Slow, full ROM each direction. Spine wave wakes the segment-by-segment articulation.
30s each side. Thoracic rotation + shoulder opener — same as W11, the rotation should reach further this week.
Knees under hips, chest sinks toward the floor with arms extended forward. **Lat + shoulder opener** — primes the overhead pattern that the morning will need.

sets.notes: Slightly different opener arc than W11 — drops the cobra pulse, adds puppy pose for the lat/shoulder stretch.

sets.name: Work Block — Bodyweight Activation (~5 min)

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Lunge + thoracic rotation — covers hip flexor + t-spine in one shape. **Replaces W11's inchworm-walkout** — same goal (full-body warm), different shape.
45s alternating. Plank with single-arm shoulder taps — anti-rotation under the hand-lift. **Replaces W11's plank-bird-dog** — different anti-rotation pattern.
