45s alternating. From down-dog, draw one knee toward the forehead, hold a beat, switch. **Same as W11** — too good to drop, the core+hip+shoulder integration earns its place.
45s slow. Same as W11 — durable hip opener under plank load.
30s each side. Same as W11 — windmill on the floor, the lateral hinge cue translation.

sets.notes: World's greatest stretch + plank shoulder tap rotate in. Anti-rotation gets a different shape.

sets.name: Yoga Flow — Deep Stretch Arc (~7 min)

exercises.notes: 30s each side. **Stays every week** — signature spine move.
30s each direction. Quadruped hip abduction — slow, full ROM. **Replaces W11's hip-circle** — more abductor focus, complements the lateral hinge work in the loaded sessions.
30s each side. Hip flexor + adductor + back-leg quad. Drop to elbows if accessible. **Stays** — durable hip opener.
Bodyweight, slow. **Stays every week** — mirrors the Tuesday loaded cossack.
30s each side (the universal flow's version — legs day add-on does 90s for the QL protocol). **Replaces W11's mountain-to-pigeon** — back to the static hold for deeper release.
60s slow. Hip + lower back release. **Replaces W11's frog-pose-pulses** — gentler, easier to bail if not opening up that day.

sets.notes: Continuous arc — flow between shapes, breathe deeply.

sets.name: 📍 Day-Specific Add-On — Pick by Today's Training (~3-5 min)

exercises.notes: **📍 PUSH DAYS — KEY W9 PROTOCOL MOVE.** 30s each at LOW / MID / HIGH elbow positions. Arm bent 90°, forearm on door frame, step through and rotate torso AWAY. Desk work continuously re-tightens pec minor — daily.
**📍 PUSH DAYS** — Active side of the pec-minor fix. Lower trap + rotator cuff.
**📍 PUSH DAYS** — Serratus activation, helps unstick a tight pec minor.
**📍 PUSH DAYS (optional)** — Slow rotator cuff work. Add if shoulder feels off.
**📍 PUSH DAYS (optional)** — Pins shoulder blades in corrected position.
**📍 PULL DAYS** — 25s. Decompress shoulders, warm grip.
**📍 PULL DAYS** — Scapulae retracting only, no elbow bend. Crisp scap engagement before loaded pulls.
**📍 LEGS DAYS — W8 QL-SPASM PROTOCOL.** 90s each side (longer than the universal flow's 30s). The long hold is what lets QL/psoas release. **Non-negotiable on legs day.**
**📍 LEGS DAYS** — 60s each side. Gentle twist for the QL. Lacrosse-ball QL roll above iliac crest beforehand if available.
**📍 LEGS DAYS (optional)** — Deep hip flexor stretch.
**📍 LEGS DAYS (optional)** — Most direct quad + hip flexor stretch.

sets.notes: **Pick today's day-specific protocol — push / pull / legs.** ~3-5 min total. KB days (Tue/Thu) skip this block — the in-workout banded prep covers the equivalent.

tips: **Same shape as W11, rotated moves.** Variety keeps the flow interesting — same intent, different shapes.
**15 min main flow + 3-5 min day-specific add-on** — universal across W12 (Mon push / Tue KB / Wed pull / Thu KB / Fri legs).
**Scorpion + cossack BW + lizard pose stay every week** — durable spine + hip + adductor anchors.
Low lunge thoracic rotation + plank shoulder taps + fire hydrant + happy baby rotate in this week. Plank shoulder taps is a different anti-rotation pattern than W11's bird-dog.
**Day-specific add-on at the end** — push days do the pec-minor protocol, pull days do dead hang + scap pull, legs days do 90s pigeon + supine twist. KB days skip the add-on.
If short on time: scorpion + lizard + cossack BW + pigeon, plus today's day-specific add-on.
formPhilosophy: Same as W10/W11. Variety = different shapes hitting the same patterns. Scorpion-family spine work + hip openers + adductor stretches. Designed for home, structured around the in-workout banded prep's logic.

name: Week 12 Pull — Form-First (Spotlight: Face Pull + Reverse Fly)
description: Third week of the form-first cycle. Spotlights rotate to **face pull + reverse fly** — both posterior-shoulder lifts that pair naturally with the right-shoulder rehab work on push days. **NEW (W12D1 discussion):** behind-back DB shrugs added as a final block — see `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md` for the full reasoning. The grip → shoulder pain pattern responds to controlled load on the trap chain; W12D1 post-session shrugs already relaxed the right shoulder. **W11D3 takeaways baked in:** pull-ups stepped up to 7/7/7/6 (clean) on adjustable handles → flat bar wide → flat bar close → wide slow; chest-supported row dropped to 30 (35 N/A); DB reverse fly dropped to 20 (22.5 N/A — none of the weights around); lat pulldown forced to dual pulley 70/side ≈ 140 (regular lat pulldown N/A); high cable curl 150 cable column 'felt good, could go up'; cable crunch 120 'felt a bit easy.' The forced equipment subs are noted; if the prescribed setups are free this week, use them. Equipment-consolidated layout holds.

dayOverview.theme: Face pull + reverse fly in the spotlight — both posterior-shoulder lifts that pair with the right-shoulder rehab work. Pull-ups step up to 6-8 reps after W11's clean 7/7/7/6. High cable curl + cable crunch get a small push (W11 said both had headroom).

spotlights.why: W11D3 hit 42.5 x 20 with 'last 5 of last 2 sets hard but finished with ok form' — that's the right state. The cue is the progression now: drill elbows-high + external rotation + 1s peak hold. The lift exists for the rotation, not the volume.

W11D3 was forced to 20lb (22.5 N/A) — 'lighter but somehow never easier, could complete all sets slowly though, still a struggle at end.' Lighter load + clean form = the form-first thesis. W12 drills the seated bent-over + lead-with-elbow at 20-22.5 (whatever's available).

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 pull-up + DB row · W11 chest-supported row + lat pulldown · W12 face pull + reverse fly (current) · W13 preacher curl + high cable curl + cable crunch · W14 cycle back to pull-up + DB row.
watchpoints: **Forced equipment subs from W11D3:** chest-supported row 30 (35 N/A), DB reverse fly 20 (22.5 N/A), lat pulldown 70/side dual pulley (regular machine N/A). If the prescribed setup is available this week, use it; if not, the W11 numbers stand. **These were availability subs, not preferences.**
**Left bicep weaker than right** (W10D3 + W11D3 incline curl: right 10 strict, left 5 + assist). Hit left first, freshest reps to the weak side. Stop at strict failure — don't assist past asymmetry.
**~15lb recent weight gain** is making pull-ups harder than W7's 32. W11D3 hit 7/7/7/6 clean — that's the new W12 floor, push to 6-8 with mixed grips.
**High cable curl + cable crunch had headroom** — W11D3 said 150 'could go up,' 120 'felt a bit easy.' Small push this week, NOT a jump.

**NEW: behind-back DB shrugs as the final block** — W12D1 discussion outcome. The grip → right-shoulder pain pattern responds to controlled load on the trap chain. Light to moderate weight, 2-3s top hold, slow eccentric, 'down + back, not up.' See `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`. Stop and report if shrugs WORSEN the grip-shoulder coupling — that flips the working hypothesis from functional to structural and means PT eval.

exercises.notes: Scap activation. Squeeze shoulder blades together, soft elbow.
Spine warm — slow, full ROM each direction.
Active hang, not collapsed — engage lats, pull shoulders DOWN away from ears even at full extension.

**LOAD:** Bodyweight. **Reps adjusted UP from W11's 5-7** — W11D3 hit 7/7/7/6 clean, the floor moved up. 6-8 strict; stop the set when form breaks, never kip.

**Mix grips across sets (W11D3 sequence worked):**
- Set 1: adjustable rack handles (the 'climber-style' handles that don't actually rotate — adjustable, not climber)
- Set 2: flat bar, **wide** grip
- Set 3: flat bar, **close** grip
- Set 4: wide slow — go for clean reps, stop when the rep stops being clean

**Form cues:**
- Active hang start (not collapsed)
- Pull elbows DOWN to ribs, NOT chin to bar
- Chest leads to bar at the top
- Eyes ahead, head neutral — no chin tucking
- Slow eccentric, ~2-3s, return to active hang

**W11D3 watch:** front of right shoulder lit up on the wide grip — stretches help (used to be rotator/scalp, all connected). If it talks this week, drop the wide grip set and replace with another close or neutral set.

sets.name: 🔆 Station 2: DB Rack / Bench Block (Reverse Fly Spotlight + Chest-Supported Row + DB Row + Incline Curl)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** **35lb if available** (prescribed default), 30lb if 35s aren't around (W11D3 forced sub — 'hard doing these slow up to 12 x 3 sets, really tight at end'). 3 sets.
- **Chest stays GLUED to the bench** — if it lifts off, the weight is too heavy
- Pull elbow toward HIP, not toward armpit (toward armpit = rear delt; toward hip = lat)
- Both elbows pull together — asymmetric = slow down
- 2s eccentric, 1s squeeze
- No DB slam at the bottom

**LOAD:** Pyramid 60x10, 70x10, 80x8 (HOLD — W11D3 hit the pyramid; 80 'really hard, kind of broke slow form, more momentum naturally'). Spotlight was W10; sharp cues only.
- Chest-supported on incline bench
- Pull elbow toward HIP, not armpit
- Don't rotate the torso
- Slow 2s descent, 1s squeeze

**W11D3 form watch at 80:** form broke from naturally taking momentum. If 80 still goes ugly this week, hold 75 instead — 80 with clean form > 80 with momentum.

**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD — W7+ proven swap from preacher; preacher curl bench is always taken at Arrilaga). 3 sets.
- Sit on incline bench (~45-60°), back against the bench, DBs hanging at sides
- **Full stretch at the bottom — pause 2s** (W11D3 user cue)
- Curl smoothly to peak squeeze
- 2-3s eccentric — fight the long-head stretch

**Left first today** — W10D3 + W11D3 both showed left bicep weaker (right got 10 strict, left got 5-7 + assist). Freshest reps to the weak side. Stop the set at strict failure — don't assist past asymmetry.

**Swap:** preacher curl at 12.5/side if it's actually free — usually not.

**LOAD:** **22.5lb if available**, 20lb if not (W11D3 forced sub — 'none of the fucking weights are around'). 3 sets.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Seated bent-over** — chest toward thighs (W3 default form). Rounding the upper back instead of hinging at the hips kills the lift.
- **DBs hanging fully extended at start, soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED throughout** — locked elbow recruits lats, not rear delts.
- **Lead with the elbow, hand passive** — same pattern as lateral raise. Wrist follows.
- **Wings out and slightly UP to shoulder height** — sweep, don't lift. The 'slightly up' is the rear delt; pure horizontal is more middle trap.
- **Slow controlled both directions** — 2s eccentric, 1s squeeze at peak. No swing, no momentum.
- **Breath:** out on lift, in on descent.

**Reflection:** does 20lb feel different at rep 12 of set 3 with the strict bent-over form than W11D3's 'lighter but somehow never easier'? If the lift gets harder as the form gets cleaner, that's the spotlight working — load goes up later, after the cue is automatic.

**Setup option:** reverse-incline (chest-down on incline bench) is even better when the bench is free — removes the awkward seated hunch.

sets.notes: **One trip to the DB rack covers all 4 lifts.** Either circuit (A→B→C→D, rest, repeat) or straight sets (3 of A, then 3 of B). Bench at incline ~30° for the rows + incline curl; sit upright (or use reverse-incline if bench is free) for the reverse fly.

exercises.equipment: regular lat pulldown (default, 140) — dual pulley fallback (70/side ≈ 140 per W11D3 calibration)
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** regular lat pulldown 140 (HOLD — W10D3 working number). **Forced sub from W11D3:** dual pulley 70/side ≈ 140 (user calibration: 'it's like half the weight, so 70 is 140 more or less'). 3 sets.

**Spotlight was W11; sharp cues only:**
- **Wide grip near the bend** — more lat isolation, less bicep
- **Slight backward lean ~15°, NO further** — more = becomes a row
- **Pull bar to UPPER CHEST — NEVER behind the neck**
- 'Lats squeezing oranges into back pockets' at the bottom
- 1s pause at contraction, 2s controlled return to full overhead stretch
- Knees pinned firmly under pad

**Set count cut from 4 to 3** — W11D3 ran 4 sets at 140 and high cable curl was the lift that 'felt pretty good could go up.' Trim a set here, redirect intensity to the curl + crunch finisher.

exercises.equipment: cable column (mid/upper-chest height + rope)
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column **42.5** (HOLD — W11D3 'last 5 of last 2 sets hard but finished with ok form'). The machine doesn't have 45; 42.5 is the right working number. 3 sets.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Pull rope to face level** — not to chest (becomes a row, loses rear delt focus)
- **ELBOWS HIGH (above shoulders)** — this is the make-or-break cue. If elbows drop below shoulders, the rear delt offloads and the lift becomes a chest-supported pull.
- **External rotate at the top — knuckles point UP, like a double-biceps pose.** This is why the lift exists — for the rotation, not the volume. No external rotation = no shoulder-health benefit, just back work.
- **1s peak hold at the contracted position** — count it. The hold is where the rear delt + external rotators get loaded longest.
- **Slow controlled return, 2s** — let the cable pull the arms back. Speed up the return = lose the stretch stimulus.
- **Breath:** out on pull, in on return.

**Reflection:** at set 3, are the elbows still ABOVE the shoulders, or have they crept down to shoulder height? If they've dropped, the rear delts have tapped out — stop the set. The lift's job is shoulder rotation health; doing it sloppy doesn't add anything.

**Setup: pick the cable column you'll use for the rest of the workout** — high cable curl + cable crunch live at the same column. Slide the rope from the chest-height attachment to the high attachment after this exercise.

sets.name: Station 5: Cable Column — Finisher (High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch)

exercises.equipment: cable column (high attachment) — same column as face pull
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column **160** (small push — W11D3 hit 150 each side x 2, 'felt pretty good, could go up'). One arm at a time. 2 sets each side.
- **Single D-handle, one arm at a time** (the W11D3 setup that 'felt nice')
- Stand sideways, arm out at shoulder level
- **Upper arm parallel to floor, FIXED** — if elbow drops, becomes a regular curl
- Curl hand to ear, peak squeeze
- Slow eccentric, no bounce at start

**Push to 170 only if 160 feels like W11's 150** — same 'felt good, could go up' state. Otherwise hold 160.

exercises.equipment: cable column (high attachment + rope)
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column **130** (small push — W11D3 hit 120 'felt a bit easy'). 2 sets.
- **High attachment + rope** (swap from D-handle if needed — same column)
- Kneel under the high cable
- Rope behind head, **HIPS LOCKED** (most important cue)
- **Crunch from the abs only** — spine flexes, hips don't move. Hips moving = hip flexor work, not abs.
- Chest toward thighs, hold the crunch 1 second
- Slow eccentric

**If 130 feels like W11's 120 ('a bit easy'), push to 140 next week.**

sets.name: Station 6: Shoulder Reset — Behind-Back DB Shrugs (NEW, W12D1 discussion outcome)

exercises.equipment: DB rack
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** Start **30-40lb DBs** — light to moderate, this is an introduction. Build the cue first, the load comes later. 3 sets.

**Why this lift, this week** (full reasoning in `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`):
- W12D1 surfaced a grip-squeeze → shoulder pain pattern + 'super tight' shoulders + tight neck + tight right pec, all as a unit.
- Loading helps (cable fly stretch, shrugs both relaxed the shoulder). That's the signal that the pattern is **functional**, not structural — upper traps locked tonic, lower/mid trap underused.
- Behind-the-back variant **biases lower trap + mid trap** vs. front shrugs which are upper-trap dominant. Lower trap is the underused side of the pattern.

**Cues (drill these every rep):**
- **DBs held BEHIND the body**, palms facing back, arms straight
- **Ribs DOWN** before the first rep — no rib flare under the shrug
- **Pull the shoulder blades into your back pockets at the top, NOT up to your ears.** Down + back, not up. The cue for this lift is opposite the cue for a standard front shrug.
- **2-3s hold at the top** — count it. The hold is where the lower trap loads.
- **Slow eccentric**, controlled all the way down. No bounce.
- Don't roll the shoulders — straight up + back, straight down.

**What to watch (REPORT BACK):**
- Does the grip → right-shoulder coupling **reduce** after these? (Hypothesis: yes — that's why we're adding them.)
- If the coupling **worsens** under shrugs → the structural side (cervical, TOS) is the real driver. Stop the protocol and book a PT eval.
- Right pec / neck tightness should also respond. If it doesn't shift over 2-3 sessions, escalate to PT.

**Rotation:** alternates with `trap-3-raise` across pull days going forward — W13D3 swaps to trap-3 raises (more lower-trap isolation, lighter load). Don't do both same day.

sets.notes: Quick block at the DB rack on the way out. 3 sets, light load, focus is the cue. Total ~4 min including rest.

sets.name: Cool-Down — 5 Min Fascia/Recovery Block (NEW from W12D1 discussion)

exercises.notes: 30s each arm at MID elbow position. Arm bent 90°, forearm on door frame, step through + rotate torso AWAY. Pec minor lengthen — the antagonist to the upper-trap chain that's been tonically loaded all session.
Passive hang from the pull-up bar (or any sturdy overhead). **Don't engage** — let the shoulders unpack and the spine decompress. The opposite of every loaded lift today.
30s each side on the mat. Lie back, draw one knee across the body, opposite arm extended. Breathe into the rib cage on the rotated side.
45s each side. Front leg + back leg at 90°. Sink hips toward the floor. Hip-rotator decompression — pulls the QL/glute pattern out of the squat/deadlift bracing.
60s slow breathing. Knees wide, big toes touching, arms extended forward. Breathe into the back ribs — parasympathetic shift before leaving the gym.

sets.notes: **5 minutes of recovery work, non-negotiable closing block (W12D1 discussion outcome).** Targets the four chains that get hammered in gym work: pec minor (doorframe), shoulder decompression (dead hang), thoracic rotation (supine twist), hip rotators (90/90), parasympathetic reset (child's pose). Goes after the last lift, before walking out. This is what catches the connective-tissue debt that builds across hard training weeks.

tips: **Spotlights: face pull + reverse fly.** Both posterior-shoulder lifts that pair with the push-day shoulder rehab. Drill the cues, hold the loads.
**Face pull: elbows HIGH + external rotation IS the lift.** Without those cues it's just a row.
**Reverse fly: lighter + cleaner > heavier + sloppy.** W11D3 'lighter but somehow never easier' is the form-first thesis in one sentence.
**Pull-ups bumped to 6-8** after W11's clean 7/7/7/6. Mix grips: rack handles → flat bar wide → flat bar close → wide slow.

**Forced equipment subs from W11D3** carry forward as fallbacks: chest-supported row 30 (35 if available), reverse fly 20 (22.5 if available), lat pulldown dual pulley 70/side (regular machine 140 if available).
**Left bicep weak side — left first.** Strict reps only, no assist past asymmetry.
**High cable curl 160 + cable crunch 130** — small pushes, NOT jumps. W11 said both had headroom.
**DB row pyramid 60/70/80 holds** — but if 80 still breaks slow form like W11, drop to 75 instead. Clean > heavy.

**NEW closing block: behind-back DB shrugs 30-40lb x 12 x 3.** W12D1 discussion outcome — the grip → shoulder pattern responds to loaded trap chain work. Cue is 'down + back, not up.' See `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`. **Report if it worsens the coupling** — that means PT eval.
Set count trim: lat pulldown 4→3 sets, redirected intensity to face pull + curl/crunch finisher.
Rotation plan: W13 preacher curl + high cable curl + cable crunch · W13 closing block rotates to trap-3 raises · W14 cycle back to pull-up + DB row.
Rest 2.5 min pull-ups, 90s DB block (4 lifts circuit), 2 min lat pulldown, 90s face pull, 60s curl/crunch finisher, 60s closing shrug block.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building, week 3 of the cycle. Same loads as W11D3 with sharper cues on face pull + reverse fly. Pull-ups step up to 6-8 because last week's 7/7/7/6 was clean. Small pushes on the lifts that had headroom (high cable curl, cable crunch). The cue is still the progression.

name: Week 12 Push — Form-First (Spotlight: Lateral Raise + Kinesis Cable Fly)
description: Third week of the form-first cycle. Spotlights rotate to **lateral raise + Kinesis cable fly** — the two W11D1 lifts that ran clean and have room to drill deeper. **W11D1 takeaways baked in:** chest press 165 cratered at the end (user wondered if fatigue ordering caused it — 165x10/8/3, dropped to 150x5); incline DB deloaded to 40lb (right chest/shoulder tight going in, 40 felt fairly easy); end-block all on Kinesis (cable column was occupied) — Kinesis 3 = lat raise, Kinesis 5 = tri ext, Kinesis 6 = straight-arm pulldown. W12 keeps cable column as default but locks the Kinesis fallback numbers as known-good. Equipment-consolidated layout holds.

dayOverview.theme: Lateral raise + Kinesis cable fly in the spotlight — both ran cleanly W11D1 and have headroom to drill cues deeper. Chest press fatigue protocol: drop to 3 working sets (vs W11's 4), permission to drop to 150 anytime form breaks. Incline DB held at 40 unless the right shoulder is warm and quiet. Kinesis fallback numbers locked from W11D1.

spotlights.why: W11D1 hit 15lb x 20/20/15+5 with slow tempo getting 'hard slow' at the end — that's the right load with room to drill the cue. The W10D1 scap-reset-between-sets protocol IS the spotlight: lead-with-elbow + scap-reset-default + 2s eccentric. Drill the rep itself, not the load.

W11D1 'felt great, made shoulder feel good especially at the stretch' at Kinesis 8. The lift that helps the right shoulder is the lift to drill — soft-elbow-fixed + hug-a-tree + slow eccentric for the deep stretch. The cue is the progression.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 push press + incline DB · W11 chest press machine · W12 lateral raise + Kinesis cable fly (current) · W13 cable lat raise + incline tri ext + straight-arm pulldown · W14 cycle back to push press + incline DB.
watchpoints: **Chest press fatigue ordering question** (W11D1) — user: 'really hard today, i think it was bc it was at the end?' W12 keeps the consolidated late placement (form-cues lesson: tradeoff acceptable for non-spotlight weeks) but cuts from 4 sets to 3, with explicit permission to drop 165 → 150 the moment form breaks. Test: does 3x10 at 165 feel cleaner than W11's 4-set crater?

**Right chest / right shoulder still connected and tight** (W11D1) — warmups are helping (W11 'shoulder warmer/less painful than W10'). 40lb incline DB held; only push to 45 if the right side feels warm and quiet on set 1.

**Cable column vs Kinesis** — cable column is the default; if it's occupied, the W11D1-locked Kinesis numbers are: lat raise Kinesis 3, tri ext Kinesis 5, straight-arm pulldown Kinesis 6 (≈ cable column 100). Don't re-calibrate these — they're known.
Yuri band red warmup non-negotiable. Doorframe pec stretch in the morning flow add-on.

exercises.notes: Slow, big circles. Wake up scap glide before loading.
Full sequence with red band. Non-negotiable on push day.
Ribs down, glutes tight, body in one line. Slow eccentric, 1s pause with chest 1-2" off the floor.

sets.name: 🔆 Station 1: DB Rack — Push Press + Lateral Raise (Spotlight)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 40/side (HOLD — W11D1 hit 40 x 10/10/8, very consistent). 3 sets, 8-10 clean.
- **Feet parallel, hip-width** — locked since W7
- **Ribs DOWN before the dip** — catch rib flare on set 1, don't carry it into set 2
- Shallow dip ~3-4", straight down through the heels
- Vertical DB path, head pokes through at the top
- Stop the set if rib position breaks

**LOAD:** 15lb (HOLD — W11D1 hit 15 x 20/20/15+5 with the last set 'hard slow'). 3 sets.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Lead with elbow, hand passive** — wrist follows, no wrist break. The W10D1 shooting-line came from leading with hand instead of elbow.
- **Soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED** through the rep — locked elbow recruits traps + stresses joint.
- **Stop at shoulder height — never higher.** Past shoulder height = traps eat the rep, medial delt offloads.
- **"Pour the water out of two cups"** at the top — slight internal rotation, pinky leads up by a hair.
- **2s eccentric** — that's where the deltoid grows. Speed up the descent and you've lost the stimulus.
- **Scap-reset between sets is the DEFAULT** (W10D1 protocol that worked): roll shoulders + actively engage/disengage scapula a few times. Don't wait for the crackle to show up; reset preemptively.
- **Massage out the right pec between sets** if the shoulder starts talking — connected pattern from W10D1.

**Reflection:** does set 3 feel cleaner than W11's 15+5 stop-out? The W11 'hard slow' was the right state — replicate it for all 20 reps this week, not just the last 5.

sets.name: Station 2: DB Rack — Incline Bench (Adjust Bench to 30°)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 40lb pair default; **try 45 only if right shoulder is warm + quiet on set 1**, else hold 40 (W11D1 deload from 45 was the right call when the right side was tight). 3 sets.
- **Bench at 30°**, NOT 45°. Forearm stack is automatic at 30°.
- **Shoulders packed back AND down** — pin scaps into bench like trying to break it in half.
- Elbows ~45° from torso
- 1s pause at the bottom, no DB clack at the top

**Set count cut from 4 to 3** to manage chest-press fatigue downstream — W11's set 4 of incline + 4 sets of chest press was where fatigue compounded. 3 here makes room for chest press at 165 with cleaner reps.

sets.name: Station 3: Chest Press Machine (Fatigue Test — 3 Sets, Drop to 150 If Form Breaks)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 165 (HOLD — W10D1 was the edge-of-form weight, W11D1 cratered at the end and user wondered about ordering). **3 sets, NOT 4.** Explicit permission to drop to 150 the moment form breaks — don't grind through a 3-rep set the way W11 did.

**Spotlight was W11; sharp cues only:**
- **Seat height: handles align with mid-chest / nipple line**, set BEFORE sitting
- **Shoulders pinned BACK against the back pad** — the foundation cue
- **3s eccentric** — count it, the W11 'huge struggle' was tempo getting rushed
- **Press to almost extension** — leave a hair of bend, don't lock
- **Massage out right pec between sets** (W10D1 trick) — pre-empt the referral

**Set protocol:**
- Set 1: 165 x 10 (target)
- Set 2: 165 x 10 (target — if form breaks at rep 6-7, finish what you can clean and stop)
- Set 3: **165 x 8-10 IF form is holding, else drop to 150 x 10 immediately.** No grinding 165x3 to honor the number.

**Test question:** does 3x clean at 165 (with permission to drop to 150) feel better than W11's 4 grinding sets? If yes, the order tradeoff is acceptable. If still rough, W13 tries the spotlight-earlier reorder.

**LOAD:** Kinesis 8 (HOLD — W11D1 'felt great, did all sets, made shoulder feel good especially at the stretch'). 3 sets.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Slight forward hinge at hips** — chest leads, NOT head. Standing fully upright = upper chest only, lose mid/lower pec.
- **Soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED throughout** — these are NOT bicep curls, the elbow doesn't move through the rep. Elbow angle changing = becomes a press.
- **"Hug a tree"** — squeeze comes from the chest, NOT the arms pulling together. Mental image is the difference between this lift working and not.
- **Stop a fistwidth apart at the top** — don't crash the handles. Last few inches are arm work, not chest. The squeeze peaks before contact.
- **Slow eccentric (~3s)** — let the cables pull the arms back, get the deep pec lengthen. This is where the lift earns its keep.
- **Stretch is the stimulus** — W11D1 'shoulder felt good especially at the stretch.' That's the lift telling you what to drill.
- **Breath:** out on squeeze, in on stretch.

**Reflection:** at set 3, is the squeeze coming from the chest or has it shifted to the arms? When the arms take over, the lift stops working — that's the cue to stop the set, not push through.

sets.name: Station 5: Cable Column Block (Lat Raise → Tri Ext → Straight Arm Pulldown)

exercises.equipment: cable column (default) — Kinesis 3 fallback if column is occupied
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column ~30-40lb (form-first range). **Kinesis fallback (W11D1 calibration): Kinesis 3 ≈ 30 cable equivalent.** 3 sets per side, one arm at a time.
- Stand sideways to the column, working arm farther from the stack
- Soft elbow ~10°, fixed throughout
- Lead with elbow, hand passive
- Stop at shoulder height — higher = traps
- Fight the cable on the way down, no snap-back
- **Massage right pec between sets** — kills the crackle (W10D1 protocol)
- **Skip 'run line behind body' cue** — doesn't apply at this gym's geometry

exercises.equipment: cable column kneeling (default) — Kinesis 5 fallback if column is occupied
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column kneeling — find the working number. **Kinesis fallback (W11D1 calibration): Kinesis 5 (4 was too easy).** 3 sets.
- Kneel facing AWAY from the high pulley, rope overhead
- Torso slightly forward — that's the 'incline'
- **Elbows pinned**, only forearms move
- Hands shoulder-width on rope (long-head emphasis)
- Full stretch at the bottom, stop just shy of lockout, slow eccentric

exercises.equipment: cable column (default) — Kinesis 6 ≈ 100 cable column fallback
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 100 (HOLD — W10D1 working number). **Kinesis fallback (W11D1): Kinesis 6 ≈ cable column 100** (user calibration: 'machine not linear, this feels like 100'). 3 sets.
- Stand a step back, hinge ~15° at hips
- Slight elbow bend (5°), fixed throughout — perfectly straight = elbow risk
- **Pull bar to thighs, NOT past** — past = shoulders roll forward, lats off
- Squeeze lats hard at bottom — 'oranges into back pockets'
- Slow controlled return to full overhead stretch

tips: **Spotlights: lateral raise + Kinesis cable fly.** Both ran clean W11D1; the cue is the progression at held loads.
**Lateral raise scap-reset is the DEFAULT** — don't wait for crackle, reset between every set.
**Kinesis cable fly: drill the stretch + hug-a-tree squeeze.** W11D1 said the right shoulder felt better at the stretch — that's the lift telling you what to drill.
**Chest press fatigue test:** 3 sets at 165, NOT 4. Permission to drop 150 the moment form breaks. No 3-rep grinding sets.
**Incline DB held at 40** (W11 deload was correct); only push to 45 if right shoulder is warm + quiet on set 1.
**Kinesis fallback numbers locked from W11D1** — lat raise 3, tri ext 5, straight-arm pulldown 6. Don't recalibrate; defer to cable column when free.
**Right pec massage protocol** — kills the crackle on chest press + cable lat raise. Pre-empt, don't react.
Yuri band red warmup non-negotiable. Doorframe pec stretch in morning flow add-on.
Rotation plan: W13 cable lat raise + incline tri ext + straight-arm pulldown · W14 cycle back to push press + incline DB.
Rest 2 min DB rack work, 2.5 min chest press, 75s cables.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building, week 3 of the cycle. Lifts that ran clean W11D1 (lateral raise, Kinesis cable fly) get the deep cue treatment. Lifts that struggled (chest press) get a structural fix — fewer sets, explicit drop-down protocol. The cue is still the progression. Building decades, not weeks.

name: Week 13 KB Conditioning — Heal Week, Full Variety (Spotlight: Bent Press) + Figure-8 + NEW Controlled-Power Flow
description: W13 is a shoulder-heal week; this KB day is **now on Saturday** (moved from Thursday — see the schedule change). It keeps **full variety, not trimmed** — KB flow work isn't the fatigue source. **Spotlight cycles back to bent press** per the rotation tracker — it rested W12, returns as a leverage-cue refresh. **The figure-8 → tactical clean flow stays** — a big W12D4 hit ('strength + cardio + very entertaining'). **NEW: a controlled-power flow block** (@metabolicfactor — around-body pass → side swing → high pull → goblet squat): flowy, passing-based (a cousin of the figure-8), non-overhead, the 'metabolism going' conditioning feel. **W12D5 lesson:** the figure-8 done 'more than written' tightened the QL — keep the dose honest. Legs day is now the day BEFORE this (Friday), so go easy on the loaded squatting in the flows. TGU + windmill are maintenance.

dayOverview.theme: Shoulder-heal week, KB day on Saturday now — full variety kept. Bent press cycles back to spotlight (leverage refresh). Figure-8 flow stays + a NEW controlled-power flow block adds fresh conditioning variety. Legs was yesterday — keep loaded squatting in the flows light. TGU + windmill maintenance.

spotlights.why: Cycles back to spotlight per the tracker (rested W12). W10 was its first spotlight — the leverage unlock: the body goes DOWN, the bell barely moves. W13 refreshes that cue at a light load. A deload Thursday is the ideal low-stakes week to re-groove a skill lift.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 windmill mechanics · W12 TGU left-side checkpoints · W13 bent press (current, DELOAD refresh) · W14 cycle forward — figure-8 flow stays in rotation, windmill/TGU rotate as needed.
watchpoints: **W13 is a shoulder-heal week — this KB day keeps FULL variety, now on Saturday.** The heal is in held loads, not a shortened session. Skill + flow + conditioning, no load chasing.

**Keep the dose honest on the flows.** W12D4 the user did the figure-8 'more than written' (to music) and the QL came in 'extremely tight' the next day. Legs was YESTERDAY (Friday) now — the QL is already worked, so go easy on the loaded squatting in the controlled-power + OTD flow blocks.
**TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift** — straight-arm overhead lockout, no rack hold during the get-up. Both sides feel fine now (W12D4), so this is maintenance, not rehab.
**Right shoulder watch on the figure-8 clean catch** — same crackly pattern as push days. If anything talks during the catch, drop to figure-8 passes only (no clean) and finish the round.
**Go easy on loaded squatting — legs was yesterday.** This Saturday KB day is a recovery flank for the legs; keep the goblet squats and flow squats light and controlled.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Slow circles, both directions. Shoulder loosener.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Mini-band overhead OR blue rope band gripped in hands.
Elbows drive to the ribs, scaps depress.
Press out to a T — fires rear delts and mid-traps.
Pull apart at the sternum, scaps retract, ribs down.
Sweep the arms back. Don't overarch.
15s each side. Primes the lateral hinge — directly preps the bent press.
Both arms in a V/Y overhead. Hold and breathe — lower traps fire.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Sustained tension, no rest between positions.

sets.name: OTD Flow (Light Groove)

exercises.notes: Bell starts on the floor between the feet, hips drive the lift, catch in the rack with the knees slightly bent.
Goblet/rack position. Sit tall, full depth, no bouncing.
Stride out wide, sit into the working leg, keep the other leg long. Mobility, not strength.
Control on the way down — bell to the floor as cleanly as it came up.

sets.notes: **This is a continuous FLOW, not separate stations.** Move through the chain as one — dead clean → front squat → lateral lunge → tactical clean — letting each move feed the next, then switch sides and flow back. About 2 reps' worth at each move, but don't stop to count — let the rhythm carry it. 20lb throughout, 2 rounds. Deload + light groove — should feel effortless.

sets.name: 🔆 Skill Drilling (Bent Press Spotlight + TGU + Windmill)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD — the home-base skill weight; deload, no push). 2 rounds, ~4 reps each side.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — refresh the leverage cue (W10's unlock):**
- **The body goes DOWN; the bell barely moves.** This is the whole lift. It is NOT a press — you don't push the bell up, you lower your body underneath a bell that stays roughly where it is.
- **Bell locked overhead, eyes track the bell** the entire rep
- **Hinge and rotate AWAY from the bell side** — the same lateral-hinge family as the windmill
- **Reach the free hand down toward the inside of the front foot** as the torso lowers
- **Long spine throughout** — the bend is at the hip, not a crunch of the spine
- **Slow and deliberate** — bent press is a skill lift, fluidity over speed

**Reflection:** does the 'body down, bell still' feeling click this round, or are you still pressing the bell? If it still feels like a press, slow down and lighten — a deload Thursday is the perfect low-stakes week to re-find the leverage.

**LOAD:** 25lb (light — maintenance, both sides feel fine now per W12D4). 2 rounds.
- **Straight-arm overhead lockout** — punch the bell up, arm stays locked vertical the whole way; no rack hold during the get-up
- Eyes ON the bell every checkpoint
- Pause at every checkpoint — no rushing, the reverse is half the lift
- Hip bridge: glute squeeze, ribs down

**LOAD:** 18-20lb (light — maintenance after its W11 spotlight). 2 rounds.
- Bell locked overhead, eyes track the bell
- **Hip hinge AWAY from the bell side**
- **Stretch lives in the back leg's hip flexor, NOT the lower back**
- Slow descent, brief pause, controlled return

sets.name: Skill Block: Figure-8 → Tactical Clean (Stays in Rotation — PRESCRIBED Dose Only)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD: 12kg (26lb) only.** 2 rounds of 60s continuous flow.

**The figure-8-tactical-clean is ONE compound move** — the figure-8 pass exits into a clean to rack, repeated alternating sides:
- START in the hike position (bell between the legs, hinged at the hips)
- **Figure-8 → exit into a tactical clean to rack** — pass between the legs, hand catches BEHIND, the pull becomes the clean
- Lower the bell back through the hike position; the next pass switches sides
- Continue alternating for the full 60s

**Cues:**
- **The figure-8 EXITS into the clean** — the bell does not return to a hand mid-pass; the catch becomes the clean
- **Tactical clean = controlled, threaded into the rack** — no yank, no forearm slam
- **Hike is home base** — every transition resets through hike
- **Right shoulder watch on the rack catch** — if it talks, drop to figure-8 passes only and finish the round

**⚠️ DOSE DISCIPLINE — do the prescribed 2 rounds, NOT more.** W12D4 the user did this flow 'more than written' (to music) and W12D5 came in with the QL/psoas 'extremely tight.' The flow is fun — that's the trap. 2 rounds is the dose. Legs was yesterday — the QL is already worked, no need for bonus rounds.

sets.notes: Figure-8 flow stays in rotation (W12D4 confirmed it lands well). 2 rounds at 12kg, ~3 min work + rest. The discipline this week is doing the prescribed dose and stopping — the W12D5 QL tightness traced straight back to bonus rounds here.

sets.name: 🆕 Controlled-Power Flow (@metabolicfactor — NEW, Non-Overhead Conditioning)

exercises.notes: Pass the bell around the body, hand to hand — the passing-flow cousin of the figure-8. Stand tall, ribs down, let the bell travel smoothly.
One-arm swing out to the side on a diagonal — hips drive it, not the arm. Stay light.
Hinge-driven pull — elbow leads high, bell to upper chest. Sub-overhead, no pressing out at the top.
Bell at the chest, full depth, sit tall. **Legs were yesterday — keep these light and controlled.**

sets.notes: **🆕 NEW — @metabolicfactor controlled-power circuit, added W13 for fresh variety.** Around-body pass → side swing → high pull → goblet squat, flowing continuously, 2 rounds. **Non-overhead and passing-based** — the flowy 'metabolism going' feel you liked from the figure-8, with no overhead pressing to tax the healing shoulder. Single light bell (~20-26lb). Keep the goblet squats light — legs were yesterday.

exercises.notes: 60s each side. Hip flexor release — counters the TGU hip bridge + lateral lunge work.
60s. Adductors get tight from the lateral lunges + figure-8 stance.

tips: **W13 is a shoulder-heal week — this KB day keeps full variety, now on Saturday.** Skill + flow + conditioning; the heal is in held loads, not a shortened session.
**Spotlight: bent press** — refresh the leverage cue (body goes DOWN, bell barely moves). A heal week is a low-stakes window to re-groove a skill lift.
**Figure-8 flow stays — keep the dose honest.** W12D4's bonus rounds tightened the QL the next day. 2 rounds is the dose; fun is the trap.
**🆕 NEW: controlled-power flow** (@metabolicfactor — around-body pass → side swing → high pull → goblet squat). Non-overhead, passing-based — fresh variety in the figure-8 family.
**TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift** — straight-arm overhead lockout, no rack hold during the get-up. Maintenance this week — both sides feel fine.
**Right shoulder watch on the figure-8 rack catch** — drop to passes only if it talks.
**Legs was yesterday** — go easy on loaded squatting in the flows; this Saturday KB day is a recovery flank for the legs.
Cooldown mandatory — pigeon + butterfly.
Rotation plan: W14 cycles forward — figure-8 flow stays, windmill/TGU rotate as needed.
formPhilosophy: Shoulder-heal week, KB day on Saturday — full variety kept, not a shrunken session. Skill + flow + conditioning: bent press leverage, the figure-8 flow, the new controlled-power circuit. The heal is in held loads and managed overhead volume. The discipline is dosing the flows honestly — a fun flow is the easiest thing to overdo.

name: Week 13 KB Full Body — Heal Week, Full Variety (Spotlight: C&P + TGU) + NEW Brutal Complex
description: W13 is a **shoulder-heal week** — the heavy PPL volume backs off and the right-shoulder cluster gets a beat. The KB day is **NOT trimmed**: KB flow/skill work isn't what's fatiguing (the shoulder + heavy lifting are), so this day keeps full variety. The @Asgooch overhead mobility complex runs its full **5 rounds** per side, and a **NEW non-overhead conditioning block — the @Asgooch Brutal Complex** (Swing → AB-stance goblet squat → alternating row → close-grip pushup) — joins as a finisher for fresh variety, deliberately non-overhead so it adds work without taxing the healing shoulder. Spotlights cycle back to **C&P + TGU** per the rotation tracker — a refresh of technique cues. Cossack + windmill drop to maintenance after their W12 spotlight. **W12 takeaways:** the Asgooch flow landed well; C&P hit 45 cleanly ('everything went great'); TGU both sides 'feel fine now' (W12D4).

dayOverview.theme: Shoulder-heal week — but the KB day keeps FULL variety, not trimmed (flow work isn't the fatigue source). Asgooch flow runs all 5 rounds; a new non-overhead Brutal Complex finisher adds fresh conditioning. Spotlights cycle back to C&P + TGU — technique refresh. Cossack + windmill go to maintenance. C&P holds 45, TGU holds 30. The only heal-week concession is keeping overhead-SHOULDER volume managed — modify the Asgooch overhead moves if the shoulder talks.

spotlights.why: Cycles back to spotlight per the rotation tracker. W11/W12 confirmed 45 grooves cleanly ('everything went great'). Deload week refreshes the technique cues at 45 — knee-bent catch, hips drive the clean, ribs down on the press — without the pull toward 50. The cue is the work.

Cycles back to spotlight. W12D4: 'TGU on both sides feel fine now' — the W12 left-side checkpoint drilling did its job, the left is no longer the weak link. W13 drills full checkpoint discipline at 30lb on both sides equally — the straight-arm overhead lockout, pause at every checkpoint, slow reverse. Refresh, not rehab.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 swing mechanics + bent press leverage · W12 windmill + cossack mobility · W13 C&P + TGU (current, DELOAD refresh) · W14 cycle forward — bent press / flow work returns.
watchpoints: **W13 is a shoulder-heal week — but the KB day is NOT shortened.** Full variety stays: Asgooch flow 5 rounds, plus the new Brutal Complex finisher. The heal applies to LOADS — C&P holds 45 (no 50), TGU holds 30, no load chasing. Variety is rich; weight is held.

**TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift.** Punch the bell to a stable straight-arm overhead lockout — the arm stays locked vertical the whole way up and down. There is NO rack hold during the get-up (the clean-to-rack is only the brief setup before pressing overhead). Wobble at the lockout = wobble through the lift.

**Right shoulder watch through all overhead work** — the Asgooch flow stacks 4 overhead moves. If the shoulder talks during the snatch or OH lunge, drop the weight or modify (front-rack lunge instead of OH lunge).
**Overhead squat is the hardest move in the flow** — if mobility limits, REDUCE DEPTH before reducing load.
Cossack + windmill are maintenance this week — sharp cues only, both at 25lb, range is the work.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Big slow circles — open the shoulders before loading overhead.
**LOAD:** 20lb. Light — test the overhead position. If anything cracks/twinges, do extra band warmup before the flow.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Open the hips. Sit in the bottom for a beat.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Mini-band OR blue rope band held in hands — both work.
Elbows drive to the ribs, scaps depress.
Don't overarch the lower back to chase range.
15s each side. Primes the lateral hinge.
Both arms in a V/Y, hold and breathe. Lower traps fire — preps the overhead lockout for the flow and the TGU.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Sustained tension, no rest between positions.

sets.name: Block 1: Asgooch Overhead Mobility Complex — LEFT SIDE (5 Rounds — Full Variety Kept)

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 20-25lb (start at 25 if smooth, drop to 20 if the right shoulder warmup felt off). Left hand. Controlled pull, full extension, hips hinged, back flat.
Left hand. **Hips drive the bell**, not the arm. Straight overhead, punch through at the top — smooth catch sets up the next 3 moves.
Left hand overhead. **Lock the elbow, bell over the shoulder, torso TALL.** Drive through the front HEEL. **Right shoulder talks → modify to a front-rack lunge** on this move only.
Left hand overhead. **Hardest move in the flow.** Full depth IF mobility allows; **reduce depth before load** if mobility limits. Bell stacked over the shoulder, core braced, no lower-back arching.
Left hand. **Dip and drive** — legs do the work, arm guides. Smooth descent to the rack. End of one round.

sets.notes: **5 rounds LEFT side**, all moves continuous, no rest within a round. Bell stays in the working hand for the whole round. Rest 30-45s between rounds. **Weak side first** — left gets the freshest reps.

sets.name: Block 1 (continued): Asgooch Overhead Mobility Complex — RIGHT SIDE (5 Rounds)

exercises.notes: Right hand. Controlled pull, full extension.
Right hand. Hips drive, punch through at the top.
Right hand overhead. **Right shoulder watchpoint** — most likely place for the crackle. Front-rack lunge if needed.
Right hand overhead. Same depth/load discipline as the left side.
Right hand. Finish strong. End of the complex.

sets.notes: **5 rounds RIGHT side** after the left is done. Same rest pattern. **Right shoulder watchpoint** — the snatch + OH lunge + OH squat stack the overhead position; if the shoulder talks, modify (front-rack lunge, front squat) rather than grinding. This is the one heal-week concession — keep the overhead moves clean, don't push through a cranky shoulder.

sets.name: Block 2: Cossack + Windmill (Maintenance — sharp cues only)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb goblet (HOLD). Maintenance — spotlight was W12.
- Sit DEEP, heels stay down
- Working leg in full external rotation
- Slow descent, hard stop at end-range — that's the work, not failure
- Range, not load

**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD). Maintenance — spotlight was W12.
- Bell locked overhead, eye tracks the bell
- **Hip hinge AWAY from the bell side**
- **Stretch lives in the back leg's hip flexor, NOT the lower back**
- Long spine throughout

**LOAD:** 30 (HOLD — light; the Asgooch flow already loaded the shoulder, and it's a deload). 2 rounds.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — refresh the checkpoint discipline (W12D4: both sides feel fine now, so drill BOTH equally):**
- **The TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift** — punch the bell to a stable straight-arm overhead lockout and keep the arm locked vertical the whole way up and down. There is NO rack hold during the get-up. If the overhead lockout is wobbly, the rest of the lift inherits the wobble.
- **Eyes ON the bell** at every checkpoint — eyes leaving the bell = bell drift
- **Pause at every checkpoint** — lying → elbow → hand → hip bridge → leg sweep → lunge → stand → reverse. No rushing.
- **Hip bridge: glute squeeze, ribs down** (no flare — rib flare = lower-back load)
- **Reverse with the same discipline as forward** — the descent is half the lift
- **'Focus on the clean itself'** (the W10D2 unlock) — the setup IS the work

**Pattern:** 2 forward + 2 reverse per side, left side first. **Reflection:** with both sides clean now, does the checkpoint discipline hold up under no-rush attention, or does a checkpoint still get skipped when the focus drifts? The deload is the week to make every checkpoint deliberate.

sets.name: 🔆 Block 4: Clean & Press (Spotlight — Technique Refresh at 45)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 45 (HOLD — W11/W12 confirmed 45 grooves cleanly, 'everything went great'). **NO 50 — it's a deload, and 50 was never the W13 plan.** 2 rounds.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — refresh the technique cues at 45:**
- **Catch the clean with the knees slightly bent** — the W3 cue, durable: the knee bend absorbs the clean so there's zero impact at the rack
- **Hips drive the clean**, NOT the arm — the arm just guides the bell into the rack
- **Press: ribs DOWN** (the W8 trigger) — no rib flare under the press
- **Bicep finishes by the ear, full lockout** at the top
- **Lower with control — no slam** back to the rack

**Symmetry check:** if the left at 45 feels rougher than the right, that's normal (right is ahead) — but if it's a big gap, do the left reps slower. Quality wins. **Reflection:** at a held 45 with full attention on the cues, does the clean feel automatic? If yes, that's the technique grooved — W14+ can revisit 50.

sets.name: 🆕 Block 5: Figure-8 → Tactical Clean (Skill Flow — Now Practiced Twice a Week)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD: 12kg (26lb).** 2 rounds of 60s continuous flow.

The figure-8-tactical-clean is ONE compound move — the figure-8 pass between the legs exits into a clean to rack, alternating sides:
- START in the hike position (bell between the legs, hinged at the hips)
- **Figure-8 → exit into a tactical clean to rack** — pass between the legs, hand catches BEHIND, the pull becomes the clean
- Lower the bell back through hike; the next pass switches sides
- Continue alternating for the full 60s

**Cues:**
- **The figure-8 EXITS into the clean** — the bell doesn't return to a hand mid-pass; the catch becomes the clean
- **Tactical clean = controlled, threaded into the rack** — no yank, no forearm slam
- **Hike is home base** — every transition resets through hike
- **Right shoulder watch on the rack catch** — if it talks, drop to figure-8 passes only and finish the round

This is the W12D4 flow you liked — added to Tuesday so you're drilling it twice a week (here + Saturday's kb-cond). Skill practice, light bell, keep it smooth.

sets.notes: Light figure-8 skill block — 2 rounds at 12kg, ~3 min work + rest. You're now practicing this flow twice weekly (Tuesday + Saturday). The pass exits INTO the clean — that's the skill being drilled.

sets.name: 🆕 Block 6: Brutal Complex Finisher (@Asgooch — NEW, Non-Overhead Conditioning)

exercises.notes: Hips snap the bell to chest height — it's a hinge, not a squat, not a front raise. Shoulders packed down.
Bell at the chest, offset (B-stance) goblet squat — most of the load on the front leg, the back foot is a kickstand. Sit tall, full depth. Switch the front leg each round.
Hinged over, flat back, row one side then the other. Pull the elbow to the hip, no torso rotation.
Hands close (on the floor or on the bell handle). Ribs down, body in one line, slow. Scale to the knees if the rounds get ragged.

sets.notes: **🆕 NEW — the @Asgooch Brutal Complex, added W13 for fresh conditioning variety.** Swing → AB-stance goblet squat → alternating row → close-grip pushup, 10 reps each, **continuous — don't put the bell down between moves.** 3 rounds. **Deliberately non-overhead** — it adds a real conditioning hit without loading the healing shoulder overhead. Single bell, moderate weight (start ~25-30lb). This is the 'metabolism going' kind of block — pace it, but keep moving.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 25lb. Light row, 2s pull / 2s lower. Skip if the shoulders are cooked from the flow.
60s each side. Hip flexor release.
60s. Adductors get tight on KB days from cossacks.

tips: **W13 is a shoulder-heal week — but the KB day keeps full variety, not trimmed.** Asgooch flow runs all 5 rounds; the heal applies to LOADS (C&P 45, TGU 30, no chasing), not to variety.

**🆕 NEW: Brutal Complex finisher** (@Asgooch — swing → AB-stance goblet squat → alt row → close-grip pushup, 3 rounds). Non-overhead by design — fresh conditioning that doesn't tax the healing shoulder.
**Figure-8 → tactical clean flow added to Tuesday** — you're now drilling the W12D4 flow you liked twice a week (Tue here + Sat kb-cond). Light skill practice, 12kg.
**Spotlights cycle back to C&P + TGU** — a technique refresh of two lifts that already grooved well. Drill the cues at held loads.
**TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift** — straight-arm overhead lockout, no rack hold during the get-up. Both sides feel fine now (W12D4) — drill checkpoint discipline equally.
**C&P: knee-bent catch, hips drive the clean, ribs down on the press.** 45 is the held working weight — 50 is a post-deload conversation.
**Cossack + windmill are maintenance** — sharp cues only, both at 25lb, range is the work.
**Right shoulder watch through all overhead work** — modify the OH lunge to a front-rack lunge if the shoulder talks.
Cooldown mandatory — pigeon + butterfly.
Rotation plan: W14 cycles forward — bent press / flow work returns.
formPhilosophy: Shoulder-heal week — but the KB day is where variety lives, not where volume gets cut. Full Asgooch flow + a new non-overhead conditioning complex. The heal is in the held loads and the managed overhead-shoulder volume, not in a shortened session. The C&P + TGU spotlight refreshes cues that already work.

name: Week 13 Legs — DELOAD (Spotlight: 🆕 Bulgarian Split Squat — Replaces the Walking Lunge)
description: **DELOAD WEEK.** Same deload as the rest of W13 — hold all loads, cut volume ~60-70%. Squat HOLDS at 75/side but runs as an explicit pyramid (W12D5 discovery: fewer reps per set gives the user cleaner depth and removes the rep-count fear). **NEW: the Bulgarian split squat officially replaces the walking lunge** as the program's unilateral leg lift (W12D5 user request — 'officially introduce bulgarians'). It is the W13 spotlight, introduced at conservative deload volume — bodyweight or light DBs, form-only. **W12D5 takeaways baked in:** squat self-pyramided 75 x 10/6/6/8 ('doing fewer lets me do it way better'); leg press 3 plates full ROM is 'a QL massage' — depth is the work, hold the plates; calf raise setup corrected (balls of the feet on the bottom platform edge, no plate); seated leg curl 90 at the corrected ankle lever held clean; incline RDL 25lb dead slow held. **NEW QL/psoas release move added** — the standing cross-leg lateral lean (W12D5 user discovery) is now in the warmup.

dayOverview.theme: DELOAD — hold every load, cut every exercise a set. Squat holds 75/side as a 3-set pyramid (W12D5 confirmed pyramiding beats flat sets for this user). The NEW Bulgarian split squat is the spotlight, introduced form-only at deload volume — it replaces the walking lunge permanently. Leg press holds 3 plates and keeps chasing full ROM ('the core isn't untucked yet,' W12D5). The cool-down biases toward hip/QL — and the new cross-leg lateral lean is in the warmup.

spotlights.why: 🆕 W12D5 user request — 'officially introduce bulgarians into the next leg day.' Replaces the walking lunge as the unilateral leg lift: rear foot fixed (no travel) makes it more controlled, a better single-leg builder, and gentler on the QL — a good fit for a deload introduction. As a NEW lift in a deload week the spotlight is the PATTERN, not the load: front-heel drive, vertical-shin front leg, torso tall. Bodyweight or light DBs only this week.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 leg press · W12 seated leg curl + calf raise · W13 Bulgarian split squat (current — NEW lift, DELOAD introduction) · W14 cycle back to squat + incline RDL.
watchpoints: **This is a DELOAD — hold loads, cut sets.** Squat 3 sets (not 4), leg press 3 (not 4), leg curl + incline RDL + calf raise 2 each (not 3). If a lift feels easy, the deload is working — do NOT add reps or weight.

**Squat HOLDS at 75/side — no push.** W12D5 cleared 75 cleanly. Deload runs it as a 3-set pyramid (8/6/8): the 6-rep middle set is the deepest, cleanest one. W12D5: 'doing fewer lets me do it way better and not fear the reps being too many.'

**🆕 Bulgarian split squat is a NEW lift — introduce it, don't load it.** Bodyweight or light DBs, 2 sets x 8-10 each leg, form-only. Smith machine preferred (`smith-machine-bulgarian-split-squat`) but it's always taken here — the realistic default is a bench + light DBs at the sides.

**Leg press HOLDS 3 plates — chase DEPTH, not plates.** W12D5: full ROM at 3 plates is 'a QL massage'; the deep ROM works the weak core/QL. The user's own call: full ROM at lighter load until 'the core muscles get untucked.' No 4-plate test this week — it's a deload.

**QL/psoas watchpoint.** W12D5 came in with the QL/psoas 'extremely tight' from the W12D4 figure-8 volume. The NEW cross-leg lateral lean is in the warmup specifically for this. If anything in the QL talks during the warmup, modify the day — abort any lift that twinges.
Cool-down stays — 5-min block, hip/QL-biased on legs day.

sets.name: Warmup (incl. 🆕 Cross-Leg Lateral Lean — QL/Psoas Release)

exercises.notes: Full depth, slow. Find where the hips are tight and breathe into it. Depth-check before loading.

**🆕 NEW — W12D5 user discovery, now official.** 30s each side. Scissor one foot over the other, reach the same-side arm overhead, lean AWAY from the back leg — the stretch runs down the side of the waist into the QL and the lateral hip. The user found this loosens a tight QL/psoas better than a square-stance side bend. **Do this BEFORE loading the squat** — especially if the QL feels tight coming off the KB day.
Big slow circles. Wake up the hip stabilizers.
Spine mobility — extra important post-QL-spasm history.
Bodyweight. Use this as a QL check — if anything talks here, modify the day. (It also previews the Bulgarian split squat pattern coming up.)
Set 1: 45/side x8. Set 2: 65/side x8. Same depth as the working sets.

**LOAD:** 75/side (HOLD — no push, this is a deload). **DELOAD: 3 sets as a pyramid — 8, then 6, then 8.**

**Why the pyramid** (W12D5 discovery): the user self-pyramided W12D5 to 10/6/6/8 and found **fewer reps per set = deeper, cleaner squats + no fear of overshooting the count.** The 6-rep middle set is the one to make the deepest and cleanest — treat it as the quality set. Sets 1 and 3 at 8 bookend it.

**Sharp cues only — spotlight was W10:**
- Bar high on the upper traps (NOT the neck), upper-back shelf squeezed
- **Brace like prepping for a punch** — deep belly breath at the top, HOLD through the descent, exhale on the drive up
- **Hips back AND down**, not just down
- 'Spread the floor' — screw the feet into the ground
- Knees track over the toes
- Sit in the bottom — same depth as W12
- Drive through the WHOLE foot

**Reflection:** does the 6-rep middle set feel like the cleanest, deepest one? If yes, that confirms the W12D5 pattern — W14 can build the pyramid out. Hold 75; the deload is not the week to chase depth-plus-load at once.

sets.name: Leg Press (DELOAD — 3 Sets, 3 Plates, Full ROM)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 3 plates/side (HOLD — no plate test this week). **DELOAD: 3 sets, NOT 4.** Chase DEPTH, not load.

**W12D5 framed this lift exactly right:** full ROM at 3 plates is 'a QL massage' — not hard on the leg muscles, but the deep knee-to-chest ROM works the weak core/QL at the bottom. The user's own call: **full ROM at lighter load is the way to go 'until the core muscles get untucked.'** That holds for a deload too — no 4-plate test.

- **Foot position: shoulder-width, mid-foot on the platform, slight toe-out** — set before loading
- **Press through the WHOLE foot** — heels grounded
- **Full ROM — 'knee to chest like frogger'** (W12D5), actively pushing to keep the lower back from lifting off the pad
- Slow descent (3s), no bounce at the bottom
- Don't lock out hard at the top — leave a hair of bend
- **Watch:** hips lifting off the pad at depth = too deep, back off. The hip lift IS the QL signal.
- **Breath:** in on the descent, out on the drive

sets.name: 🔆🆕 Bulgarian Split Squat (Spotlight — NEW Lift, Replaces the Walking Lunge)

exercises.equipment: bench + light DBs (realistic default) — Smith machine preferred if free (use smith-machine-bulgarian-split-squat)
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** Bodyweight or LIGHT DBs (10-15lb at the sides max). **DELOAD: 2 sets x 8-10 each leg. This is an INTRODUCTION — the load is not the point.**

**Why this lift, now** (W12D5 user request — see `docs/notes/functional-bulk/form-cues-2026.md` W13 legs note):
- The Bulgarian split squat **officially replaces the walking lunge** as the program's unilateral leg lift. Rear foot fixed = no travel = more controlled, a better single-leg builder, and gentler on the QL than a walking lunge. That control is exactly why it's a good deload introduction.
- **Equipment:** Smith machine preferred (`smith-machine-bulgarian-split-squat`, guided bar path) but it's 'always taken' here — the realistic default is a flat bench for the rear foot + light DBs held at the sides.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the pattern every rep (this is the whole lift this week):**
- **Rear foot up on the bench behind you**, laces down or toes tucked — whichever is comfortable
- **Take a long enough forward step that the front shin stays roughly VERTICAL at the bottom** — too short a stance and the knee crashes forward past the toes
- **Front knee tracks over the front foot** — not caving in
- **Lower until the rear knee nearly touches the floor** — controlled, no dropping
- **Drive up through the FRONT HEEL** — the front leg does the work; the rear leg is a kickstand for balance, not a pushing leg
- **Torso stays TALL** — slight forward lean is fine, but no folding over
- **2s descent, controlled** — own the bottom

**Reflection — the introduction question:** which is the limiter today — balance, or strength? If it's balance/coordination (very likely on rep 1 of a new lift), stay at bodyweight and just groove the pattern. If the pattern is already stable, light DBs are fine. Either way, **do NOT chase load on a new lift in a deload week** — clean reps on both legs is the entire goal. Note any side-to-side asymmetry; that's the data for W14.

**QL watch:** the Bulgarian is gentler on the QL than the walking lunge, but it's still unilateral hip work. If anything in the QL/psoas talks, drop to bodyweight or cut to 1 set — it's a new lift, no need to push it.

sets.notes: **🆕 First time the Bulgarian split squat is in the program.** It permanently replaces the walking lunge. Deload introduction — 2 sets, bodyweight or light DBs, form-only. The spotlight is the movement pattern; load is a W14+ conversation.

sets.name: Calf Raise (DELOAD — 2 Sets, Deep Heel-Drop + Slow Eccentric)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 3 plates/side, **double-leg, on the incline leg press machine.** **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.**

**Setup (corrected W12D5 — the workout used to describe this wrong):**
- **Balls of both feet on the BOTTOM EDGE of the platform, heels hanging off the edge.** No plate under the foot — the platform edge already gives the heel drop. This is a bottom-half-ROM movement.
- **Both feet on the platform** — double-leg (single-leg + slow was the W10D5 trap)

**Sharp cues — spotlight was W12:**
- **Full stretch at the bottom** — let the heels drop as far below the platform edge as they'll go
- **Drive to FULL plantarflexion at the top**
- **3-4s eccentric** — the slow descent is the stimulus, not more weight (W12D5: 'slow count is incredibly hard')
- **1s squeeze at the peak**, no bouncing
- **Breath:** continuous, don't hold

**LOAD:** 90 (HOLD — W11D5/W12D5 working weight at the corrected ankle placement). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.**

**Sharp cues — spotlight was W12:**
- **Ankles UNDER the pad** — the ankle bone is the contact point, NOT the mid-calf. The W7-W10 numbers (100-110) were on the wrong lever; 90 here is genuine hamstring work.
- **Settings: 5/m/0/1** (verify before loading)
- **Lap pad locked DOWN firmly** — loose pad = torso lift = lower-back work
- **Curl the heels toward the glutes — full ROM, full squeeze**
- **2s eccentric, 1s squeeze**
- **Don't let the torso lift forward** — stays anchored to the seat
- No bouncing out of the bottom

**LOAD:** 25lb plate (HOLD). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.** Dead slow with a 2s pause at the bottom.

**Sharp cues — spotlight was W10:**
- Pad just below the hip crease (NOT the upper thigh, NOT the stomach)
- **Hip hinge — a Romanian deadlift on the 45° bench, NOT a lumbar extension**
- Lower back NEUTRAL throughout
- At the bottom: stretch in the **hamstrings and glutes**, NOT the lower back
- Drive up by squeezing the glutes and hamstrings
- At the top: stop at neutral — past neutral = lumbar load
- Tempo: 3s eccentric, 2s pause, 1s drive

**W12D5 said extra rest between sets felt right** — keep that, even on a 2-set day.

sets.name: Cool-Down — 5 Min Recovery Block (Hip/QL-Biased for Legs Day)

exercises.notes: 30s each side. The QL/psoas release move again — bookend the session with it. Scissor the legs, reach overhead, lean away from the back leg.
45s each side. Deep hip-flexor + glute release — the long hold is what lets the QL/psoas let go (the W8 QL-spasm protocol move).
30s each side. Gentle twist for the QL — draw one knee across the body, opposite arm extended.
45s each side. Front leg + back leg at 90°. Hip-rotator decompression — pulls the QL/glute pattern out of the squat bracing.
60s slow breathing. Knees wide, arms extended forward. Parasympathetic shift before leaving the gym.

sets.notes: **5-min recovery block, permanent every-gym-day closing block (since W12D3).** On legs day it's biased toward hip/QL — the chains that take the most load from squats, leg press, and the new Bulgarian split squat. The cross-leg lateral lean opens it, child's pose closes it.

tips: **DELOAD WEEK — hold every load, cut every exercise a set.** Squat 3 sets (not 4), leg press 3 (not 4), leg curl + incline RDL + calf raise 2 each. If a lift feels easy, that's the design.

**🆕 Bulgarian split squat is the spotlight — and a NEW lift.** It permanently replaces the walking lunge. Introduce it form-only: bodyweight or light DBs, 2 sets x 8-10 each leg. Smith machine preferred (always taken) → bench + light DBs default.
**Squat HOLDS 75/side as a 3-set pyramid (8/6/8)** — W12D5 confirmed pyramiding gives cleaner depth than flat sets. Make the 6-rep middle set the deepest, cleanest one.
**Leg press: hold 3 plates, chase DEPTH.** W12D5 — full ROM at 3 plates is 'a QL massage,' the deep ROM works the weak core/QL. No 4-plate test on a deload.
**🆕 Cross-leg lateral lean added to the warmup** — W12D5 user discovery for releasing a tight QL/psoas. Also bookends the cool-down.
**QL/psoas watchpoint.** W12D5 came in tight from the KB day. Use the warmup cross-leg lean, and abort any lift that twinges.
**Cool-down stays — 5-min block, hip/QL-biased on legs day.**
Rotation plan: W14 cycles back to squat + incline RDL spotlights.
Rest 3 min squats, 2 min leg press, 90s Bulgarian + isolations.
formPhilosophy: Deload week — hold loads, cut sets. The squat pyramid and the leg-press depth focus are both about quality at held load. The Bulgarian split squat enters the program as a NEW lift, introduced the right way: form first, load later. A deload is the ideal week to learn a new movement pattern with nothing heavy at stake. Building decades, not weeks.

name: W13 Morning Flow — Bodyweight Spine + Hip (All Days, Deload-Week Variety)
description: 15 min bodyweight floor flow — **universal W13 morning flow, used every training day** (Mon push / Tue KB / Wed pull / Thu KB / Fri legs). Same three-part shape as W11/W12 with rotated moves for variety. Morning flows are already low-fatigue, so they stay unchanged in intensity through the deload — only the move selection rotates. Three-part: spine primer → bodyweight work → deep stretch arc. Day-specific therapeutic add-on at the end (push = pec-minor protocol; pull = dead hang + scap pull; legs = pigeon + supine twist + the new cross-leg lateral lean; KB days skip — banded prep covers it).

sets.name: Primer — Wake the Spine (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Slow, full ROM each direction. Spine wave wakes the segment-by-segment articulation.
30s each side. Thoracic rotation + shoulder opener — reach a little further each week.
Gentle spinal extension — counters the all-day desk flexion. **Replaces W12's puppy-pose** in the primer; the extension wakes the back line.

sets.notes: Spine extension swaps in for the lat-stretch opener — variety on the same wake-the-spine intent.

sets.name: Work Block — Bodyweight Activation (~5 min)

exercises.notes: Slow walkouts — hamstring load on the way out, plank on the way in. **Rotates back in** (W11 had it) — full-body warm in one shape.
45s alternating. Plank with opposite arm + leg extension — anti-rotation. **Rotates back in** — different anti-rotation pattern than W12's shoulder taps.
45s alternating. From down-dog, draw one knee toward the forehead, hold a beat, switch. **Stays** — the core+hip+shoulder integration earns its place.
45s slow. **Stays** — durable hip opener under plank load.
30s each side. **Stays** — the windmill on the floor, the lateral-hinge cue translation.

sets.notes: Inchworm + bird-dog rotate back in for variety. Anti-rotation gets a different shape than W12.

sets.name: Yoga Flow — Deep Stretch Arc (~7 min)

exercises.notes: 30s each side. **Stays every week** — signature spine move.
30s each direction. Quadruped hip abduction — slow, full ROM.
30s each side. Hip flexor + adductor + back-leg quad. Drop to elbows if accessible. **Stays** — durable hip opener.
Bodyweight, slow. **Stays every week** — mirrors the Tuesday loaded cossack.
30s each side. Flowing entry into pigeon. **Replaces W12's static pigeon** — the flowing version for variety; the hip release still lands.
60s slow pulses. Adductor + hip opener. **Rotates back in** (W11 had it) — gentle pulsing release.

sets.notes: Continuous arc — flow between shapes, breathe deeply. Anchors stay (scorpion, cossack, lizard); pigeon + adductor work rotate for variety.

sets.name: 📍 Day-Specific Add-On — Pick by Today's Training (~3-5 min)

exercises.notes: **📍 PUSH DAYS — KEY PROTOCOL MOVE.** 30s each at LOW / MID / HIGH elbow positions. Arm bent 90°, forearm on the door frame, step through and rotate the torso AWAY. Desk work continuously re-tightens pec minor — daily.
**📍 PUSH DAYS** — active side of the pec-minor fix. Lower trap + rotator cuff.
**📍 PUSH DAYS** — serratus activation, helps unstick a tight pec minor.
**📍 PUSH DAYS (optional)** — slow rotator cuff work. Add if the shoulder feels off — worth doing this week with the right-shoulder cluster active.
**📍 PUSH DAYS (optional)** — pins the shoulder blades in the corrected position.
**📍 PULL DAYS** — 25s. Decompress the shoulders, warm the grip.
**📍 PULL DAYS** — scapulae retracting only, no elbow bend. Crisp scap engagement before loaded pulls.

**📍 LEGS DAYS — 🆕 NEW (W12D5 user discovery).** 30s each side. Scissor one foot over the other, reach the same-side arm overhead, lean away from the back leg — QL/psoas release. Do this before heading to the gym; it's also in the legs-workout warmup.
**📍 LEGS DAYS — W8 QL-SPASM PROTOCOL.** 90s each side. The long hold is what lets the QL/psoas release. **Non-negotiable on legs day.**
**📍 LEGS DAYS** — 60s each side. Gentle twist for the QL. Lacrosse-ball QL roll above the iliac crest beforehand if available.

sets.notes: **Pick today's day-specific protocol — push / pull / legs.** ~3-5 min total. KB days (Tue/Thu) skip this block — the in-workout banded prep covers the equivalent.

tips: **Same shape as W11/W12, rotated moves.** Morning flows are low-fatigue — they stay unchanged in intensity through the deload, only the move selection rotates.
**15 min main flow + 3-5 min day-specific add-on** — universal across W13 (Mon push / Tue KB / Wed pull / Thu KB / Fri legs).
**Scorpion + cossack BW + lizard pose stay every week** — durable spine + hip + adductor anchors.
Cobra, inchworm, bird-dog, mountain-to-pigeon, frog pulses rotate in this week for variety.
**🆕 Legs-day add-on now includes the standing cross-leg lateral lean** — the W12D5 QL/psoas release move, now official.
**Day-specific add-on at the end** — push days do the pec-minor protocol, pull days do dead hang + scap pull, legs days do the cross-leg lean + 90s pigeon + supine twist. KB days skip the add-on.
If short on time: scorpion + lizard + cossack BW + mountain-to-pigeon, plus today's day-specific add-on.
formPhilosophy: Same as W11/W12 — variety means different shapes hitting the same patterns. Scorpion-family spine work + hip openers + adductor stretches. Morning flows don't deload (they're already low-fatigue); they just rotate moves to stay interesting. Designed for home, structured around the in-workout banded prep's logic.

name: Week 13 Pull — DELOAD (Spotlight: Bicep Curl + High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch)
description: **DELOAD WEEK.** Same deload as W13 push — W10-W12 were 3 hard weeks, fatigue signals are firing, the locked 3-hard/1-deload cadence puts the cut here. **HOLD all loads, CUT volume ~60-70%.** Spotlights rotate to the **curl + crunch finishers** (bicep curl, high cable curl, cable crunch) — the lightest lifts of the day, ideal deload spotlights. **Trap-3 raise replaces the behind-back shrug block** this week (planned rotation) — the shrugs gave a strong first signal W12D3 ('crackly pop, then everything aligned'); trap-3 raises isolate the lower trap even more directly at a lighter load. **W12D3 takeaways baked in:** pull-ups all-slow on straight bar 8/6/6/5 ('felt great for the first time in a long time'); chest-supported row 35 'good and easy'; DB row held form at 80; reverse fly 30° reverse-incline variant clicked; face pull cue change was transformative ('like another exercise, neck feels less engaged'); high cable curl ramped to 170.

dayOverview.theme: DELOAD — hold every load, cut every exercise a set. The curl + crunch finishers are the spotlight because they're the lightest work of the day; a deload is the right week to drill cues with nothing heavy competing. Trap-3 raise replaces the behind-back shrug block on the planned rotation — same trap-chain intent, lower-trap isolation, even lighter load. Watch the right-shoulder cluster: if it's worse than W13D1 push, that's the structural signal — PT eval.

spotlights.why: The bicep curl is the W13 spotlight (tracker says 'preacher curl' — incline curl is the de-facto lift here, preacher bench is always taken). W12D3 had BOTH sides weak with 8+2 assisted on set 3 — but the user flagged they skipped the left-first protocol and there was cumulative fatigue before it. A deload puts the curl on fresher legs: drill left-first, strict reps, full stretch + 2s pause. The reflection question: do the numbers recover when the fatigue ahead of them is cut?

W12D3 ramped to 170 ('approaching working weight; 180 would be hard but doable'). Deload HOLDS 170 — drill the upper-arm-parallel-and-fixed setup instead of chasing 180. The pec-stabilizes-the-arm sensation the user noted is positional and fine.

Held at 130 (W12D3 done without comment). Deload drills the cue that makes it an ab lift and not a hip-flexor lift: hips LOCKED, spine flexes, crunch from the abs only. Light, controlled, full attention.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 chest-supported row + lat pulldown · W12 face pull + reverse fly · W13 bicep curl + high cable curl + cable crunch (current, DELOAD) · W14 cycle back to pull-up + DB row.
watchpoints: **This is a DELOAD — hold loads, cut sets, resist pushing.** Pull-ups stay 6-8 but 3 sets not 4. DB row drops to 2 sets at 70 only (skip the 60 and 80). Lat pulldown, face pull → 2 sets. If a lift feels easy, that's the deload working.

**Right shoulder symptom cluster — cross-check against W13D1 push.** If the line-of-sensation / grip-triggered pain is WORSE than it was on push day, that flips the working hypothesis from functional to structural — **book a PT eval, don't wait.** If it's the same or better, the deload + trap-chain work is doing its job.

**Trap-3 raise replaces the behind-back shrug block** (planned rotation). The shrugs got a strong W12D3 signal; trap-3 raises hit the lower trap even more directly. Start VERY light — 5-10lb. The lower trap is small and fatigues fast. Same report-back: does it relieve or aggravate the shoulder cluster?

**Left bicep weaker than right** (W10D3 + W11D3). W12D3 both sides were weak but the user skipped the left-first protocol. This week: **left first, every set.** Strict reps only — no assist past asymmetry.
**DB row holds the MIDDLE of the pyramid (70), not the top (80).** Skipping the heavy 80 set IS the deload for this lift — don't substitute it back in.
Cool-down stays — 5-min block, permanent every gym day.

exercises.notes: Scap activation. Squeeze shoulder blades together, soft elbow.
Spine warm — slow, full ROM each direction.
Active hang, not collapsed — engage lats, pull shoulders DOWN away from the ears even at full extension.

**LOAD:** Bodyweight. **DELOAD: 3 sets, NOT 4.** Reps stay 6-8 (W12D3's all-slow straight-bar 8/6/6/5 was clean — 'felt great for the first time in a long time').

**Straight bar is fine** — W12D3 the user chose it deliberately ('wanted to get basics working') and it worked well. Stick with straight bar, or mix grips if preferred; either way:
- Active hang start, not collapsed
- Pull elbows DOWN to ribs, NOT chin to bar
- Chest leads to the bar at the top
- Eyes ahead, head neutral — no chin tucking
- **Slow eccentric, ~2-3s**, return to an active hang — the all-slow tempo was the W12D3 win
- Stop the set when the rep stops being clean — never kip

**Right shoulder watch:** W11D3 the front of the right shoulder lit up on a wide grip. If it talks, drop to a close or neutral grip set.

sets.name: 🔆 Station 2: DB Rack / Bench Block (Bicep Curl Spotlight + Chest-Supported Row + DB Row + Reverse Fly — DELOAD)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 35lb (HOLD — W12D3 had 35 available and 'good and easy'). **DELOAD: 2 sets.** Hold 35 — do not push to 40 even though W12 said it had headroom; that's a W14 decision.
- **Chest stays GLUED to the bench** — if it lifts off, too heavy
- Pull the elbow toward the HIP, not the armpit
- Both elbows pull together — asymmetric = slow down
- 2s eccentric, 1s squeeze, no DB slam at the bottom
