**LOAD:** 70lb (HOLD — the MIDDLE of the W12 pyramid). **DELOAD: 2 sets at 70 only — skip the 60 warmup set AND the 80 top set.** Dropping the heavy 80 is the deload for this lift; do not add it back.
- Chest-supported on the incline bench
- Pull the elbow toward the HIP, not the armpit
- Don't rotate the torso
- Slow 2s descent, 1s squeeze
- 70 should feel controlled and clean — W12D3 held form even at 80, so 70 on a deload is honest practice reps, not a grind

**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD). **DELOAD: 2 sets.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Sit on the incline bench (~45-60°)**, back against the bench, DBs hanging at the sides — this is the de-facto curl (preacher bench is always taken; preacher at 12.5/side is the swap if it's somehow free)
- **Left arm first, every set** — W10D3 + W11D3 showed the left weaker; W12D3 the user skipped left-first and both sides went weak. The weak side gets the freshest reps. This week, no excuse — the deload cut the fatigue ahead of this lift.
- **Full stretch at the bottom — pause 2s** (the user's own cue from W11D3)
- **Curl smoothly to a peak squeeze**, no swing
- **2-3s eccentric** — fight the long-head stretch
- **Stop each arm at strict failure** — do NOT assist past the asymmetry. A clean 8 beats 8+2 assisted.

**Reflection — the deload question:** W12D3 both sides struggled (8+2 assisted), possibly from cumulative fatigue. This week the volume ahead of the curl is cut. Do the numbers recover? If left hits a clean 8-10 today, fatigue-ordering was the W12 explanation. If it's still weak fresh, the curl placement may need to move earlier in W14.

**LOAD:** 20lb (HOLD — 22.5 'doesn't exist at this gym,' confirmed). **DELOAD: 2 sets.**
- **30° reverse-incline (chest-down on the incline bench)** — W12D3 locked this as the default form ('WAY better than the hunched-over version'). Set the bench to ~30°.
- DBs hanging fully extended at start, **soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED throughout**
- **Lead with the elbow, hand passive** — same pattern as the lateral raise
- **Wings out and slightly UP to shoulder height** — sweep, don't lift
- **2s eccentric, 1s squeeze** — no swing, no momentum

sets.notes: **One trip to the DB rack covers all 4 lifts.** Circuit or straight sets — your call. Bench at incline ~30° for the rows + incline curl AND the reverse fly (30° reverse-incline). 2 rounds, not 3 — this is the deload.

exercises.equipment: regular lat pulldown (default, 140) — dual pulley fallback 70/side ≈ 140 if the machine is occupied
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 140 (HOLD — W12D3 confirmed clean on the regular machine). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.**
- **Wide grip near the bend** — more lat isolation, less bicep
- **Slight backward lean ~15°, NO further** — more = it becomes a row
- **Pull the bar to the UPPER CHEST — NEVER behind the neck**
- 'Lats squeezing oranges into the back pockets' at the bottom
- 1s pause at the contraction, 2s controlled return to a full overhead stretch
- Knees pinned firmly under the pad

sets.name: Station 4: Cable Column — Face Pull (DELOAD — 2 Sets)

exercises.equipment: cable column (mid/upper-chest height + rope)
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 42.5 (HOLD). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.** Keep the W12D3 cue protocol — that's the lift now, the user said it 'felt like another exercise' and 'neck feels less engaged.'
- **Pull the rope to face level** — not to the chest
- **ELBOWS HIGH (above the shoulders)** — the make-or-break cue; if they drop, the rear delt offloads
- **External rotate at the top — knuckles point UP, like a double-biceps pose.** This is why the lift exists.
- **1s peak hold** at the contracted position — count it
- **Slow controlled return, 2s** — let the cable pull the arms back

**Setup: pick the cable column you'll finish the workout on** — the curl + crunch finisher lives at the same column. Slide the rope to the high attachment after this.

sets.name: 🔆 Station 5: Cable Column Finisher — High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch (Spotlight, DELOAD)

exercises.equipment: cable column (high attachment) — same column as face pull
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 170 (HOLD — W12D3 actual working weight). **DELOAD: 1 working set per arm is the prescription** — treat the 2nd round as optional, stop at 1/arm if it feels complete. Do NOT push to 180; that's a post-deload decision.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the setup every rep:**
- **Single D-handle, one arm at a time** (the W12D3 setup that 'felt nice')
- **Stand sideways, arm out at shoulder level**
- **Upper arm parallel to the floor, FIXED** — if the elbow drops, it becomes an ordinary curl. The fixed upper arm is the entire point of the high-cable variant.
- **Curl the hand to the ear**, peak squeeze
- **Slow eccentric, no bounce at the start**
- The pec-stabilizes-the-arm sensation the user noted W12D3 is positional and normal — the upper arm parallel at shoulder level recruits the pec to hold position. Not a sign anything's wrong.

**Reflection:** with the upper arm truly fixed, does 170 feel like honest bicep work, or is the shoulder/pec doing the holding job? On a deload, get this dialed at 170 before W14 considers 180.

exercises.equipment: cable column (high attachment + rope)
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 130 (HOLD — W12D3 done without comment). **DELOAD: 2 sets.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the cue that makes this an ab lift:**
- **High attachment + rope** (swap from the D-handle — same column)
- **Kneel under the high cable**
- **HIPS LOCKED** — the single most important cue. The hips do not move.
- **Crunch from the abs ONLY** — the spine flexes, the hips stay put. Hips moving = hip-flexor work, not abs. If you feel it in the hip flexors, you've lost the cue.
- **Chest toward the thighs**, hold the crunch 1 second
- **Slow eccentric**

**Reflection:** at the bottom of each rep, is it the abs that flexed the spine, or did the hips fold? Locking the hips is the whole lift.

sets.notes: **The spotlight block — lightest work of the day, drilled deep.** High cable curl: 1 working set per arm (deload). Cable crunch: 2 sets. Same cable column as the face pull.

sets.name: 🆕 Station 6: Trap-3 Raise (Replaces Behind-Back Shrug Block — Planned Rotation)

exercises.equipment: incline bench + light DBs
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 5-10lb DBs — start at 5. The lower trap is small and fatigues fast; 5-10lb is genuinely plenty. 3 sets.

**Why this lift, this week** (continues the W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion — see `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`):
- Behind-back shrugs got a strong signal W12D3 ('crackly pop, then everything aligned'). **Trap-3 raise rotates in as planned** — it isolates the lower trap ('trap 3') even more directly than the shrug, at a much lighter load. The lower trap is the underused side of the upper-trap-dominant pattern.
- The shrugs and trap-3 raises alternate across pull days — don't do both the same day.

**Cues (drill every rep):**
- **Lie face-down on an incline bench (~30-45°)**, a light DB in each hand
- **Arms hang toward the floor, thumbs pointing UP** (external rotation) — the thumbs-up is non-negotiable, it sets the lower-trap line
- **Lift the arms into a Y — at ~135° from the torso**, NOT straight overhead. Raise until the arms are in line with the ears or slightly above.
- **Lift from the SHOULDER BLADES, not the hands** — the cue is the scapula sliding down-and-in, the arms just go along for the ride
- **1-2s hold at the top**, slow eccentric back to neutral
- No momentum, no bouncing off the bottom

**What to watch (REPORT BACK):** does this relieve the right-shoulder cluster the way the W12D3 shrugs did? Same hypothesis test — relief = functional pattern (keep going), aggravation = structural (PT eval). Also note whether the lower trap fatigues fast — if 5lb is hard by rep 8, that IS the lower trap working (it should feel disproportionately weak vs. its size).

sets.notes: Closing trap-chain block at the incline bench. 3 sets, very light load, the cue is the whole lift. ~4 min including rest. Alternates with behind-back shrugs across pull days — trap-3 this week, shrugs return W14.

sets.name: Cool-Down — 5 Min Fascia/Recovery Block (Permanent Every-Gym-Day Closing Block)

exercises.notes: 30s each arm at MID elbow position. Pec minor lengthen — the antagonist to the upper-trap chain loaded all session.
Passive hang from the pull-up bar. **Don't engage** — let the shoulders unpack and the spine decompress.
30s each side on the mat. Draw one knee across the body, opposite arm extended. Breathe into the rotated rib cage.
45s each side. Front leg + back leg at 90°. Sink the hips toward the floor — hip-rotator decompression.
60s slow breathing. Knees wide, arms extended forward. Breathe into the back ribs — parasympathetic shift before leaving the gym.

sets.notes: **5 minutes of recovery work, non-negotiable closing block (permanent since W12D3).** On a deload week this is the connective-tissue catch-up window — give it the full 5 minutes.

tips: **DELOAD WEEK — hold every load, cut every exercise a set.** Pull-ups 3 sets (not 4), DB row 2 sets at 70 only (skip 60 and 80), lat pulldown + face pull 2 sets, high cable curl 1 set/arm. If a lift feels easy, that's the design.
**Spotlight: the curl + crunch finishers** (bicep curl, high cable curl, cable crunch) — the lightest work of the day, drilled deep on a week with nothing heavy competing.

**Bicep curl: LEFT FIRST, every set, strict reps.** W12D3 the user skipped left-first and both sides went weak. The deload cut the fatigue ahead of the curl — the reflection question is whether the numbers recover.

**Trap-3 raise replaces the shrug block** (planned rotation). VERY light (5-10lb), thumbs up, lift from the shoulder blades, arms in a Y at 135°. Report whether it relieves or aggravates the right shoulder.
**Right shoulder cluster — cross-check vs. W13D1 push.** Worse than push day = structural signal, book a PT eval. Same or better = the deload + trap work is helping.
**Face pull holds 42.5 + the W12D3 cue protocol** — elbows high, external rotation, 1s hold. The cue is the lift, not the load.
**Cool-down stays — 5-min block, permanent every gym day.**
Rotation plan: W14 cycles back to pull-up + DB row; behind-back shrugs return W14 (alternating with trap-3 raise).
Rest 2.5 min pull-ups, 90s DB block, 2 min lat pulldown, 90s face pull, 60s finisher, 60s trap-3 block.
formPhilosophy: Deload week — hold loads, cut sets, let connective tissue catch up. The curl/crunch spotlight and the trap-3 raise are both light by nature, which makes this the right week to drill their cues with full attention. The shoulder-cluster cross-check against push day is the most important data point of the week. Building decades, not weeks.

name: Week 13 Push — DELOAD (Spotlight: Cable Column Block — Lat Raise + Tri Ext + Straight-Arm Pulldown)
description: **DELOAD WEEK.** W10/W11/W12 were 3 hard weeks; W12 also added new lifts (behind-back shrugs, cool-down block). Fatigue signals are firing: the new right-shoulder symptom cluster (W12D1 — 'line from hand to shoulder,' grip-triggered shoulder pain), universal tightness, multiple soft-tissue patterns. Per the locked 3-hard/1-deload cadence, W13 is a deload. **HOLD all loads, CUT volume ~60-70%** — drop a set per exercise. Spotlights rotate to the **cable column block** (cable lat raise + incline overhead tri ext + straight-arm pulldown) — the lightest lifts of the day, perfect for a deload where the cue is the only thing being trained. **NEW: farmer carry** joins as a closing block (W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion outcome — loaded carry trains the same packed-shoulder pattern as the shrugs). **W12D1 takeaways baked in:** chest press 165 x 10 x 3 ran clean at the 3rd-station placement ('way easier when this is third'); incline DB went to 45 cleanly ('weirdly good no pain'); Kinesis fly 8 'stretching and relaxing everything.'

dayOverview.theme: DELOAD — first deload since W9. Motivated by W10-W12 being 3 hard weeks plus W12's new lifts, and by the W12D1 fatigue cluster: the new right-shoulder symptom pattern (line-of-sensation + grip-triggered pain), universal tightness, multiple soft-tissue patterns. HOLD every load, cut every exercise by a set. The cable column block is the spotlight precisely because it's the lightest work of the day — a deload is the right time to drill cues with nothing else competing. NEW farmer carry closing block extends the trap-chain work the shrugs started.

spotlights.why: W12D1 surfaced a real cue interaction — 'if I lead with elbow and focus on back, I feel it in the elbow even tho not using it at all.' A deload is the week to untangle that: drill lead-with-elbow WITHOUT letting the elbow take load, soft-fixed elbow, stop at shoulder height. Light load, full attention on the path.

W12D1 locked cable column 70 as the working number (first cable-column data point — W10/W11 were on Kinesis). Deload drills the setup: kneel facing away, torso-forward 'incline,' elbows pinned, full overhead stretch. Hold 70, drill the elbow pin.
Cable column ~100 working number. The lift most likely to drift into a lat-pulldown — drill the fixed slight-elbow-bend + 'pull to thighs not past' + lat squeeze. Deload load, deep cue.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 chest press machine · W12 lateral raise + Kinesis cable fly · W13 cable column block — lat raise + tri ext + straight-arm pulldown (current, DELOAD) · W14 cycle back to push press + incline DB to refresh.
watchpoints: **This is a DELOAD — resist the urge to push.** Loads HOLD at W12 numbers; every exercise drops a set. If a set feels easy, that is the deload working as designed — do NOT add reps or weight. The point is to let connective tissue catch up to muscle (it lags 6-8 weeks).

**Right shoulder symptom cluster (W12D1) is the headline watchpoint.** 'Line from hand to shoulder' at 90°, raising the arm to 90° getting hard, squeezing the right hand triggering shoulder weakness/pain. The deload + cool-down + shrug/carry work is the intervention. **If the cluster is WORSE by W13D3 pull day, that flips the hypothesis from functional to structural — book a PT eval.** Don't escalate push press load until it settles.
**Chest press cut to 2 sets at 165** — W12D1 confirmed 165 x 10 x 3 ran clean at the 3rd-station slot; deload trims to 2. Hold 165, do NOT chase a 3rd set.
**Incline DB holds 45** — W12D1 went to 45 'weirdly good no pain.' Deload: 2 sets, hold 45. Drop to 40 only if the right shoulder is loud on set 1.

**NEW: farmer carry closing block** — extends the W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion. The packed-shoulder loaded carry trains the same down-and-back trap pattern as the behind-back shrugs. Introduce light: 2 walks, 40-50lb DBs. Cue is 'pack DOWN not up.'
Yuri band red warmup non-negotiable. Doorframe pec stretch in the morning flow add-on.

exercises.notes: Slow, big circles. Wake up scap glide before loading.
Full sequence with red band. Non-negotiable on push day — extra important this week with the right-shoulder symptom cluster active.
Ribs down, glutes tight, body in one line. Slow eccentric, 1s pause with chest 1-2" off the floor. (Deload: 10, not 12.)

sets.name: Station 1: DB Rack — Push Press + Lateral Raise (DELOAD — 2 Sets Each)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 40/side (HOLD — W12D1 hit 40 x 10/10/8). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.**
- **Feet parallel, hip-width** — locked since W7
- **Ribs DOWN before the dip** — W12D1 flagged rib flare creeping in on set 2; on a 2-set day there is no excuse for it, catch it on set 1
- **Pack DOWN, not up** — shoulder blades into the back pockets, neck long. If you have to shrug up to lock out, the load is too heavy *for the cue* — that check matters more than ever with the shoulder cluster active.
- Shallow dip ~3-4", vertical DB path, head pokes through at the top
- Stop the set the moment rib position or the packed shoulder breaks

**LOAD:** 15lb (HOLD — W12D1 hit 15 x 20 x 2, slow eccentric 'super hard'). **DELOAD: 2 sets, ~18 reps** (slightly trimmed from 20).
- **Lead with elbow, hand passive** — wrist follows, no wrist break
- **Soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED** through the rep
- **Stop at shoulder height — never higher** (past = traps eat the rep)
- **2s eccentric** — slow descent is the stimulus
- **Scap-reset between sets** — roll shoulders, engage/disengage scapula
- Massage the right pec between sets if the shoulder talks

sets.name: Station 2: DB Rack — Incline Bench (DELOAD — 2 Sets)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 45lb pair (HOLD — W12D1 hit 45 x 10 x 3, 'felt weirdly good no pain'). **DELOAD: 2 sets.** Drop to 40 only if the right shoulder is loud on set 1.
- **Bench at 30°**, NOT 45° — forearm stack is automatic at 30°
- **Shoulders packed back AND down** — pin scaps into the bench
- Elbows ~45° from torso
- 1s pause at the bottom, no DB clack at the top
- This is a deload — 45 should feel controlled, not a grind. If it grinds, the right shoulder is telling you something — drop to 40.

sets.name: Station 3: Chest Press Machine (DELOAD — 2 Sets at 165)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 165 (HOLD — W12D1 ran 165 x 10 x 3 clean at this 3rd-station placement, 'way easier when this is third'). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.** Do not chase a 3rd set just because 165 felt good last week — the deload is the cut.
- **Seat height: handles align with mid-chest / nipple line**, set BEFORE sitting
- **Shoulders pinned BACK against the back pad** — the foundation cue
- **3s eccentric** — count it
- **Press to almost extension** — leave a hair of bend
- Massage out the right pec between sets — pre-empt the referral

**LOAD:** Kinesis 8 (HOLD — W12D1 'felt great, stretching and relaxing everything'). **DELOAD: 2 sets.** This is the lift that loosens the right shoulder — protect it even on a deload.
- **Slight forward hinge at hips** — chest leads, not head
- **Soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED throughout** — not a press, not a curl
- **"Hug a tree"** — squeeze from the chest, not the arms
- **Slow eccentric (~3s)** — let the cables pull the arms back for the deep pec stretch
- **Stretch is the stimulus** — W12D1 said the shoulder felt good at the stretch; that's the cue

sets.name: 🔆 Station 5: Cable Column Block — Lat Raise → Tri Ext → Straight-Arm Pulldown (Spotlight, DELOAD — 2 Rounds)

exercises.equipment: cable column (default — W12D1 setup) — Kinesis 3 fallback if column is occupied
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column ~30-40lb (HOLD — form-first range). **DELOAD: 2 rounds, one arm at a time.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the W12D1 cue interaction every rep:**
- W12D1 observation: **'if I lead with elbow and focus on back, I feel it in the elbow even tho not using it at all.'** That sensation means the elbow is becoming a load path it shouldn't be.
- **Lead with the elbow as a DIRECTION, not a lever** — the elbow points the way the arm travels; it does not bend, extend, or grip. If you feel the elbow *working*, you've turned a direction cue into a joint action. Reset: soft elbow, fixed angle, let the medial delt lift the whole arm as one unit.
- **Soft elbow ~10°, FIXED throughout** — the angle never changes from start to top
- **Stand sideways to the column**, working arm farther from the stack
- **Stop at shoulder height — higher = traps**
- **Fight the cable on the way down**, no snap-back, 2s eccentric
- **Massage the right pec between sets** — kills the crackle
- **Skip 'run line behind body' cue** — doesn't apply at this gym's geometry

**Reflection:** by round 2, is the elbow quiet (just pointing) or is it still 'feeling' the rep? If it's still feeling it, slow down and lighten — the deload is the week to get this clean.

exercises.equipment: cable column kneeling (default — W12D1 working number) — Kinesis 5 fallback if column is occupied
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 70 (HOLD — W12D1 locked 70 as the working number; 50 was too easy). **DELOAD: 2 rounds.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the setup every rep:**
- **Kneel facing AWAY from the high pulley**, rope overhead
- **Torso slightly forward — that's the 'incline.'** It biases the long head; without the forward lean it's an ordinary pressdown.
- **Elbows PINNED** — only the forearms move. The elbow is the hinge and nothing else. If the elbows drift forward or flare, the long head offloads.
- **Hands shoulder-width on the rope** (long-head emphasis)
- **Full stretch at the bottom** — let the rope pull the hands behind the head
- **Stop just shy of lockout**, slow eccentric

**Reflection:** at round 2, are the elbows still pinned in the same spot they started, or have they crept forward? The pin is the lift.

exercises.equipment: cable column (default — W12D1 setup) — Kinesis 6 ≈ cable column 100 fallback
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 100 (HOLD — W12D1 working number, done clean 'no specifics needed'). **DELOAD: 2 rounds.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the cues that keep this from becoming a lat pulldown:**
- **Stand a step back, hinge ~15° at the hips** — fixed hinge, the torso doesn't pump
- **Slight elbow bend (~5°), FIXED throughout** — perfectly straight arms = elbow risk; bending arms = it becomes a pulldown. The elbow holds ONE angle the whole set.
- **Pull the bar to the thighs, NOT past** — past the thighs the shoulders roll forward and the lats switch off
- **Squeeze the lats hard at the bottom** — 'oranges into the back pockets'
- **Slow controlled return to a full overhead stretch** — the stretch at the top is half the lift

**Reflection:** does it feel like a straight-arm lat movement, or has the elbow started bending to help? If the elbow is bending, that's the lat tapping out — stop the set.

sets.notes: **The spotlight block — and the lightest work of the day, which is exactly why it's the deload spotlight.** Nothing here is heavy; the entire training effect this week is the quality of the cue. One trip to the cable column covers all three (slide the attachment between exercises). 2 rounds, not 3.

sets.name: 🆕 Station 6: Farmer Carry (NEW — W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion outcome)

exercises.equipment: DB rack
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 40-50lb DBs — start at 40, this is an introduction. **2 walks of ~30-45s.**

**Why this lift, now** (W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion — see `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`):
- The behind-back shrugs gave a strong first signal W12D3 ('crackly pop, then everything aligned'). Farmer carry trains the **same packed-shoulder pattern** under a different stimulus — the traps engage tonically to hold the shoulder packed while you walk.
- It directly drills the **'pack DOWN, not up'** correction the user flagged W12D1.

**Cues (drill every step):**
- **Stand TALL** — ribs down, crown of the head reaching up
- **Shoulders pulled DOWN and BACK** — into the back pockets, NEVER shrugged toward the ears. This is the whole point of the lift. The DBs will try to pull the shoulders up; resist that the entire walk.
- **Neck long, eyes forward** — no chin poke
- **Walk a straight line at a deliberate pace**, breathing steadily — don't rush
- **Grip firm but not death-clenched** — note whether a hard right-hand squeeze provokes the shoulder (W12D1 grip→shoulder coupling watch)

**What to watch (REPORT BACK):** does keeping the shoulder packed under the carry feel like it RELIEVES the right-shoulder cluster (like the shrugs did), or aggravate it? Same hypothesis test as the shrugs — relief = functional pattern, aggravation = structural, escalate to PT.

**DELOAD note:** keep this genuinely light — 2 walks, 40lb. It's a new lift in a deload week; build the cue, the load comes later.

sets.notes: Quick closing block at the DB rack on the way to the cool-down. 2 walks, ~30-45s each, light load. Focus is the packed-shoulder posture, not the weight. Total ~3 min including rest.

sets.name: Cool-Down — 5 Min Fascia/Recovery Block (Permanent Every-Gym-Day Closing Block)

exercises.notes: 30s each arm at MID elbow position. Arm bent 90°, forearm on the door frame, step through + rotate the torso AWAY. Pec minor lengthen — the antagonist to the upper-trap chain that's been loaded all session.
Passive hang from the pull-up bar. **Don't engage** — let the shoulders unpack and the spine decompress. Especially valuable this week for the right-shoulder cluster.
30s each side on the mat. Lie back, draw one knee across the body, opposite arm extended. Breathe into the rib cage on the rotated side.
45s each side. Front leg + back leg at 90°. Sink hips toward the floor — hip-rotator decompression.
60s slow breathing. Knees wide, big toes touching, arms extended forward. Breathe into the back ribs — parasympathetic shift before leaving the gym.

sets.notes: **5 minutes of recovery work, non-negotiable closing block (permanent since W12D3).** On a deload week this block matters even more — it's the connective-tissue-catch-up window. Goes after the last lift, before walking out.

tips: **DELOAD WEEK — hold every load, cut every exercise a set.** First deload since W9. If something feels easy, that's the design. Do not add reps or weight.

**Spotlight: the cable column block** (cable lat raise + incline overhead tri ext + straight-arm pulldown) — the lightest work of the day, drilled deep because a deload has nothing else competing for attention.
**Cable lat raise: 'lead with elbow' is a DIRECTION, not a joint action.** If you feel the elbow working, you've corrupted the cue (W12D1 observation). Reset to a fixed soft elbow.
**Right shoulder symptom cluster is the headline watchpoint.** If it's worse by W13D3 pull day, the hypothesis flips to structural — book a PT eval.
**Chest press 2 sets at 165, incline DB 2 sets at 45** — both held from W12D1's clean numbers. Deload trims the volume, not the load.
**Pack DOWN, not up** — push press, incline DB, chest press, lateral raise, farmer carry all carry the cue. If you must shrug up to finish a rep, the load is too heavy for the cue — drop it.

**NEW: farmer carry closing block** — 2 walks, 40-50lb DBs, 'tall posture, packed shoulders, breathe.' Extends the trap-chain work the shrugs started. Report whether it relieves or aggravates the right shoulder.
**Cool-down stays — 5-min block, permanent every gym day.** On a deload it's the connective-tissue catch-up window.
Yuri band red warmup non-negotiable. Doorframe pec stretch in the morning flow add-on.
Rotation plan: W14 cycles back to push press + incline DB to refresh those cues.
Rest 2 min DB rack work, 2.5 min chest press, 75s cables + carry.
formPhilosophy: Deload week — the first since W9, locked in by the 3-hard/1-deload cadence. The training effect this week is NOT load; it's letting connective tissue catch up to muscle (it lags 6-8 weeks) and drilling cues with the volume turned down. The cable column spotlight, the cool-down, and the new farmer carry all serve recovery and pattern quality. Building decades, not weeks.

name: Week 2 KB Conditioning
description: Thursday = refine what you learned Tuesday + your requested moves. Skill block first (TGU + windmill practice), then your picks (overhead lunges + Simba lift), then a short complex to finish. Still a real workout — just with more brain.

exercises.notes: Loosen up fast
Easy swings to prime the hinge
PRACTICE — this is Tuesday's new move. Go light (15lb or less). Focus on left side catching up to right. Slow is smooth, smooth is fast.
You loved these in W1. Light weight, eyes on the bell, deep hip hinge. Pairs beautifully with TGU — same shoulder stability pattern.

sets.name: Your Picks: Overhead Lunges + Simba Lift

exercises.notes: Lock KB out overhead with one arm. Step forward into lunge. Keep arm LOCKED and torso upright. Shoulder stability + legs. Go light — 15-25lb to start.
The 'Simba lift' — goblet squat down, then as you stand, extend the KB straight out in front at shoulder height, arms locked. Hold for a beat. Present that lion cub. Use a light KB.
Two-handed, explosive
Clean to rack, strict press. Tuesday you did these at 50lb — use the same or lighter here.
Squat to press, one fluid motion

tips: Thursday = refine Tuesday's new moves + try your requested exercises
Skill block is NOT conditioning — take your time, focus on form. Rest as needed between sides.
TGU: your right side got fluid Tuesday, now get the left side there too
Overhead lunges: start lighter than you think — the stability demand is sneaky
Simba lift: the front raise portion is the hard part. Arms locked, hold at the top for a beat.
Complex is only 3 rounds — it's the finisher, not the main event
Light bell for skill block (15-25lb), working bell for complex (50lb)

name: Week 2 KB Full Body
description: Same structure as W1 — squat, hinge, push, pull, core. Two swaps: high pulls replace swings in the hinge block (progression — more explosive pull), Turkish get-ups replace windmills in the core block (more complex movement, full-body coordination). Single bell, ~40 min.

exercises.notes: Light, loosen shoulders and thoracic spine
Easy reps, find your depth
Easy swings, groove the hinge
Same as W1 — aim for cleaner depth and more control. Full depth, elbows inside knees.
Hold KB goblet style. Rear foot on bench/chair. You know the movement now — push the depth.
Same as W1 — slow descent, squeeze glutes at top.
NEW — replaces swings. Explosive hip snap, pull the bell to shoulder height. More upper back work than swings.
Same as W1 — clean to rack, strict press. Try to go heavier or cleaner.
Same as W1 — hinge position, pull elbow past ribs.
NEW — replaces windmill. Go LIGHT. This is a complex full-body movement — learn the pattern. Look up the steps if needed.
Last push — full power, empty the tank. Same as W1.

High pulls are a progression from swings — same hip snap, but you pull the bell higher. More explosive.
Turkish get-ups: go VERY light the first time. This is skill work, not strength work yet.
If TGUs feel too awkward, do windmills instead — no shame, they're still great
Progress by adding reps, then adding a round, then going heavier

name: Week 2 Legs (Quads/Hamstrings/Glutes/Calves)
description: Same core lifts — beat your Week 1 numbers. Swap: step-ups replace lunges (different unilateral pattern, easier to control when fatigued). You know your working weights now, so push harder.

Open up the hips
Bodyweight walking lunges, feel the stretch

W1 working weight: 65lbs plates (110 total). Aim for 4x10. Slow descent, squeeze glutes at top.

W1 working weight: 360lbs. Full stretch at bottom, squeeze at top. Push toward 20 reps.
W1 working weight: 50lbs. Full ROM, 2-second negative. If 3x12 is easy, bump to 60.
NEW — replaces lunges. DBs at sides, step onto bench. Drive through the top leg, don't push off the back foot. More controlled than lunges when your legs are cooked.

name: Week 2 Pull (Back/Biceps/Rear Delts)
description: Same core lifts, one key swap: DB rows replace barbell rows (you skipped BB rows W1 because the gym was packed — DB rows need less space and let you go heavier per side). Added a 4th set of pull-ups since you hit 3x8.

exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades, warm up upper back
Loosen thoracic spine
Decompress spine, activate grip and lats
You hit 3x8 in W1 — now do 4 sets. If you can't hit 8 on set 4, that's fine. Get the volume in.
NEW — replaces barbell row. One arm at a time, brace on bench. Pull elbow past ribs, squeeze at top. Needs less space than a barbell.
W1 working weight: 70lbs. Lean back slightly, pull to upper chest.
W1 working weight: 140lbs. Chest up, pull to belly button.
W1 working weight: 47.5lbs. High cable, rope. Pull to face, externally rotate.
W1 working weight: 42.5lbs. Full extension, squeeze at top.
W1 working weight: 20lbs. Bent over, squeeze rear delts.
W1 working weight: 20lbs. Neutral grip, hits brachialis.

tips: Pull-ups go from 3 sets to 4 — you earned it by hitting 3x8 last week
DB rows replace barbell rows — more practical at Arrilaga and you can go heavier per arm
Everything else stays the same — beat your Week 1 numbers
Rest 3 min between pull-ups, 2 min between rows, 75s between cable isolation
Cable station: swap attachments, don't move. Same efficiency trick as W1.
If a machine is taken, check Training Notes for swap options
Total volume: ~19 working sets for pull muscles (up from 18)

name: Week 2 Push (Chest/Shoulders/Triceps)
description: Same core lifts as Week 1 — beat your numbers. Swaps: pec deck replaces cable fly (constant tension, easier to load), overhead tricep extension replaces pushdown (hits long head). Station-based grouping.

exercises.notes: Forward then backward, loosen shoulders
Light band, squeeze shoulder blades
Easy reps, get blood flowing to chest and tris
W1 you used DB bench at 50lbs. Try barbell if rack is free, otherwise DB bench — aim for 4x8 this week.
W1 working weight: 45lbs. Aim for 4x8 minimum, push toward 10 if you can.
W1 working weight: 40lbs. Aim for 3x8 clean reps before going up.
W1 working weight: 15lbs. Hit 3x15 before bumping to 17.5 or 20.

W1 working weight: 12.5lbs. Single arm cross-body. Constant tension.

NEW — replaces pushdown. HIGH cable, bench ~45° facing away (or kneel at cable column facing away). Hands near chest, extend out. Hits lats + tricep long head. Go lighter than you think. Video: x.com/deanttraining/status/2027798729642655818

tips: Same core lifts as Week 1 — your job is to beat your numbers
Two swaps: pec deck for cable fly (better tension curve), overhead tricep extension for pushdown (hits long head)
If pec deck is taken, cable fly is fine — just use the same weight from W1 and add reps

Rest 3 min between bench sets, 2 min between shoulder press, 75s between cable/isolation sets
If a machine is taken, check Training Notes for swap options
Total volume: ~16 working sets for push muscles

name: W3 KB Day Morning Flow (Explore Prep)
description: 15 min flow before KB Explore day. Core is harder this week (plank variations + hollow body), mobility targets the hips and thoracic spine you'll need for bent press and cossack squat. Doubles as a standalone flow if you're short on time.

exercises.notes: NEW — hollow body position but rocking back and forth. Gymnastic core work. Keep lower back pressed down the entire time.
NEW — in plank, tap opposite shoulder without rotating hips. Anti-rotation core work. Harder than a regular plank.
Familiar from W1-W2. Opposite arm/leg, hold 2 seconds. The stable base for everything else.
NEW — legs wide, fold forward. Opens adductors for cossack squats later. Go slow, breathe, walk hands forward.
NEW — kneel, lean back. Opens quad and hip flexors deeply. Start gentle — if it's too intense, put hands behind you for support.
30s each side. Thoracic rotation you'll need for bent press. Knees to one side, arms spread.
Deep squat hold. Elbows press knees out. Primes the deep squat pattern for goblet squats and cossack squats.
NEW — hands and feet on ground, knees hovering 1 inch off floor. Hold still. Fires up everything — core, shoulders, quads. Sneaky hard.
Flow between positions. Wakes up the whole posterior chain.

tips: 15 min — harder core + KB-specific mobility. This is a real flow, not just stretching.
Hollow body rocks: if you can't maintain position, just hold static hollow body
Saddle pose: go easy first time. If knees hurt, come out immediately — it's a flexibility thing, not a pain thing.
Bear crawl hold: the goal is stillness. If you're shaking, that's working.
This flow prepares you specifically for bent press (thoracic rotation) and cossack squat (adductors)

name: W3 KB Conditioning Morning Flow
description: 15 min hip-heavy flow before KB Refine day. Different from the D2 flow — this one is more recovery-oriented since you've had 3 training days already. Plow pose for spinal decompression, deep stretches for sore hips, light core.

exercises.notes: Slow transitions. Inhale arch, exhale round. Gets into the thoracic spine and shoulders.

NEW — lie on back, legs over head, toes touch floor behind you. Full posterior chain stretch + spinal decompression. If you can't reach the floor, just go as far as comfortable. Skip if neck feels weird.
Grab feet, knees toward armpits. Rock gently side to side. Resets the lower back and opens hips.
30s each side. Keep hips square, fold over front leg. Different angle than Tuesday's hip work.
Hands and knees, wide knees, sink back. Intense inner thigh opener. Breathe through the discomfort.
Kneel, lean back. Quad and hip flexor stretch. If you did this Tuesday, you should be able to go deeper today.
Opposite arm/leg. Keep lower back glued to floor. Familiar and effective.
20s each side. Obliques + shoulder stability. Primes the lateral stability you need for KB work.

tips: 15 min — recovery-focused. You've had push, KB, and pull this week already.
Plow pose: if it feels weird on your neck, skip it. Do a forward fold instead.
Frog stretch: second time this week if you did the pull day flow. Should feel easier.
If you're time-crunched like last week: do the decompress + deep hips blocks, skip core.
This flow + the KB Refine workout should take ~45 min total

name: W3 Legs Morning Flow
description: 15 min squat prep + hip primer. More dynamic than W1-W2 — warrior flow to open hips, crow pose attempt for fun, deeper stretches to prime the heavy squats. Your squats are going to 65/side today, so prep matters.

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Deep lunge, arms overhead. Opens hip flexors before you load them with a barbell.
30s each side. From warrior II, reach over. Lateral hip + groin stretch that squats and lunges need.
Squat to standing, hands at toes. Groove the deep squat pattern before you go heavy.
Deep side lunge, alternating. Primes the same lateral hip pattern as cossack squats from KB day.
30s each side. Elbows down for deeper stretch. Hip flexors and groin.
30s each side. Internal and external hip rotation. Your hips need this before heavy squats.
NEW — try it! Hands on ground, knees on triceps, lean forward until feet lift. If you fall, you fall. It's fun and builds the body awareness you're developing from KB work. Even 3 seconds is a win.
NEW — in plank, rotate hips side to side, tapping the floor. Obliques + hip mobility in one move.
Lower back pressed down. Arms and legs hovering. 20s of pure core.

tips: 15 min — more dynamic and intense than W1-W2 leg day flows
Warrior + side angle: think yoga class energy. Breathe deep into each one.
Crow pose: just TRY it. Even if you only hold for 2 seconds, you're building something. Put a pillow in front of you if you're worried about face-planting.
This flow specifically primes heavy squats — the hip work matters today
If short on time: do dynamic warmup + hip mobility, skip core/balance

name: W3D6 GTC Survival Floor Workout
description: Boredom-busting floor workout for streaming training at home. 4 blocks: barre burn, plyo sweat, push-up party, core destroyer. Do blocks between sessions or all at once if you're dying of boredom. Will make you sweat.

exercises.notes: Deep wide stance, tiny pulses at the bottom. Feel it in inner thighs immediately.
Plie down, rise onto toes at the top. Calves + quads + balance.
15 each side. All fours, kick up and pulse. Glute fire.
15 each side. All fours, knee out to the side. Hip abductors.
Explode up, soft landing. This is where you earn the sweat.
Fast but controlled. Drive knees to chest.
Wide stance jump — land soft in plie. Barre meets plyo.
Only 6, but make them count. Full extension at the top.
Standard. Full ROM.
Hands together under chest. Tricep destroyer.
Plank to push-up position and back. Alternating arms.
In plank, drive knee to elbow. 5 each side. Core + hip flexors.
Slow and controlled. Opposite arm/leg extends. Lower back stays glued to floor.
20 total (10 each side). Elbow to opposite knee.
Arms overhead, legs hovering, rock like a banana. If you can't rock, just hold.
In plank, rotate hips side to side. 6 each side. Oblique fire.

tips: Do all 4 blocks for a full 25-min sweat session, or cherry-pick blocks between training streams
Block 1 (barre) is sneaky hard — the pulses accumulate fast
Block 2 (plyo) is where you'll actually sweat — push the pace
Block 3 (push-ups) builds on your bench press gains — you're stronger than you think
Block 4 (core) — hollow body rocks are humbling. Just try.
Rest day tomorrow — go hard if you want to

name: Week 3 KB Conditioning (Refine & Drill)
description: Thursday = refine Tuesday's new stuff + keep your favorites. Drill bent press and cossack squat from Tuesday. Keep overhead lunges and Simba lift at heavier weights (you earned the bump). Finisher complex stays — it's a real workout, not just practice.

exercises.notes: Loosen up fast
Easy swings to prime the hinge
Drill from Tuesday. Same light weight (18lb). Focus on the path — your body moves down and around the bell. If Tuesday felt ok, try to go slightly deeper.
Drill from Tuesday. KB goblet hold. Push for deeper range than Tuesday. Heels down, chest up.
Drill from Tuesday. 25lb — left side needs the reps. Eyes on the bell, slow and controlled. Focus on cleaning up any wobbly transitions.
W2 you did these at 50lb and they were fine. Stay at 50 or try alternating arms each set. Lock out overhead, torso upright.
Simba lift — W2 was 18lb (too easy). Bump to 25lb or 35lb. If 50lb is the max, add a 2-3 second hold at the top with arms locked.
Your comfort move. Light bell. Keep it in the rotation — it's the perfect active rest between the harder stuff.
50lb. Two-handed, explosive.
50lb. Stay at 50, flex reps based on feel. If you get 5 each, great. If you get 4, that's fine too.

Simba lift: bump the weight. 18lb was too easy. 25-35lb range, or add the pause hold at top.
Windmill is your palate cleanser — light, familiar, keeps shoulders healthy between the harder stuff
Complex: 50lb for everything. Flex reps on clean & press if needed.
Total time ~30 min — longer than W2 because you added windmill to the favorites block

name: Week 3 KB Full Body (Explore Day)
description: Tuesday = Explore. Two new weird movements this week: bent press (overhead strength through rotation) and cossack squat (deep lateral hip mobility + strength). Plus your now-familiar TGU and windmill. Traditional KB strength stays in the mix — you're building a toolkit.

exercises.notes: Light, loosen shoulders and thoracic spine
Easy reps, find your depth
Easy swings, groove the hinge

NEW — the weird one. Hold KB overhead, then bend your torso sideways and DOWN while keeping the KB locked out above. Your body moves around the weight, not the other way. Go LIGHT (18lb). It's like a windmill's cooler older sibling.
NEW — deep side lunge. One leg straight, one bent in a deep squat. Hold KB goblet style for counterbalance. Opens hips laterally in a way nothing else does. Go slow, go deep.
W2 you went from bodyweight to 18lb. Try 25lb if 18 felt controlled. Left side should feel less derpy by now. Eyes on the bell the whole time.
You love these. Same weight as TGU or lighter. Deep hip hinge, eyes on the bell. These are your comfort zone now — push the depth.
50lb. W2 was brutal — you got 6/6/5. Aim for 3x6 this week. The movement should feel smoother by now.
50lb. Hinge position, pull elbow past ribs. Solid and familiar.
50lb. Deep, controlled. You know this one cold.
50lb. Empty the tank. Full hip snap.

Bent press: go LIGHT. 18lb max. This is a skill that takes weeks to learn. Watch the weight stay still while your body moves under it.
Cossack squat: use KB as counterbalance. Heels stay down. If you can't go deep, go as deep as you can — it'll improve fast.
TGU: if 18lb felt easy in W2, try 25lb. Left side should be catching up.
Clean & press: stay at 50lb, push for more consistent reps
Light bell (18lb) for new movements, working bell (50lb) for familiar movements
