name: Week 3 Legs (Quads/Hamstrings/Glutes/Calves)
description: Big corrections from W2: leg curl jumps to 90 (real working weight), calf raises bump up, squat starts at 65/side. Walking lunges replace step-ups (back to unilateral basics). You're stronger than you thought.

exercises.notes: Full depth, arms in front for balance
Open up the hips
Bodyweight walking lunges, feel the stretch
W2: pyramided to 65/side (175 total) and got 6. Start at 55 for set 1, then 65 for sets 2-4. Goal: 3x6+ at 65/side.
W2: 65/side was hard but doable (8/10/8). Same weight, aim for more consistent reps — 3x10 at 65.
W2: 360x10 all sets, went deeper on last two. Same weight, keep the deeper ROM, push for 12 reps. If 3x12 deep is easy, bump to 400 (4.5 plates/side).
W2: 360 was too easy. Bump to 450 (5 plates/side). Full stretch at bottom, squeeze at top.
W2: 50 was a joke, 90 was real working weight (negatives hard). Start at 90. Make sure the pad position matches what you found in W2. 2-second negatives.
Back to lunges — W1 had these, W2 swapped to step-ups. DBs at sides, 40lbs. Long stride, back knee kisses the floor. Don't rush — your legs are cooked by now and that's the point.

tips: Squat: warm up at 45/side, then 55 for set 1, 65 for sets 2-4. You've earned it.
Leg curl: 90 is your real weight. Don't second-guess it — position matters more than the number on the stack.
Calf raises: bump weight. Calves are stubborn — they need more load, not more reps.
Walking lunges at the end will be brutal on tired legs. That's intentional — time under tension when fatigued = growth.
Rest 3 min between squat/RDL sets, 2 min on leg press, 90s on isolation
If a machine is taken, check Training Notes for swap options
Total volume: ~17 working sets for legs

name: Week 3 Pull (Back/Biceps/Rear Delts)
description: DB rows now pyramid by design (you were doing it naturally). Preacher curl replaces hammer curl — way harder, way better for bicep growth. Note which lat pulldown/cable row machine you use so numbers are comparable.

exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades, warm up upper back
Loosen thoracic spine
Decompress spine, activate grip and lats
W2: 8/6/8/5. Goal: more consistent across sets. Aim for 4x7+ or try to beat total reps (W2 total: 27).
PYRAMID — you did this naturally in W2: 50→55→60→65x7. Now it's official. Set 1: 55x10, Set 2: 60x8, Set 3: 65x8, Set 4: 70x6-8 (new PR attempt). One arm at a time, brace on bench.
W2: 140x11, 140x10, 160x7. Pulley variance is real — different machines run different scales. Start at your W2 weight on whichever machine you're on.
W2: 120x10 was working weight (140 was immovable on a different machine — scales differ). Start at 120, push for 130 if it feels right.
W2: 47.5 x20/18/18/15. Try 50lbs. High cable, rope, pull to face and externally rotate.
W2: 42.5x10 then dropped to 37.5. Try 42.5 for all sets — you're stronger than last week.
W2: 20x15, getting hard by set 3. Try 22.5 or 25 if available. Bent over, squeeze rear delts.

NEW — replaces hammer curl (too easy at 20lb). Sit-down preacher bench with fixed EZ curl bars. Try a few weights to find your working weight — it'll be humbling. Full stretch at bottom, squeeze at top.

tips: DB rows are now a pyramid by design — go heavier each set, top set is a PR attempt
Preacher curl: the fixed EZ bars have set weights. Try a few to find your working weight — probably 30-40lb range
WRITE DOWN which lat pulldown and cable row machine you use. Different pulleys = different numbers.
Pull-up goal: beat your W2 total of 27 reps across 4 sets
Cable curls at 42.5 for all 4 sets — you can do it, you're just 2 weeks stronger
Rest 3 min between pull-ups, 2 min between rows, 75s between cable isolation
Total volume: ~20 working sets for pull muscles

name: W3 Pull Morning Flow
description: 15 min — back/lat primer, core mini-block to get warm, then a flowing hip sequence to finish. Moving before stretching = deeper stretches. Core won't interfere with pull day — different muscle groups.

exercises.notes: Decompress the spine. Relax shoulders, let gravity pull you. Wakes up grip and lats for later.
From the hang: pull shoulder blades down and together without bending arms. Activates the pull muscles you'll need at the gym.
On hands and knees — reach one arm under, temple to floor, then open to ceiling. Flow back and forth. Opens thoracic spine and lats. Perfect pull-day prep.
Opposite arm/leg. Lower back GLUED to floor. Slow and controlled — 2 seconds out, 2 seconds back.
Arms by ears, legs hovering. Lower back pressed down. If 20s is easy, go 30s. If impossible, bend knees.
20s each side. Obliques + shoulder stability. Stack or stagger feet. Don't let hips sag.
Slow — touch elbow to opposite knee, fully extend other leg. No rushing. Quality over speed.
You're warm now — these will feel way better. Slow transitions, inhale arch, exhale round.
Flow: child's pose → slide forward through cobra → push back to child's pose. Smooth, continuous.
Flow: plank → push hips high to down dog → pedal feet → float back to plank. One continuous movement.
30s each side. From down dog, step one foot outside same hand. Sink hips. You're warm — go deeper than you think.
Sit, legs wide. Walk hands forward slowly. Then reach to each side. Your adductors are ready for this now.
Soles together, knees out. Fold forward. From the straddle, just bring your feet in — natural transition.
Roll onto back, grab feet, knees toward armpits. Rock gently side to side. This is your cooldown.

tips: 15 min — three parts: back primer (3 min), core work (5 min), yoga hip flow (7 min)
Order matters: primer → core gets you warm → stretches go deeper because you're not cold.
Part 3 is meant to FLOW. Don't reset between moves — transition from one into the next.
Cat-cow → child's-to-cobra → plank-to-down-dog → lizard is one continuous sequence on the floor.
Then sit up for straddle → butterfly → roll back for happy baby. The whole thing is one arc.
No pull-up bar at home? Do the hang/scap work at the gym as your first warmup set.
Short on time? Do Part 1 + Part 3, skip core. Still a solid 10 min flow.

name: Week 3 Push (Chest/Shoulders/Triceps)
description: You own these lifts now. Bench at 55/side minimum, push toward 65. Swap: push press replaces strict shoulder press (uses legs to drive heavier overhead). Everything else stays — beat your W2 numbers.

exercises.notes: Forward then backward, loosen shoulders
Light band, squeeze shoulder blades
Easy reps, get blood flowing
W2 working weight: 55/side (155 total). Start at 55, push for 65/side (175) on sets 3-4 if set 1-2 felt clean. You hit 65x2 in W2 squats — your bench can get there too.
W2 working weight: 50lbs. Aim for 4x10. If you hit it clean, try 55s next week.
NEW — replaces strict shoulder press. Small dip with legs, drive the weight overhead explosively. You can go heavier than strict press — try 45lbs, maybe 50. More athletic, more fun.
W2 working weight: 15lbs. If 15x15 is clean, bump to 17.5 or 20. Last set: drop set — do 15s to failure, immediately grab 10s for 10 more reps.
W2: Kinesis machine at 6. Try the actual pec deck if it's free. Squeeze hard at center, 2-second hold.
W2 working weight: 15lbs (too easy). Bump to 20. Single arm cross-body.
W2 working weight: 45lbs (cable column). Same setup — HIGH cable, kneeling facing away. Try 50 if 45 felt manageable.

tips: Push press is the big swap — uses leg drive to get heavier weight overhead. It's a progression from strict press, not a cheat.
Lateral raise drop set on set 3: go to failure at working weight, immediately grab lighter DBs for 10 more. Burns.
Bench: you're ready for 65/side. Even if you only get 4-5 reps, that's progress.
Cable lat raise: 15 was too easy. Bump to 20, no excuses.
Rest 3 min between bench sets, 2 min between push press, 75s between cable/isolation sets
Total volume: ~16 working sets for push muscles

name: W3 Push Morning Flow
description: 12 min shoulder/chest opener with more intensity than W1-W2. Warrior flow for dynamic shoulder opening, plank-to-down-dog for active mobility, hollow body hold for core. Same targets, new movements.

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Arms overhead, sink into the lunge. Opens hip flexors AND shoulders simultaneously.
30s each side. Open the hips, arms wide. Feel the chest stretch as arms extend.
30s each side. Reach long, side body stretch. Gets into the lats and obliques — great pre-push day.
Smooth transitions. Plank loads the chest/shoulders, down dog stretches them. Best of both worlds.
Lie face down, reach one leg across to the opposite side. Opens chest, shoulders, and hip flexors in one move.
From a hang or doorframe. Pull shoulder blades down and back. Activates stabilizers before you push heavy.
Lower back pressed into floor, arms and legs hovering. This is the gymnastic core position — harder than a plank.
20s each side. Obliques and shoulder stability. Stack your feet or stagger if needed.

tips: 12 min — warrior flow + active mobility + core. More movement than W1-W2 flows.
Warrior flow: think yoga class energy. Breathe into each position.
Scorpion stretch is sneaky good — you'll feel it in your chest and hip flexors
Hollow body hold: if 20s is easy, extend to 30s. If it's impossible, bend your knees.

name: Week 4 KB Conditioning (Refine & Drill)
description: Saturday = drill Tuesday's progressions. Bent press at 30lb if Tuesday went well. TGU cleaning up left side. Overhead lunges and Simba lift heavier. Finisher circuit is earned — legs just went yesterday so keep it controlled.

exercises.notes: Loosen up fast
Easy swings to prime the hinge
If Tuesday's 30lb felt ok, stay at 30 and add a rep (aim for 5). If it felt shaky, drop to 25 and nail the path. Thursday off means your shoulder is fresh — use it.
Drill from Tuesday. Push for deeper range. Heels down, stay in the hole for a beat.
30lb. Left side: slow down at every checkpoint. Right side is already solid — use it as the template for left.
50lb. Legs just did heavy work yesterday — keep torso upright and locked, let the shoulder stability do the work, not brute force.
W3 Refine: pyramided to 45lb with pause on top. Stay at 45lb, do all 3 sets there instead of pyramiding. 2-second hold at top.
25lb (bumped from Tuesday's 18lb in prior weeks). This is your palate cleanser — light relative to everything else, but the new weight demands focus.
50lb. Two-handed, explosive.
50lb. Legs are tired from yesterday — 2 rounds instead of 3. Quality over grinding.
50lb. 6 reps instead of 8 — legs day was yesterday. Don't be a hero.

tips: Saturday after legs day — keep the finisher to 2 rounds, not 3. Your legs already worked.
Bent press: if 30lb felt clean Tuesday, go for 5 reps each side instead of 3.
TGU left side: pause at the elbow prop, the hand prop, the hip hinge. Don't rush the wobbly transitions.
Simba lift at 45lb flat across all 3 sets — no pyramid, just consistent heavy reps.
Windmill at 25lb: new weight, same movement. Trust it.

name: Week 4 KB Full Body (Explore — GTC Light)
description: GTC day — you're on your feet for 6 hours after this. Flow is the centerpiece, skill work is lighter, strength block is trimmed. Get the movement quality in without cooking your legs for a day of standing.

exercises.notes: Light, loosen shoulders and thoracic spine
Easy swings, groove the hinge

sets.name: Block 1: KB Flow (OTD Flow)

exercises.notes: Dead stop on floor, explosive pull to rack.
Full depth from rack position.
Step wide, hips back, trailing leg straight.
Bend AWAY from the bell, lock out overhead.
Control the descent back to start. Complete one side, then switch.

sets.notes: OTD flow (Madeline Conti): dead clean → squat → lateral lunge → bent press → tactical clean. All 5 moves connect seamlessly — no pausing. Use 25lb. 3 rounds each side, do the whole flow 3 times. Focus on making it smooth.

exercises.notes: 25lb — keep it light. You just did bent press in the flow, now isolate it for 3 clean reps per side.
25lb. Deep hip hinge, eyes on the bell.
25lb goblet. Lighter than last week — save your legs for standing all day.
50lb. 2 sets only. Keep the technique gains from W3 — catch with knee bend, full lockout.
50lb. Finish strong, then go demo some GPUs.

tips: GTC light day — 25 min, not 40. You're standing for 6 hours after this.
Block 1 (flow) is the main event. Light bell, smooth transitions.
Block 2 skill work: lighter weights, fewer rounds. Saturday you'll go heavier.
Block 3: just 2 sets of C&P and swings. In and out.
Skip TGU today — save it for Saturday when you're not about to stand all day.
Cossack squat at 25 instead of 45 — your legs will thank you at hour 5 of demos.

name: W4 KB Morning Flow (Saturday)
description: 10 min KB-specific prep. Light and flowing — end of a big GTC week. Open the thoracic spine for bent press, activate hips for TGU and lunges, and get the shoulder ready for overhead work.

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Thoracic rotation for bent press and windmill. Go deeper than earlier in the week.
Slow and deliberate. Full spinal wave.
Chest toward floor, arms extended. Shoulder + lat opener for all the overhead work ahead.
30s each side. TGU and overhead lunges both need open hips.
Bodyweight. Groove the pattern before you load it with a bell. Heels down, chest up.
Low lunge + twist. Hip flexors and thoracic. Primes overhead lunge.

tips: 10 min — end of GTC week, keep it light and loose.
Cossack squat in the warmup: bodyweight first, then load it in the workout.
Thoracic rotation is key for bent press — if your T-spine is locked, the bent press path gets weird.
This flows into the KB session directly — no need for a separate warmup block.

name: Week 4 Legs (Quads/Hamstrings/Glutes/Calves)
description: Your legs are fresh — no KB conditioning the day before this time (Thursday is rest). Squat from 65/side without the extended warmup you needed in W3. Leg curl pushes to 105 for all sets. Leg press at 4 plates + 25 across the board. Calf raises need more load — bump to 6 plates.

exercises.notes: Full depth, arms in front. You're not sore today — these will feel easier.
Open up the hips
Bodyweight, feel the stretch

W3: needed 2x55 warmup then got 65x6, 65x6, 65x8, 65x8. Today your legs aren't dead — try 1 warmup set at 55 then straight to 65 for 4 sets. Goal: 4x8 at 65/side with full depth. You know you can do it.
W3: 65/side 8/10/10. Goal: 3x10 consistent. If 65 is easy across all sets, try 70/side on set 4.
W3: 4 plates/side for 2 sets, then 4 plates + 25 on last set — all with great ROM. This week: 4 plates + 25 for all 3 working sets. Knee to chest depth, no half reps.
W3: 5 plates/side x3x20 = too easy (you said so). Bump to 6 plates/side. That posterior chain activation you felt in W3 — chase that again.
W3: 90x12, 90x12, 105x12 (last set hard). This week: try 90x12, 105x12, 105x12. Get that 105 for two sets instead of one. Slow negative — 2 full seconds down.

W3: 45lb (all 40s taken) — absolute death, split last set. Today grab 40s if they're free, otherwise 45 again. Goal: don't split the last set. Long stride, back knee to floor. Your legs are fresher than W3.

tips: KEY DIFFERENCE from W3: your legs are not pre-cooked today. You had two rest days. Use that.
Squat: one warmup set at 55 then 4x8 at 65/side. You've done 65x8 twice already — it's your working weight now.
Leg press: 4 plates + 25 for all sets, not just the last one. Your ROM held up fine in W3.
Leg curl at 105: two sets instead of one. Slow negative on every rep.
Calf raises at 6 plates: 5 was too easy, you said it yourself. Time to find the new hard.
Lunges: you survived 45lb x3 sets on dead legs in W3. Fresh legs today means no splitting.
Rest 3 min between squat/RDL, 2 min on leg press, 90s on isolation

name: W4 Legs Morning Flow
description: 15 min hip + squat prep. Different from W3 — no crow pose, add pigeon prep and a deep malasana hold. KEY DIFFERENCE today: your legs are not pre-cooked. This flow should feel easier and looser than W3.

exercises.notes: Standing, big slow circles. Feel the hip socket open up.
Squat to standing, hands at toes. This week go SLOWER — 3 seconds down, 2 seconds in the hole, drive up. Groove the pattern.
Low lunge, back knee down, reach inside arm toward front foot and twist. Hip flexor + thoracic.
45s each side. Front shin parallel or angled, sink into it. This is a deeper hip opener than anything in W3's flow. Breathe.
30s each side. Internal and external hip rotation. You know this one — go deeper than last week.
Deep side lunge alternating. Same as W3 but you've been doing cossack squats for 2 weeks — you have more range now. Use it.
Feet hip-width, drive hips up, squeeze glutes at top. Activates glutes before you squat — reduces knee stress.
Opposite arm and leg extend slowly. Lower back stays down. Core bracing before loading a barbell.
20s. Arms overhead, legs hovering. Same as W3. Just do it.

tips: 15 min — pigeon pose is new this week (deeper than lizard). Take your time in it.
Your legs are FRESH today (no KB yesterday). This flow should feel noticeably easier than W3 legs day.
Glute bridge before squats: activates the glutes so they share the load. Reduces the knee-dominant pattern.
Malasana: slower this week. You've drilled the squat pattern for 3 weeks — now feel it.
If short on time: hip warmup + pigeon + glute bridge. 8 min minimum.

name: Week 4 Pull (Back/Biceps/Rear Delts)
description: Beat W3's 32 pull-up total. DB row pyramid goes to 75 (W3 PR was 70x6). Preacher curl: build from W3's humbling intro — try 20/side. Cable row: push for 130 on back right machine. Your back is stronger than it's ever been.

exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades, warm up upper back
Loosen thoracic spine
W3: 20s. Try 25s. Decompress, grip, lats.
W3: 10/8/8/6 = 32 total. Goal: beat 32. Aim for 4x8 (32 total) but with more consistent sets — ideally no set lower than 7. Full ROM on every rep.
W3 pyramid: 55x10, 60x8, 65x8, 70x6 (PR). This week: 55x10, 65x8, 70x8, 75x5-6 (new PR attempt on top set). Brace hard on 75 — at this weight form really matters.
W3: 140x11, 140x12, 150x8 (back left machine). This week: 145x10, 145x10, 155x8. Write down the machine — numbers don't transfer between pulleys.
W3: 120x10 (honest), 140x8 (leaning back). This week: try 130 — that's the middle ground. Back right machine. Keep torso upright, no leaning.
W3: 50 x20/20/18. Stay at 50. Goal: 3x20 all clean. External rotate hard at the end of each rep.
W3: 42.5x12 x3, 42.5x10 on set 4. This week: 42.5 x4 sets, all 12 reps. You were there in W3 — repeat it cleanly.
W3: 25x15, 25x14, 25x12+4. Seated bent-over modification is your version now. Stay at 25, try to get 3x15 clean.
W3: 15/side worked (humbling at 10 reps). This week: try 20/side for sets 1-2. Drop back to 15 on set 3 if form breaks. Find out where 20/side lands.

tips: Pull-up goal: beat 32. Consistency matters more than one big set — 4x8 > 10/8/8/6.
DB row top set: 75x5-6 is a PR attempt. Don't sacrifice form for reps — a clean 5 beats a dirty 7.
Cable row: 130 is the target. If back right machine isn't available, note whichever you use.
Preacher curl at 20/side: it'll be humbling again. That's normal — it's a strict isolation, no cheat possible.
Hands were dead in W3 from all the pulling — if grip fails, that's fine. Lats are the actual target.
Rest 3 min between pull-ups, 2 min between rows, 75s between cable isolation

name: W4 Pull Morning Flow
description: 12 min back + grip primer. Different from W3 — adds dead hang progression and cat-cow with reach. Your pull numbers are climbing fast; make sure the shoulder and lat prep matches what you're asking them to do.

exercises.notes: Slow. Exhale into cat (round spine), inhale into cow (arch). 10 full breaths.
Child's pose, but walk hands to one side then the other — 15s each side. Lateral lat stretch.
30s each side. Same as push day but focus on upper back rotation, not just shoulder.
20s passive hang. Feel the lats stretch and activate. Grip strong but shoulders relaxed.
Dead hang, then retract scapulae — no elbow bend, just shoulder blades pulling down and together. Activates the exact muscles you need for pull-ups and rows.
Light band. Straight arms, pull to chest height. Rear delts and scaps warm.
On back, grab outer feet, knees wide. Gentle hip opener — your DB rows and TGU both need open hips.
Legs straight, reach toward feet. Hamstring + lower back release. Don't force it — breathe and sink.
30s each side. On back, one knee across the body. Releases lower back tightness from sitting at GTC all day.

tips: 12 min — scapular pull-ups are new this week. They directly prime pull-up form.
Dead hang: let the shoulders fully relax at the bottom. Most people never hang passively — it decompresses the whole shoulder.
Supine twist: if you've been standing all day at GTC on Tuesday, your low back will love this Wednesday morning.
If short on time: spine primer + dead hang + scapular pull-ups. 5 min minimum.

name: Week 4 Push (Chest/Shoulders/Triceps)
description: GTC week push day. Swapped bench for chest press machine (shoulder comfort + no spotter needed). Push press stays at 45/side — 50 wouldn't go overhead. Cable lat raise jumped to 50 (from W3's 30). Straight arm pulldown added back.

exercises.notes: Forward then backward, loosen shoulders
Light band, squeeze shoulder blades
Warm up the chest, get blood flowing

W4 actuals: 175x6 (struggled), 175x8 (adjusted start position forward), 175x8, 185x4, 180x3. Working weight is 175. Adjust start position so handles aren't too far behind back — that loads the shoulder joint.
W4 actuals: 55x10, 55x10, 55x10 (really hard), 55x9. Hard from set 1 but consistent. Stay at 55 until 4x10 is clean.
W4 actuals: 50/side couldn't get overhead, dropped to 45/side — 5/6/5. Stay at 45 until 3x6+ is consistent. 50 is not ready yet.
W4 actuals: 15x15 x4 sets + drop to 10. Probably too easy — try 20s if available, otherwise 5 sets at 15 with drop set.
W4 actuals: 105x12, 105x12, 115x9 (failure). W3's '100' was actually 105 (5lb plate was on). 115 is the new challenge weight.
W4 actuals: 50x12, 50x12, 65x6/55x4. Huge jump from W3's 30. Working weight is 50, 65 is a stretch goal.
W4 actuals: 55x12 (too easy), 65x10, 65x12 (form breaking at end). 65 is the new working weight. Keep hands apart.
W4 actuals: 70/90/105 all x12, all too easy. Start at 105+ next time. This is a pull exercise but works great as a push day finisher when lats are fresh.

tips: Chest press machine: adjust start position so handles aren't behind your back. That loaded the shoulder.
Push press at 45: 50 wouldn't go overhead. Stay at 45 until you get clean 3x6+.
Pec deck: W3's weight was actually 105 not 100 (hidden 5lb plate). 115 is the real progression.
Cable lat raise: 30→50 in one week. Real strength gain.
Tri extension: 65 is the new working weight. Hands apart.
Straight arm pulldown: start at 105+ — the pyramid was too easy.

name: W4 Push Morning Flow
description: 12 min shoulder + chest primer. Different from W3 — scorpion stretch is out, we add thread-the-needle for thoracic rotation and a wall angel for shoulder mobility. Your push is getting stronger; open up the range before you load it.

exercises.notes: Big circles, loosen the joint before loading it
30s each side. From all fours, thread one arm under and through. Opens thoracic rotation — where push press range comes from.
Back flat against wall, arms slide up and down like a snow angel. If you can't keep your back flat, that's the tightness you're working on.
Easy and controlled. Full ROM — this is warming, not working.
Hands close, feel the triceps engage. Not to failure — just activation.
Flow between plank and downward dog. Opens chest and shoulders, warms the pushing muscles.
30s each side. Low lunge, twist toward front knee, reach overhead. Opens hip flexors AND thoracic spine — primes push press.
Hands on a doorframe or wall corner, lean forward gently. Feel the pec stretch. 30s.
All fours, walk hands forward, chest toward floor. Deep shoulder and chest stretch to close out.

tips: 12 min — different from W3's warrior flow. Focus on shoulder mobility before the heavy pressing.
Thread-the-needle is new — go slow, breathe into the rotation. Tight thoracic = limited push press lockout.
Wall angels: if your back peels off the wall, you found your sticking point. Work through it gently.
Puppy pose last — warm muscles stretch deeper. Let gravity do the work.
If short on time: skip chest activation block, just do shoulder opener + stretch

name: Week 5 KB Conditioning (Thursday Refine — Light)
description: Movement practice, not a workout. Light bells, low volume, zero leg loading. Legs day is tomorrow. Drill the OTD flow and skill movements at easy weights. Get in, get smooth, get out in 20 minutes.

exercises.notes: Light bell. Just loosen up the shoulders.

sets.name: Block 1: OTD Flow (Easy)

exercises.notes: Smooth pull, no effort.
Light and easy — don't sit in the hole.
Easy range, don't push depth today.
20lb makes this feel like nothing. Focus on the path.
Controlled descent. Complete one side, then switch.

sets.notes: OTD flow at 20lb — lighter than Tuesday. 2 rounds each side, do the whole flow twice. This should feel effortless. You're polishing the pattern, not building it.

exercises.notes: 20-25lb. If Tuesday's 30lb went well, use 25 here max. This is about the groove, not the load.
18-20lb. Light. Deep hip hinge, eyes on the bell, zero grinding.
25lb — lighter than Tuesday's 30. Drill the transitions. Pause at each checkpoint for a full breath. Left side: own every position before moving to the next.

tips: This is NOT a workout. It's movement practice. 20 minutes, light bells, zero intensity.
Legs day is tomorrow. No swings, no C&P, no squats, no lunges, no finisher. Nothing that loads the legs.
OTD flow at 20lb should feel like a warm-up. If it feels like work, the bell is too heavy.
Bent press and windmill: you're greasing the groove, not testing limits.
TGU at 25lb is about transitions, not strength. Pause everywhere. Breathe.
Do not get inspired. Do not add sets. Do not grab the 50. You will regret it tomorrow under a barbell.

name: Week 5 KB Full Body (Tuesday Explore)
description: Full KB session. OTD flow stays at 25lb to keep drilling fluidity. Skill work graduates bent press to 30lb and cossack squat back to 45lb. TGU returns at 30lb after skipping W4. C&P pushes for 8 reps. This is the real KB day.

exercises.notes: Loosen shoulders and thoracic spine
Easy swings at moderate weight, groove the hinge

sets.name: Block 1: KB Flow (OTD Flow)

exercises.notes: Dead stop on floor, explosive pull to rack.
Full depth from rack position.
Step wide, hips back, trailing leg straight.
Bend AWAY from the bell, lock out overhead.
Control the descent back to start. Complete one side, then switch.

sets.notes: OTD flow (Madeline Conti): dead clean → squat → lateral lunge → bent press → tactical clean. 25lb. 3 rounds each side, do the whole flow 3 times. W4 felt fluid by the last set — maintain that quality, don't rush.

exercises.notes: 30lb — up from 25lb in W4. Skipped the jump last week due to GTC, so this is the first attempt at 30. If it feels off, drop back to 25 and own it.
45lb goblet — back to full weight after the intentional deload to 25lb for GTC week.
25lb. Solid last week, keep it here and focus on depth.
30lb. Skipped in W4, was 30lb in W3. Left side is the project — pause at every transition. Right side sets the template. No rushing.
50lb. W3 and W4 both got 6. Push for 8 — catch with knee bend, full lockout. If form breaks at 7, stop there.
50lb. Full stretch at bottom, squeeze at top.
50lb. Full depth, pause at the bottom.
50lb. Up from 15 in W4. Two-handed, explosive hips.

tips: Full explore session — 35-40 min. This is the real KB day.
Block 1 (flow) stays at 25lb. Fluidity over weight — you earned it in W4.
Block 2: bent press jumps to 30lb for the first time. If it doesn't feel right, no shame in dropping back.
TGU is back after skipping W4. Left side instability is the focus — slow is smooth, smooth is fast.
C&P: 8 reps is the target. W3 and W4 both hit 6. If you get 7 clean, that's still progress.
Swings go up to 20 reps per set. You've been doing 15 — earn the volume.

name: W5 KB Morning Flow
description: 10 min. Used for both Tuesday Explore and Thursday Refine. Thoracic + hips for bent press and flow work. Keep it light — especially Thursday since legs are tomorrow.

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Deep thoracic rotation — bent press prep.
Full spinal wave, slow.
On stomach, bring one heel across toward opposite hand. Deep thoracic + hip rotation. 20s each side.
30s each side. TGU and overhead lunge need open hips.
Bodyweight. Deep and slow — groove the pattern.
NEW — pigeon with active push to deepen. 20s each side. Opens the hip for bent press and windmill.

tips: 10 min — same flow for Tuesday and Thursday.
Scorpion stretch is new — go easy, it's a deep rotation. Don't force it.
Active pigeon: press gently into the stretch rather than just sitting in it.
Thursday: this flows into light skill work only. Don't let the warmup make you want to go hard.
If short on time: thoracic opener block only. 4 min.

name: Week 5 Legs (Quads/Hamstrings/Glutes/Calves)
description: W4 proved fresh legs = PRs. Squat jumped to 70/side for a clean 8 on the last set. RDL was skipped in W4 — bring it back at W3 weight. Leg press and calf raises were too easy — loading up. Leg curl found a real working weight at 110. Lunges stay at 40lb because they're already death.

exercises.notes: Full depth, arms in front. Thursday KB should have been light — legs should be fresh.
Open up the hips
Bodyweight, feel the stretch
Warmup set: 55/side x8. Deep — same depth as your working sets.
W4: hit 70x8 on last set as a PR with fresh legs. This week: 70/side for all 4 sets. Full depth on every rep. If 70x8 is clean across all 4, try 75/side on the last set — that's your next frontier.
Skipped in W4, W3 was 65/side 8/10/10. Back to 65/side x10 for all 3 sets. If it moves easy, try 70/side on the last set. Slow eccentric, feel the hamstring stretch.

W4: 4 plates + 25/side — hips tight first set but second set full ROM. Probably too easy. This week: 5 plates/side. Full ROM check on set 1 — knee to chest depth. If hips are tight, take a longer warmup but don't back off the weight.

W4: 6 plates/side x3x20 — still too easy even with slow tempo and pause at top. Try 7 plates/side. If you max out the machine, switch to single-leg calf raises — halves the load, doubles the work, and fixes any L/R imbalance.
W4: 90 too easy, 110x12 hard but completed. Working weight is 110 now. 3x12 at 110. If clean on all 3 sets, try 115 on the last set. Slow negative — 2 full seconds down.
40lb DBs. Absolute death as always. Stay at 40. Long stride, back knee to floor. Don't split the last set — you've earned fresher legs this week. The finisher that finishes you.

tips: Thursday KB should be light enough not to matter — fresh legs proved = PRs in W4.
Squat: 70/side is the working weight now. 4x8 deep. If all clean, 75 on the last set.
RDL is back after skipping W4. 65/side x10, try 70 if it's easy.
Leg press: 5 plates/side — W4 was too easy. Full ROM or it doesn't count.
Calf raises: 7 plates/side. You keep outgrowing these. When you max out the machine, switch to single-leg.
Leg curl: 110 is the real working weight. 3x12, try 115 on last set if clean.
Lunges at 40lb: respect them. They don't care how strong your squat got.
Rest 3 min between squats, 2.5 min on RDL, 2 min on leg press, 90s on isolation

name: W5 Legs Morning Flow
description: 15 min hip + squat prep. Same structure as W4 with deeper holds. Legs should be fresh — Thursday KB was light skill work only. 70/side is the floor, 75 is the goal.

exercises.notes: Standing, big slow circles.
3 seconds down, hold at the bottom for 2, drive up. You've been squatting for 4 weeks — own this pattern.
Low lunge + twist. Hip flexors and thoracic.
45s each side. By now this should be noticeably deeper than W4.
30s each side. Elbows down if you can — deeper than pigeon for the hip flexor.
Deep side lunge. Heels down. Your cossack squat practice makes these feel natural now.
Squeeze at top. Activates glutes before you squat.
Core bracing before loading the bar.
NEW — replacing hollow body. Oblique activation + hip mobility in one move. 6 each side.

tips: 15 min — lizard pose is new this week. Deeper hip flexor stretch than pigeon.
Thursday KB was light skill work — legs should feel fresh. If they don't, something went wrong Thursday.
Plank hip dips: new core move. Obliques + hip mobility for squat stability.
You're squatting 70/side as a baseline this week. This flow matters.
If short on time: hip warmup + pigeon + glute bridge. 8 min minimum.

name: Week 5 Pull — Shuffle Week (Back/Biceps/Rear Delts)
description: Shuffle week: preacher curl moves right after pull-ups so biceps get an honest test before they're fried. W4 actuals: 33 pull-ups (9/8/8/4+4 — heat + GTC fatigue), lat pulldown 150 weirdly easy, cable row 130 confirmed, preacher curl completely dead at end (10/side x8). Cable curl dies last anyway, so it stays at the end.

exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades, warm up upper back
Loosen thoracic spine
W4: 25s. Push to 30s. Decompress, grip, lats.
W4: 9/8/8/4+4 = 33 total (heat + GTC fatigue dragged down sets 3-4). Goal: 34+. Aim for 4x9 = 36 in better conditions. Full ROM every rep, no kipping.

sets.name: Station 1: Preacher Curl (MOVED UP) + DB Rows

exercises.notes: SHUFFLE: moved early so biceps are fresh. W4: 10/side x8, x8 — completely dead because biceps were already toast from 4 exercises. Start at 15/side now that they're fresh. If 15 is easy for 10, bump to 17.5 or 20 on set 2-3. This is the real test — find your honest preacher curl weight.
W4 pyramid: 55-75, good form on later sets. This week: 55x10, 65x8, 75x6-8 (top set — W4 confirmed 75 is doable). Drop the 4th set, keep it at 3 rounds to match preacher curl.

sets.name: Station 2: Lat Pulldown + Seated Cable Row

exercises.notes: W4: 150x10, 150x12, 150x12 (back left machine) — weirdly easy. Bump to 155 or even 160. If 160x10 is clean, that's the new working weight. Same machine (back left).
W4: 130x12 x3 (back right machine) — confirmed working weight. Stay at 130 for 3x12. If all sets hit 12 clean, try 135 on set 3. Torso upright, no leaning.

sets.name: Station 3: Face Pull + Reverse Fly + Cable Curl

exercises.notes: W4: 50x20 x3, form breaking around 17 on sets 2-3. Stay at 50. Goal: 3x20 all clean reps. If form breaks before 17, drop to 45 and get the full 20. External rotate hard at top.
W4: 25x15 x3, form dropping around 12 on set 2. Stay at 25, seated bent-over. Goal: 3x15 with no form drop. If you can't hold 15 clean, do 12 strict + pause.

Stays last — it was dying at the end anyway (W4: 42.5x12 x3 then x9). With preacher curl moved early, biceps might be even more tired here. Try 42.5 again, accept 10-rep sets. If you get 3x12, bump to 45 next week.

tips: SHUFFLE WEEK: Preacher curl moved right after pull-ups. Fresh biceps = honest numbers. Find your real working weight.
Pull-up goal: beat 33. No GTC the day before should help. 4x9 = 36 is the target.
Preacher curl at 15/side fresh should feel very different from 10/side dead. If 15 is easy, go heavier.
Lat pulldown: 150 was easy — try 155-160 and see where it lands. Same back left machine.
Cable curl is now last intentionally. It was dying at the end anyway, and preacher curl gets the fresh slot where it matters.
Rest 3 min between pull-ups, 90s between station 1, 75s between stations 2-3.

name: W5 Pull Morning Flow (Shuffle)
description: 12 min. Preacher curl is coming right after pull-ups today — prime the biceps and elbows, not just the back. Grip work + elbow prep added.

exercises.notes: Full spinal wave. Slow.
20s passive hang. Decompress shoulders, warm the grip.
Retract scapulae only, no elbow bend. Activates lats for pull-ups.
Loosen wrists and forearms. Grip was a limiter in W3 — prep it.
Small circles, then big. Warm the bicep insertion points.
20s. Arms back — stretches biceps and chest. Primes the preacher curl angle.
Hamstrings and lower back. Breathe and sink.
30s each side. Releases lower back. These keep getting deeper week over week.
Walk hands to each side, 15s each. Lateral lat stretch before you pull.

tips: 12 min — new this week: bicep/elbow prep block since preacher curl is coming early.
Dead hang: passive, shoulders relaxed. Grip warm for pull-ups.
Doorframe stretch opens the bicep insertion — primes the preacher curl angle where the EZ bar stretches you at the bottom.
If short on time: spine primer + arm/elbow prep. 6 min minimum.

name: Week 5 Push — Shuffle (Chest/Shoulders/Triceps)
description: Shuffle week: reordered exercises to get honest numbers on lifts that have been dying at the end. Push press moves to station 1 (before chest fatigue) to test true overhead capacity. Chest press machine stays as main chest exercise. Based on W4 actuals.

exercises.notes: Forward then backward, loosen shoulders
Light band, squeeze shoulder blades
Warm up the chest, get blood flowing

SHUFFLE: moved first — fresh shoulders, no chest pre-fatigue. W4 actuals: 50/side couldn't go overhead (done after chest work), 45/side got 5/6/5. Try 50/side again here. If 50 goes up clean, that confirms the W4 ceiling was fatigue, not strength.
W4 actuals: 15x15 too easy. Try 20s if available. Otherwise 15s with slow eccentric or 5 sets with drop set.
W4 actuals: 175x6/8/8, 185x4, 180x3. Working weight 175. Try 180 for all sets — if 180x6+ is clean, push to 185 on last set. Keep handles forward, not behind back.
W4 actuals: 55x10/10/10(hard)/9. Push for clean 4x10 at 55 before going up. This is an honest 4x10 check — chest press came first now so incline may feel slightly harder.

sets.name: Station 3: Pec Deck + Cable Lat Raise + Triceps

exercises.notes: W4 actuals: 105x12, 105x12, 115x9 (failure). Start at 110, try 115 on set 2-3. Goal: 115x10+.
W4 actuals: 50x12/12, 65x6 then 55x4. Working weight 50, try 55 for all sets. 65 remains stretch goal.
W4 actuals: 55x12 (easy), 65x10, 65x12 (form breaking). Working weight 65, try 70 if 65 feels clean. Keep hands apart.
W4 actuals: 70/90/105 all x12, all too easy. Start at 110+. If 110x12 is easy, jump to 120. Pull exercise as push day finisher — lats are fresh.

tips: SHUFFLE WEEK: Push press is first exercise. This tests whether W4's 50/side ceiling was fatigue or actual strength limit.
If 50/side push press goes up clean here, future weeks can keep it early OR rotate position.
Chest press at 180: handles forward, not behind back. Shoulder-safe start position.
Incline DB at 55: moved after chest press now — note if the order change affects reps.
Pec deck: 110 start, push to 115. Cable lat raise: 55 target, 65 stretch.
Tricep extension: 65 working, try 70. Straight arm pulldown: start at 110+.

name: W5 Push Morning Flow (Shuffle)
description: 12 min. Push press is FIRST today — this flow prioritizes overhead mobility and thoracic rotation. Get those shoulders ready to find out if 50/side goes overhead with fresh muscles.

name: Week 6 KB Full Body (Explore)
description: New flow day! B-Stance Flow (@Asgooch) replaces OTD — unilateral, don't put the bell down, includes your first snatch work. Start at 20lb to learn transitions. Bent press at 30 for real. TGU at 30 — left side is the project. C&P pushing for 8 reps.

exercises.notes: Light, loosen shoulders
Find your depth
Easy swings

sets.name: Block 1: B-Stance Flow (@Asgooch)

exercises.notes: B-stance: one foot back, heel up. Swing between legs, explosive hip drive. Bell swings up to about chest height.
→ On the next swing, CATCH it directly into rack position at your shoulder. Don't dead stop — ride the momentum into the clean.
→ Bell is in rack. Stay in B-stance (back foot heel still up). Squat down and stand back up. Bell stays at shoulder.
→ From rack, punch the bell straight overhead in one motion. Lock out arm, stand tall. This is the split stance snatch — you're still in B-stance when it goes up.
→ Bell locked out overhead, step forward into a lunge. Stand straight up from the lunge with arm STILL locked overhead. That's 1 rep of the full flow.

sets.notes: NEW FLOW: B-Stance Flow (@Asgooch). 5 reps each side × 5 rounds. Don't put the bell down between moves — it's one continuous chain. Start at 20lb to learn the transitions, especially the swing clean and split stance snatch. Video: https://x.com/Asgooch/status/2026011472489324584

exercises.notes: 30lb. W5: first attempt at 30. If W5 was solid, push for 5 reps per side. If shaky, stay at 3.
45lb goblet. Full depth, heels down, sit in the hole for a beat.
25lb. Solid and comfortable. If 25 is genuinely easy, try 30 on last set.
30lb. Left side: pause at elbow prop, hand prop, hip bridge. Right side is the template — match it on the left. If 30 is smooth both sides, try 35 on a single rep.
50lb. W5: did 6 (down from target 8 due to shoulder). This week fresh: push for 8. Catch with knee bend, full lockout.
50lb. Easy at 50 — slow it down. 2-second pull, 2-second lower.
50lb. Pause at bottom for 1 second. Max KB weight.
50lb. Full hip snap. Save the circuit for Saturday if you want it.

tips: Tuesday = EXPLORE. New flow!
B-Stance Flow: 5 reps each side × 5 rounds. Watch the video first: https://x.com/Asgooch/status/2026011472489324584
20lb to learn. The transitions are the workout — swing → catch into rack → squat → punch overhead → lunge → stand up arm locked.
Swing clean = catching a full swing into rack. Split stance snatch = punch overhead while still in B-stance.
Bent press at 30: this is week 2 at this weight. Should feel more controlled.
TGU left side: the project continues. Smooth > fast.
C&P: 8 reps is the goal. You hit 6 when tired last week. Fresh = 8.
No circuit finisher — save that for Saturday Refine if you want it.

name: Week 6 Legs (Quads/Hamstrings/Glutes/Calves)
description: 70/side is the squat baseline — felt safe and strong in W5 for all 3 working sets. Try 75 on the last set. RDL at 70 baseline. Leg press starts at 6 plates. Calves trying 7 plates — if you max out, single-leg is the next move.

exercises.notes: Full depth. Thursday KB was light — legs should be fresh.
Open up the hips
Bodyweight, feel the stretch
55/side x8. Deep — same depth as working sets.

Sets 1-3: 70/side x8 deep. Set 4: TRY 75/side. W5: 70x8 across all sets felt safe and strong. 75 is the next frontier — even 5-6 reps at 75 is a win. If 75 feels sketchy, stay at 70x8 and add a 5th set instead.
70/side for all 3 sets. W5: bumped to 70 on last set and it felt great. Now 70 is the baseline. Slow eccentric, hamstring stretch.

6 plates/side. W5: 5 plates x12 was easy, tried 6 plates x8 at the end. Now start at 6. Full ROM — mobility is the limiter, not strength. Lower mount point. If 6x12 is clean, you've found the new weight.
7 plates/side. W5: 6 plates finally burned after 4 sets of 20. Try 7. If you literally run out of plates, do single-leg at 4 plates — halves the load, doubles the work.
110. W5: 12/12/12 (last 3 of set 3 dying). Stay at 110, aim for 3x12 all clean. If first 2 sets are easy, try 115 on set 3. 2-second negatives.
40lb DBs. W5: completed all sets, still hard, ugly last set. Stay at 40. The goal is clean form on all 3 sets — THEN bump weight. Walking lunges not stationary. Long stride, back knee toward floor.

tips: Squat 70 is the baseline — 75 on the last set is the test. Even 5 reps at 75 is a W.
RDL at 70 for all 3 sets — first time at this weight for a full session.
Leg press: 6 plates is real now. Focus on ROM depth, not just moving weight.
Calf raises: 7 plates. You're a menace. Single-leg if the machine runs out.
Leg curl: 110x12 with clean form. 115 is the stretch goal.
Lunges: form before weight. Clean all 3 sets at 40 before going to 45.
Rest 3 min between squats, 2.5 min RDL, 2 min leg press, 90s isolation

description: Preacher curl stays early — 15/side x10 is the honest number. DB rows pushing for 80lb top set. Lat pulldown at 160 wide grip. Pull-ups: stop fragmenting, aim for clean sets even if fewer reps.

exercises.notes: Scap activation
Spine warm
Grip and shoulder warm
W5: 9/9/6+2/4+2+2 = 34 but fragmented. New rule: CLEAN SETS ONLY. If you can only do 7, do 7. No splitting. Quality over total count. Goal: 4 clean sets, aim for 9/8/7/6 = 30 clean.
15/side. W5 shuffle: 15/side x10 is the honest number. 20 failed at 2 reps. Stay at 15/side, aim for 3x10. If all hit 10, try 17.5/side on last set. Drop set: 15→10 to failure on last set.
Pyramid: 60x10, 70x8, 80x6-8 (PR attempt!). W5: 75x10 was a PR and felt easy. Your back is ready for 80. Brace hard on the top set.
160. Back left machine. Wide grip (near the bend) — takes biceps out after preacher curl. W5: 160x12/10/9. Goal: 3x10+ clean.
130. Back right machine. W5: 12/12/10. Consistent. Try 135 on last set if first two are clean 12s.
50. W5: 20/20/20 but form breaking at 17. Goal: 3x20 ALL clean. If form drops, do 55x15 instead — shorter sets, heavier.
25lb. Seated bent-over. W5: hard from set 1. Stay at 25, aim for strict form on all 15.
42.5. Stays last — accept 10-rep sets since preacher already worked biceps. W5: 10/10/10. If 3x12 is clean, bump to 45 next week.

tips: Pull-ups: CLEAN SETS. Stop fragmenting. 30 clean reps > 34 fragmented reps.
Preacher curl stays early — 15/side is your number. Own it before chasing 17.5.
DB row 80lb PR attempt: your back handled 75x10 on a bad day. Brace and go.
Lat pulldown wide grip: keeps biceps out of it after preacher curl. Different exercise than close grip.
Cable curls will feel harder after preacher. That's fine — 10 reps is the new baseline here.

name: Week 7 Legs — Form Check Week (Quads/Hamstrings/Glutes/Calves)
description: Form Check Week. 75/side is the squat baseline — felt as easy as 70 in W6. RDL is the focus: back was kicking in at 65 on first 2 sets, third set was best (slow, static, good bracing). Stay at 65-70 and nail the bracing pattern. Leg press, calf raise, leg curl, lunges all hold steady.

exercises.notes: Full depth. Wake up the legs.
Open up the hips
Bodyweight, feel the stretch
55/side x8. Deep — same depth as working sets.

Sets 1-3: 75/side x8 deep. Set 4: TRY 80/side. W6: 75x8 across all 3 working sets felt as easy as 70. 75 is the confirmed baseline. 80 on the last set is the test — even 5-6 clean reps at 80 is a win.

SWAPPED from barbell RDL this week — incline RDL on the 45° hyperextension bench. Hold a plate at your chest (start with 25lb, go up if easy). HINGE AT THE HIPS, not the spine — keep back neutral, bend at the hip crease. You should feel hamstrings and glutes, NOT lower back. If you only feel your back, you're rounding. Slow: 2 seconds down, squeeze glutes at top, 2 seconds up. This teaches you what the RDL posterior chain activation is supposed to feel like without barbell bracing stress.
6 plates/side. W6: hard but good ROM after first set. Stay at 6 plates. Full ROM — lower mount point. Focus on depth, not speed.
SINGLE LEG: 4 plates/side x20, other foot stabilizing on the plate. W6: graduated to single leg. Stay here — this is the new baseline.
110. W6: 12 reps, hard last 3. Stay at 110. 2-second negatives on every rep. Clean form before any weight bump.
40lb DBs. W6: still death but no sitting between sets — that's progress. Stay at 40. Walking lunges, long stride, back knee toward floor. Form check week = clean all 3 sets before any weight bump.

tips: FORM CHECK WEEK — RDL is the main focus. Brace before every rep, slow eccentric, no back compensation.
Squat 75 is the baseline. 80 on the last set if sets 1-3 are clean.
RDL cues: brace hard, 3-count eccentric, hips back not down, feel hamstrings not lower back.
If RDL bracing is solid all 3 sets at 65, try 70 on set 3 only.
Calf raises are single leg now — 4 plates x20, other foot just stabilizing.
Leg curl: 110x12 with 2-second negatives. Stay here.
Lunges: 40lb, survive all 3 sets with clean form.
Rest 3 min between squats, 2.5 min RDL, 2 min leg press, 90s isolation

name: Week 7 Pull — Form Check (Back/Biceps)
description: Form Check Week. Less yanking, more static control. W6 pull-ups hit 36 clean — keep the clean-only rule. DB rows hit 80x7 PR but form changed; keep 80 as top set with strict form. NEW: chest supported DB row as strict accessory, high cable bicep curl replaces cable curl, cable crunch added as true finisher.

exercises.notes: Scap activation
Spine warm
Grip and shoulder warm
W6: 10/10/9/7 = 36 clean. The clean-only rule is working. Form check week: controlled descent, no kipping, full dead hang at bottom. Don't chase 10 on every set — 8 strict beats 10 sloppy.
15/side. W6: 9/9/8 + drop. Stay at 15/side — form check week means no bumping. Full ROM, slow negative, no cheating the bottom.
Pyramid: 60x10, 70x8, 80x7 (strict form). W6: 80x7 PR but had to brace everything. Form check week: 80 stays as top set but zero momentum. Brace core, pull to hip, squeeze at top, slow lower.

sets.name: Station 2: Chest Supported Row + Lat Pulldown

exercises.notes: NEW. 40-50lb. No momentum possible — the bench does the work of keeping you honest. Squeeze at top, 2-second negative. Start at 40, go to 50 if 40 is easy.
170 (up from 160). Back left machine. Wide grip near the bend. W6: 160x12 x3 clean. Form check: lean back slightly, pull to chest not behind neck, hold the squeeze 1 second.
130 (120+10 add-on). Back right machine. Upright posture, slower tempo. W6: form is the progression now. 2-second concentric, 1-second hold, 2-second eccentric. No leaning back.
50 (back from 57.5). W6: 57.5x15 form broke down. Form check week: 50x20 all clean. External rotate at the top, elbows high, hold 1 second. If 20 is easy, stay — the point is perfect reps.
25lb. W6: form breaks last 2 reps. Form check week: if you can only do 13 strict, stop at 13. Seated bent-over, slow squeeze, no swinging.

sets.name: Finisher: High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch

exercises.notes: NEW — replaces cable-bicep-curl. Seated at lat pulldown, two handles, curl down. Find your working weight this week. Start light (30-40), slow and strict. Biceps are pre-fatigued from preacher so go lighter than ego says.
NEW — true finisher. Find working number this week. Kneel facing away from cable, crunch down. Keep hips locked — movement comes from abs, not hip flexors. Start around 50-60, adjust.

tips: FORM CHECK WEEK: every rep should be textbook. If form breaks, stop the set. Less yanking, more static control.
Pull-ups: 36 clean last week was great. Don't force 10 on every set — 8 strict is the priority.
DB row 80lb stays but zero momentum. The chest supported row afterward will expose any cheating habits.
Chest supported DB row is NEW: the bench removes all body english. Start at 40lb, find your honest number.
Face pull dropped back to 50x20 on purpose. 57.5 broke form — own 50 with perfect external rotation.
High cable bicep curl is NEW: seated at lat pulldown with two handles. Find your weight — biceps are already cooked from preacher.
Cable crunch is NEW: abs finisher on the cable column. Keep hips locked, crunch from the abs only.
Both finishers are on the cable column — no moving between stations. Efficient.
Rest: 2.5 min pull-ups, 2 min stations 1-2, 90s station 3, 75s station 4, 60s finisher

exercises.notes: Loosen shoulders
Scap activation, slow and controlled
Shoulder rehab — Yuri band warmup. Keep in every push session.
Get blood flowing
45/side. W7D1: 45x8x3 with parallel feet — way cleaner than 50. Stay at 45 for form check. Last 2 reps of set 3 started to collapse — stop there if form breaks. No stagger, no momentum hacks.
20lb. W7D1: right shoulder crackly but improved each set with warmup. Stay at 20x15. Watch the shoulder — injury prevention is paramount.
180. W7D1: 180x8x3 clean then died on 4th. Confirmed working weight at station 2. Full ROM, controlled eccentric.
50lb. W7D1: dropped from 55 to 50 for pause at bottom — 4x10 all completed with better ROM. 50 with pause > 55 without. Last set mega struggle but locked it out.
Working number: 8. W7D1: 6 too easy, 9 losing form, 8x11 felt most like pec deck. Feels like DB fly with constant tension — less support than pec deck. Stay at 8.
45. W7D1: dropped from 50 to 45 for stability. Both sides crackly at 50. 45 feels more controlled. Stay here.
54x12x3. W7D1: surprisingly strong, can feel all the fibers. Steady and safe. Love these.
110. W6: 10/10/9. Stay at 110, form focus. Straight arms, lats do the work, stop when shoulders start taking over.

tips: FORM CHECK WEEK: Stop every set when form breaks. If you only get 6 clean reps, that's the set. Don't grind ugly reps to hit a number.
Push press: parallel feet, no stagger. Shallow dip, big drive, full lockout. This is the cue to drill this week.
Kinesis cable fly is new — treat the first session as exploration. Find the working number, learn the movement path.
Weights stay flat or dip. The goal is perfect reps, not PRs. PRs come from form weeks.
Shoulder rehab stays in the warmup — band pull-aparts + kneeling cross-body external rotations every push day.

description: 15 min. Beefed up shoulder health block — more bodyweight shoulder strength/rehab. No equipment needed. Overhead mobility + shoulder stability before push press.

exercises.notes: Big circles, warm the joint
Lie on your side, elbow at 90, rotate forearm up. No band needed — gravity is the resistance. Targets the rotator cuff. Go slow.
Face down, lift arms in a Y shape overhead. Hits lower traps + rotator cuff. Hold at the top for 1 second.
Push-up position, arms locked. Only the shoulder blades move — sink down (retract) then push the floor away (protract). Strengthens serratus anterior.
Back flat against wall. Getting deeper each week.

description: Selective push week. Form check discoveries locked in — parallel feet push press, 50lb incline pause, cable lat raise at 45. Where form was clean, nudge weight: chest press tries 185 on later sets, straight arm pulldown tries 115. Everything else holds.

exercises.notes: Loosen shoulders
Full Yuri band warmup with red band. W6/W7: this clears the shoulder crackle better than anything else. Do the full sequence before touching any weight.
Get blood flowing, activate chest and triceps
45/side. W7 actuals: 45x8x3 parallel feet, clean. DO NOT rush back to 50 — 45 with perfect form is the play. Parallel feet, shallow dip, explosive drive. Stop the set if form breaks, even at rep 6.
20lb. W7 actuals: 20x15, shoulder crackly but improved with Yuri warmup. Stay at 20x15 — form was clean and shoulder tolerated it. Not chasing 25 yet.

54. W7 actuals: 54x12x3, surprisingly strong. Stay at 54 — can feel all the fibers, steady and safe. Hands shoulder-width for full long head emphasis.
110 base. W7 actuals: 110x12x3 clean. OK TO PUSH: try 115 on set 2 or 3. Straight arms, lats do the work. Stop when shoulders start taking over.

Push press at 45 is NOT a regression from 50 — it's better reps, better form, better long-term strength. Stay patient.

Chest press 185 attempt: only on set 2 or 3 when you're warmest. If form breaks, drop back to 180 immediately.
Incline DB: the pause at bottom IS the progression. 50 with pause > 55 without.
Rest 2 min between push press, 2.5 min on chest press, 75s on cables

3 plates/side (drop from 4), medium ROM — NOT the full-depth 4-plate version from W8. The deep 4-plate version is what's been hammering the hip stabilizers. Medium ROM this week, stay in the strong part of the range.
90 at the new settings (5 m 0 1). W8D5 confirmed 90 as warmup/calibration, 110 as working. Stay at 90 this week — hamstrings don't need the stimulus, we're recovering. Fully seated = QL-safe.
3 plates/side x20, single leg. No plate under foot this week — skip the deeper stretch progression, save it for W10 when the back is quiet.

60s each side. Deep hip flexor release — directly addresses what's been fried. The longer you hold, the more the QL/psoas lets go.

SLOW. Gentle core engagement, no crunching. 8 reps, not 10.

Pigeon for 90s each side — the longer hold lets the QL/psoas actually let go.

Skip glute bridge + dead bug if anything feels twitchy. The stretches are the whole workout if needed.

Gentle spine warm — go slow this week, feel for any QL tightness
Pull-ups are spinal DECOMPRESSION — safe and actually helpful this week

3 sets this week (down from 4). Clean reps only. Stop 1-2 shy of failure on every set — no grinding. Pull-ups decompress the spine so they're actually good for recovery, but don't fatigue to the point where form breaks and you start bracing hard.
10/side (drop from 15). Preacher is supported = QL-safe. Easy strict reps, no grinding. If 10 feels nothing, it's fine — don't go up.
60lb all 3 sets (drop from 80-85 pyramid). W8 rows required full body bracing at 80+ — that bracing is what we're avoiding this week. 60 is light enough to stay loose. Keep it strict, don't yank.

exercises.notes: 35 each side (drop from 40-45). Chest supported = QL-safe, no spinal bracing. 2 sets only. Squeeze at top.
130 (drop from 160). Light, slow, strict. 2 sets only. Wide grip, pull to chest.
40 (drop from 50). Rotator cuff + posterior delt, minimal spinal load. 2 sets.
20 (drop from 25). Seated bent-over, slow squeeze. 2 sets only.
45 (drop from 60). 2 sets, easy pump. SKIP cable crunch this week — direct abs work can re-activate the QL. Crunch will be back in W10.

tips: RECOVERY WEEK — pull-ups are spinal decompression, so they're helpful. Heavy DB rows are not — bracing is the enemy this week.
DB row 60lb all sets (NOT pyramiding to 80). The full-body brace at 80+ is exactly what re-lit the QL on W8D5's session.
Cable crunch is OUT this week — direct abs/flexor work can wake up the QL.
Chest supported row is the star of this week — supported = safe.
Finisher is optional. Skip if the back is talking.
Form over numbers. Stop 2 reps shy of failure on everything.

Full Yuri warmup, red band. Especially important this week — body is reactive.
Easy. Just blood flow, not a test.

SEATED on a bench with back support. 30lb each. SWAP for push press this week — standing OH press loads the spine and makes the QL brace, exactly what we're recovering from. Seated removes all of that. Don't chase weight, these are just to keep the shoulder pattern active.
140-150 (70-80% of W8's 180). W8 actuals: 180x8 locked in. DO NOT touch 180 this week. Full ROM, slow tempo, 2 reps shy of failure. The goal is movement quality, not load.
40lb pause at bottom (dropped from 50). Lighter than W8. The pause is the work. If 40 feels easy, that's fine — stay at 40. Do not creep up.
Working number 6 (down from 8). Light constant tension, pec isolation. 2 sets only.
35 (down from 45). Shoulder is still a bit reactive — stay light and clean. 2 sets only.
45 (down from 54). 2 sets only. Easy squeeze, don't chase pump.

Seated DB press REPLACES push press this week. Standing OH press is exactly what reloads the QL.

Stop every set 2 reps shy of failure. If form breaks, rack it.

name: W9 Push Morning Flow (Pec Minor Release)
description: 15 min. Same shoulder health structure as W8 + added pec minor release block. W9D1 discovery: the right-shoulder-blade-to-front-pec connection felt on slow chest press is classic pec minor tightness. Tight pec minor tilts the shoulder blade forward, which strains what's behind it. The fix is stretching the front, not strengthening the back. Uses a doorframe (in addition to bodyweight).

exercises.notes: Big circles, warm the joint
Lie on your side, elbow at 90, rotate forearm up. Gravity is the resistance. Slow and controlled.
Face down, arms in Y overhead. Lower traps + rotator cuff. Pulls the shoulder blade down and back — direct antagonist to tight pec minor. Hold at top 1 second.
Arms locked, only shoulder blades move — retract then protract. Serratus + pec minor fight over the shoulder blade — activating serratus helps 'unstick' a tight pec minor.
Back flat against wall. Pins the shoulder blades in the corrected position — counteracts forward rolling from pec tightness.
Overhead pressing pattern. Shoulder-to-ceiling.
Standard, easy. Chest activation before the gym.

30s each arm, at LOW/MID/HIGH elbow positions (each hits a different pec minor fiber angle). Arm bent 90°, forearm on door frame, step through and rotate torso AWAY. THIS IS THE KEY MOVE. Do it daily, not just on push mornings — desk work retightens it continuously.
Deep shoulder stretch. Hands far in front, hips over knees, chest sinks toward floor. Stretches lats + pec minor together.
30s each side. Deep thoracic rotation — opens up the front shoulder at the same time.
Squeeze shoulder blades down and back at top. Chest + hip flexors together.

tips: 15 min. Pec minor release is the W9 focus.

The right-shoulder-blade ache → front-pec connection felt on W9D1 chest press is classic pec minor tightness. Tight pec minor tilts the shoulder blade forward, straining what's behind it. Stretch the front to relieve the back.
Doorframe chest stretch is the most important move this week. Do it daily — desk work continuously retightens pec minor, 3x/week doesn't keep up.
Prone Y raises + scapular push-ups are the ACTIVE side of the fix — retrain the shoulder blade to sit back and down.
If short on time: doorframe stretch + prone Y + scapular push-ups. 5 min covers the pec minor protocol.
Track for 2-3 weeks. If the shoulder blade ache fades, diagnosis confirmed. If not, we escalate (rhomboid trigger point, actual lower trap weakness).

name: 30 Min HIIT: Home vs Gym
description: 6-exercise HIIT circuit from @Selfdefencess with bodyweight home variations and weighted gym alternatives. Each exercise has a floor/bodyweight option and a dumbbell/kettlebell option. Pick one column or mix and match based on your equipment.

exercises.notes: Explosive alternating jump lunges. Land softly, keep torso upright.
Jump feet wide into squat, jump back to standing. Maintain squat depth.
Alternate wide and narrow hand placement each rep.
Fast pace, hips low, drive knees to chest.
Hinge forward, walk hands out to plank, walk back to standing.
Full burpee with push-up. Step instead of jump to scale down.
Alternating weighted lunges with torso rotation. Hold dumbbell at chest.
Squat with dumbbell front raise on the way up to shoulder height.
Raise dumbbells out and up in a wide arc. Squeeze at the top.

name: Reddit RRR Progressions

tips: Rest 90s between exercises in each pair (gives ~3 min recovery per muscle group)
Each pair: do exercise A, rest 90s, do exercise B, rest 90s, repeat 3x
Pick the progression level that lets you do 5-8 clean reps with good form
When you can do 3×8, move to the next progression level at 3×5
Stay 1 rep short of failure — do 7-7-7 instead of 8-6-5
All exercises use 10X0 tempo: 1s down, no pause, explode up, no pause at top
BARBELL OPTION: If you have a barbell, you can swap in barbell squats (3×5) for the squat progression, and Romanian deadlifts (3×8) / deadlifts (3×5) for the hinge progression
referenceText:
REDDIT RECOMMENDED ROUTINE (RRR)
— r/bodyweightfitness Wiki —

GOALS
This routine covers strength, hypertrophy/muscle gain (with proper diet), and fat loss (with proper diet). It's a general-purpose full-body program.

EQUIPMENT NEEDED
• A pull-up bar (monkey bars, doorframe bar, rings, etc.)
• A place to do rows (low bar, rings, sturdy table, or even a knotted bedsheet in a door) — this is non-negotiable
• Parallel bars for dips (kitchen counter corners, two sturdy chairs, etc.) — alternatives exist if you truly can't find any

HOW PROGRESSIONS WORK
• Each exercise has a chain of variations from easy to hard (e.g., wall push-up → incline → knee → full → diamond → pseudo-planche)
• Pick the variation where you can do 3 sets of 5 reps with good form
• Each session, try to add 1 rep per set until you reach 3 sets of 8 reps
• Once you hit 3×8 with good form, move to the next harder variation at 3×5
• You only perform ONE exercise from each progression per session — drop the easier one
• For isometric holds (support hold, tuck front lever, etc.): hold 10-30 seconds per set, advance when you hit 3×30s

WARMUP (5-10 min)
  Reps     Exercise                          Notes
  5-10     Yuri's Shoulder Band Warmup        Alternative: stick/towel dislocates
  5-10     Squat Sky Reaches                  Can be done assisted
  10+      GMB Wrist Prep                     Do as many reps as you want
  30s      Deadbugs                           Core activation
  10       Arch Hangs                         Add after you reach negative pull-ups
  30s      Support Hold                       Add after you reach negative dips
  10       Easier Squat Progression            Add after you reach Bulgarian split squats
  10       Easier Hinge Progression            Add after you reach banded Nordic curls

STRENGTH WORK STRUCTURE (40-60 min)
Done as supersets — alternate between the two exercises in each pair.

  Pair 1: Pull-ups & Squats
    3×5-8 Pull-up progression ↔ 3×5-8 Squat progression

  Pair 2: Dips & Hinges
    3×5-8 Dip progression ↔ 3×5-8 Hinge progression

  Pair 3: Rows & Push-ups
    3×5-8 Row progression ↔ 3×5-8 Push-up progression

  Core Triplet
    3×8-12 Anti-Extension ↔ 3×8-12 Anti-Rotation ↔ 3×8-12 Extension (60s rest between each)

HOW PAIRING WORKS
Do a set of exercise A, rest 90s, do a set of exercise B, rest 90s, repeat 3 times. This gives each muscle ~3 min total recovery between its own sets while keeping the workout efficient. For the core triplet, use 60s rest between each of the 3 exercises.

Example:
  Pull-ups → rest 90s → Squats → rest 90s →
  Pull-ups → rest 90s → Squats → rest 90s →
  Pull-ups → rest 90s → Squats → rest 90s →
  move to next pair

REST TIME
90s between exercises is the baseline. If you're dropping reps (e.g., 8 on set 1 but only 5-6 on set 2), extend rest up to 3 minutes. Quality reps matter more than speed.

TEMPO
All exercises use 10X0 tempo: 1 second on the way down, no pause at bottom, explode up (intent matters even if the actual movement is slow), no pause at top.

REP STRATEGY
Stay 1 rep short of failure. If your max is 8 reps, do 7-7-7 instead of 8-6-5. Going to failure on early sets kills your volume on later sets, which reduces total work and slows progress.

BARBELL INTEGRATION (Optional)
If you have access to a barbell and squat rack, you can swap in barbell work for the lower body progressions after mastering the basics:
• Replace squat progression with barbell squats: 3×5
• Workouts 1 & 3 of the week: replace hinge progression with Romanian deadlifts (weighted): 3×8
• Workout 2 of the week: replace hinge progression with deadlifts: 3×5
• Result: squatting 3x/week, Romanian deadlifting 2x/week, deadlifting 1x/week
• Warm up with progressively heavier sets before your work sets

IF THE ROUTINE IS TOO MUCH
• Reduce sets to 1-2 instead of 3 (preferred modification for beginners)
• Increase rep range to 5-12 or 5-15 before progressing to the next exercise
• Reduce from 3x/week to 2x/week
• Split into upper/lower across different days (e.g., MWFSat)
• Start with just 3 exercises: squat progression + push-ups + rows

TIMELINE FOR RESULTS
• Performance improvements: 2-3 workouts in
• Visible to yourself: 4-6 weeks (with proper diet)
• Visible to others: 12+ weeks (with proper diet)

WHEN TO MOVE ON
Move to an intermediate routine when you plateau for weeks despite good diet, sleep, and stress management. Or when you exhaust all progressions. The r/bodyweightfitness community recommends developing your own intermediate routine based on the programming principles you'll learn along the way.

name: Heavy Duty: Chest & Back
description: Classic Mentzer chest/back split using pre-exhaustion. Isolate the target muscle first, then hit it with a compound while fatigued. This forces the target muscle to fail before your arms give out.

exercises.notes: ISOLATION: Pec deck or cable fly. Squeeze hard at contraction. 4-2-4 tempo. To FAILURE.
COMPOUND: Immediately into press (no rest). Chest pre-fatigued = chest fails first. To FAILURE.
ISOLATION: Pullover or straight-arm pulldown. Feel the lats stretch. 4-2-4 tempo. To FAILURE.
COMPOUND: Immediately into row (no rest). Lats pre-fatigued = back fails before biceps. To FAILURE.
Only if you have more in the tank. 1 set, absolute failure, rest-pause if needed.
Only if you have more in the tank. 1 set, absolute failure.

tips: PRE-EXHAUST: Isolation exercise first tires the target muscle. The compound then forces it to fail before synergists.
NO REST between isolation and compound - this is one continuous superset
FAILURE on both exercises. If you can do more, the weight was too light.
WAIT 5-7 DAYS before training chest/back again
This workout should take ~25-30 min if done with proper intensity

name: Heavy Duty: Consolidation
description: Mentzer's famous 'Ideal Routine' - the ultimate in training economy. Just 5 exercises, hitting every major muscle group. Only train this 1x per week (or even less). Maximum results, minimum time.

exercises.name: Mentzer's Most Controversial Routine
exercises.notes: This is HIGH INTENSITY training taken to its logical conclusion. 5 exercises, 1 set each, to absolute failure. Train ONCE per week or LESS. Sounds too little? Try it - the intensity makes up for the volume.
Just enough to break a sweat. Save energy for the working sets.
1 warmup set. 1 ALL-OUT set to absolute failure. Full range of motion. 4-2-4 tempo.
1 warmup set. 1 ALL-OUT set. Full stretch at bottom. 4-2-4 tempo. To FAILURE.
1 warmup set. 1 ALL-OUT set. Squeeze shoulder blades. 4-2-4 tempo. To FAILURE.
1 working set. Full range of motion. 4-2-4 tempo. To FAILURE.
The grand finale. 1 working set. Full body engagement. 4-2-4 tempo. To FAILURE.

tips: This routine is NOT for beginners - you need to know how to truly train to failure
5 sets total. That's it. If you need more, you're not training hard enough.
WAIT 7+ DAYS between sessions. Mentzer trained some clients only once every 2 weeks.
Progressive overload: add weight or reps each session. If you can't progress, you're overtraining.
This works because intensity trumps volume. 1 set at 100% effort beats 10 sets at 50%.

name: What is Heavy Duty Training?
description: An educational introduction to Mike Mentzer's Heavy Duty system. Learn the principles before starting the program. This explains WHY you train less but harder.

exercises.name: The Man Behind Heavy Duty
exercises.notes: Mike Mentzer (1951-2001) was a champion bodybuilder and philosopher of training. He won the 1978 Mr. Universe with a perfect score. After competing, he developed Heavy Duty - a training system based on scientific principles and logic, not gym bro science.

exercises.name: Train HARDER, Not Longer
exercises.notes: The stimulus for muscle growth is INTENSITY - how hard you train, not how much. One set taken to true muscular failure stimulates growth just as well as 3-5 sets. More sets just add fatigue without additional benefit.

exercises.name: What 'Failure' Really Means
exercises.notes: FAILURE means you cannot complete another rep with proper form, no matter how hard you try. Not 'this is getting hard' - true failure. When you reach this point, you've triggered the adaptive response. Anything beyond is unnecessary.

sets.name: Principle 3: Recovery is Where Growth Happens

exercises.name: You Don't Grow in the Gym
exercises.notes: Training damages muscle. RECOVERY is when you grow. Most people overtrain - they return to the gym before fully recovering, making zero progress. Heavy Duty prescribes 4-7+ days between training the same muscle. Sounds crazy? Try it.

exercises.name: Tire the Target Muscle First
exercises.notes: Problem: On bench press, your triceps often fail before your chest. Solution: Pre-exhaust. Do an isolation exercise (pec fly) immediately before the compound (bench). Your chest is pre-fatigued, so it fails first during the press.

exercises.name: The Only Way to Know It's Working
exercises.notes: If you're getting stronger (more weight or more reps), you're growing. If not, something's wrong - usually overtraining. Log every workout. Add weight when you hit the top of your rep range. Small increments add up to big gains.

exercises.name: Slow and Controlled
exercises.notes: 4 seconds lifting (concentric), 2 second squeeze at contraction, 4 seconds lowering (eccentric). No bouncing, no momentum. This ensures the muscle does ALL the work and maximizes time under tension. If you can't control it, it's too heavy.

exercises.name: How Often to Train
exercises.notes: BEGINNER: Full body 2x/week (e.g., Mon/Thu). INTERMEDIATE: 2-way split, each 1x/week. Day 1: Chest/Back. Day 2: Legs/Shoulders/Arms. ADVANCED: Consolidation routine 1x every 5-7 days. Yes, really. More rest = more growth.

tips: Start with the 'Intro Full Body' workout to learn the techniques
Keep a training log - track weight, reps, and how you felt
If you're not progressing after 2-3 weeks, you're training too often
Eat enough protein and sleep 7-8 hours - recovery requires resources
This system challenges everything you've been told. Give it 8 weeks before judging.

name: Heavy Duty: Intro Full Body
description: A beginner-friendly introduction to Mike Mentzer's Heavy Duty system. Learn the principles: train to failure, use slow controlled reps, and keep volume low. This workout hits all major muscle groups with 1-2 sets each.

exercises.name: Read Before Starting
exercises.notes: Heavy Duty training means: 1) SLOW reps (4 sec up, 4 sec down), 2) Go to COMPLETE failure on working sets, 3) Rest 2-3 min between sets, 4) Only 1-2 working sets per exercise. Quality over quantity!
Just get blood flowing - save energy for working sets
Warmup: 1 light set of 12. Then 1 ALL-OUT set to failure. 4-2-4 tempo.
Isolation first: 1 set to failure. 4-2-4 tempo. Feel the chest burn.
Immediately into compound press. Chest is pre-fatigued, so it fails first!
1 warmup set, then 1 ALL-OUT set. Squeeze shoulder blades. 4-2-4 tempo.
1 working set to failure. Control the negative. 4-2-4 tempo.
Biceps to failure, slow negative
Immediately to triceps. No rest. Both to failure.

tips: TEMPO IS KEY: 4 seconds up, 2 second squeeze, 4 seconds down
FAILURE MEANS FAILURE: When you can't move the weight with good form, you're done
REST LONG: 2-3 minutes between exercises - you need full recovery for max effort
WAIT 4-7 DAYS before training these muscles again - recovery is when you grow
Log your weights and reps - progress means adding weight or reps over time

name: Heavy Duty: Legs
description: Mentzer's brutal leg workout. Pre-exhaust quads with leg extensions, then destroy them with leg press or squats. Minimal sets, maximum intensity. Your legs will thank you (eventually).

exercises.notes: Light movement to get blood flowing. Hip circles, leg swings.
1 LIGHT warmup set on leg extension. Just to feel the muscle.
LEG EXTENSION: Squeeze at top for 2 sec. 4-2-4 tempo. All the way to FAILURE.
LEG PRESS or SQUAT: Immediately (no rest). Quads are pre-fatigued. Deep range of motion. To FAILURE.
1 working set to failure. Squeeze at contraction. 4-2-4 tempo.
Full stretch at bottom, hard squeeze at top. 1 set to failure. Hold top for 2 sec each rep.

tips: The quad pre-exhaust superset is BRUTAL - your quads will be screaming before compound
On leg press/squat: go deep, control the negative, explode (but controlled) on positive
You may need to reduce weight significantly on the compound since quads are pre-fatigued
WAIT AT LEAST 5-7 DAYS before training legs again - they need recovery time
If you can walk normally after this workout, you didn't go hard enough

name: Heavy Duty: Rest-Pause Technique
description: Learn Mentzer's rest-pause method for going beyond failure. Hit failure, rest 10 seconds, continue. This extends the set past your normal limits and triggers maximum growth stimulus.

exercises.name: The Rest-Pause Technique
exercises.notes: REST-PAUSE: 1) Perform reps until failure. 2) Rest 10-15 seconds (rack the weight). 3) Continue for 2-4 more reps until failure again. 4) Optional: repeat one more time. This extends time under tension and recruits more muscle fibers.
REST-PAUSE: Go to failure (~8-12 reps). Rack weight, rest 10 sec. Continue to failure (2-4 reps). Rest 10 sec. Final push to failure.
REST-PAUSE: Go to failure. Rest 10 sec. Continue to failure. Rest 10 sec. Final push.
REST-PAUSE: Same technique. Keep form strict even when fatigued.
REST-PAUSE: Failure, 10 sec rest, failure, 10 sec rest, final failure. Feel the pump.
REST-PAUSE: Same technique. Lock out fully on each rep.

tips: REST-PAUSE lets you do more work in less time with the same weight
The 10-second rest allows partial ATP recovery - just enough for a few more reps
Use machines for rest-pause - easier to rack/unrack weight safely
Don't use rest-pause on deadlifts or squats as a beginner - form breaks down
This is ONE extended set - counts as your working set for that muscle group

name: Heavy Duty: Shoulders & Arms
description: Mentzer's shoulder and arm routine. Pre-exhaust delts with lateral raises, then press. Arms get supersetted for maximum pump with minimum volume. Short, intense, effective.

exercises.notes: LATERAL RAISE: Strict form, no swinging. Pause at top. 4-2-4 tempo. To FAILURE.
SHOULDER PRESS: Immediately (no rest). Delts pre-fatigued = shoulders fail before triceps. To FAILURE.
1 set to failure. Squeeze shoulder blades together. 4-2-4 tempo.
BICEPS: Full range of motion. Squeeze at top. 4-2-4 tempo. To FAILURE.
TRICEPS: Immediately (no rest). Lock out fully. 4-2-4 tempo. To FAILURE.
Only if you have more. Rest-pause if needed: hit failure, rest 10 sec, continue to failure again.
Same technique. 1 more superset, both to failure.

tips: Lateral raises: keep strict form - if you're swinging, the weight is too heavy
Pre-exhaust on shoulders means you'll use less weight on press - that's the point
Arms superset = biceps to failure, immediately triceps to failure. No rest.
REST-PAUSE technique: at failure, rest 10 sec, continue until failure again
Total arm volume is only 2-4 sets - but each set is 100% effort

exercises.notes: Stay low in a wide stance, pass the bell in a figure-8 between legs. 4x25 = 100 total.
Circle the bell around your head. Alternate directions each set. 4x25 = 100 total.
Pass KB around your hips/body. Alternate directions. 4x25 = 100 total.
Seated, feet up, rotate side to side with the bell. 4x25 = 100 total.
Hold bell in one hand, side bend away then back. Switch sides each set. 4x25 = 100 total.
Side-to-side swings across the body. 4x25 = 100 total. Use core rotation.

Slow controlled circles around head. Great for shoulder mobility. 4x25 = 100 total.
Strict pull — elbows high, shrug at top, no hip snap. 4x25 = 100 total.
Slow hinge, feel the hamstring stretch, squeeze glutes at top. 4x25 = 100 total.
Wide stance, toes out, hinge to grab bell, drive through heels. 4x25 = 100 total.

Standard two-handed swings. Squeeze glutes hard at top. 4x25 = 100 total.
Tricep extension then circle into a halo. Smooth transitions. 4x25 = 100 total.
Lie on floor, press bell up. Alternate arms each set. 4x25 = 100 total.
Hold by horns overhead, lower behind head, extend. Elbows stay close. 4x25 = 100 total.
Two KBs on floor, hinged over, alternating explosive rows. Anti-rotation. 4x25 = 100 total.
Hinged, one bell, alternate arms each rep. Pull past ribs. 4x25 = 100 total.
Both hands on horns, bent over, row to hips and extend. 4x25 = 100 total.
